Phase 4 director real-Chrome verification surfaced that the URL-construction approach was BUSTED — Etsy's "View all reviews for this item" overlay loads via AJAX on the same listing URL with NO separate URL for filters or pagination. **Architecture pivot via FF#1 `67aeacd`** (2 files +1058/-705) rewrote `etsy-review-extractor.ts` from URL-construction to live-DOM driver (click View-all-reviews button → wait for overlay → click histogram filter → wait for AJAX content swap → walk rows → click pagination Next → wait → repeat) based on director's paste of the 41 KB overlay outerHTML (the empirical evidence that broke the speculative-FF cycle EARLY this time; empirically-grounded selectors: `.deep-dive-sheet` + `[data-deep-dive-reviews-container="true"]` + `[data-review-region]` + `.wt-text-body` + `a[href*="/people/"]` + `[role="img"][aria-label^="Rating:"]` + `[data-reviews-histogram="true"] button[data-rating-value="N"]` + `nav[aria-label="Pagination of reviews"]`). FF#1 BUSTED silently (scrape ended with 0 captures + no error toast). **Rule 14f mid-Phase-4 picker rerouted to diagnostic-instrumentation FF#2 `a3107b6`** (1 file +163/-2; TEMPORARY `[PLOS ETSY DIAGNOSTIC]` console.log at every step + auto-download of overlay HTML to Downloads via programmatic `<a download>`; reused yesterday's eBay FF#4 Pattern). Director ran ONE scrape with DevTools Console open + uploaded the diagnostic console output + the overlay HTML → **FF#3 `41b03c5` empirical fix — the WIN** (2 files +73/-190). **Root cause:** `findOverlayContainer` fallback selector `[aria-modal="true"][role="dialog"]` matched Etsy's hidden `#customer-photo-overlay-carousel` (review-photo lightbox; pre-rendered in DOM with `aria-hidden="true"` + `wt-display-none` class but present at page load). Fix: restrict to `.deep-dive-sheet` only + reject hidden variants (aria-hidden, wt-display-none, hidden attribute). All FF#2 diagnostic instrumentation removed in same commit (mirrors yesterday's eBay FF#5 removing FF#4 instrumentation). **Director Phase 4 PASS verdict on FF#3 = "Everything worked perfectly this time"** (identical phrasing to yesterday's eBay FF#5 PASS — same outcome shape twice in a row across different platforms + different root causes). Defensive coding instinct said "add more fallbacks for resilience" but the empirical failure mode was the OPPOSITE — too many fallbacks let the wrong element through. Lesson: prefer a single canonical-class selector + a hidden-state filter over multiple progressively-broader fallbacks; generalizes to all 4 platform extractors: each `findXxxContainer` helper should use the platform-canonical class + reject hidden variants (aria-hidden, wt-display-none, hidden attribute), not fall back to bare role/modal selectors; applies directly to W2 Walmart Session 1 — `findWalmartContainer` (if it exists) should follow this pattern from the start; (d) **NEW reusable Pattern "AJAX-loaded overlay scraping requires live-DOM driver, not URL-construction"** — Etsy's "View all reviews for this item" overlay loads via AJAX on the same listing URL (no separate URL exists for the overlay OR for per-star filters OR for pagination); the Amazon + eBay URL-construction Pattern (FF#4 from 2026-05-28; reused for eBay 2026-05-30) doesn't apply; Etsy required architecture pivot to live-DOM driver (click trigger → wait for overlay → click histogram filter → wait for content swap → walk rows → click pagination Next → wait → repeat); generalizes to W2 Walmart sub-cluster — investigate Walmart's URL contract FIRST (per director-verbatim spec the Walmart URLs `/reviews/product/<ID>?ratings=N` separate URLs exist so URL-construction applies, NOT live-DOM driver like Etsy); but future per-platform sub-clusters where the platform uses AJAX overlays will need the live-DOM driver Pattern; recommendation for FUTURE: build a small URL-contract-vs-AJAX detection helper at design-session time per platform so the architecture choice is locked before Session 1 begins; (f) **NEW reusable Pattern "Editor save-on-Done race condition (debounced save + unmount cleanup)"** — Issue 5 from today's Phase-4 walkthrough root-caused to RichTextEditor cleanup CLEARING the pending 500ms debounce timer WITHOUT flushing on unmount; AnalysisEditor's parent page.tsx also never refreshed loadState after a successful PUT so read-mode showed stale content even when the save succeeded; two-layer fix: (i) RichTextEditor cleanup flushes pendingContent before clearing timer; (ii) AnalysisEditor exposes onSaved callback; page.tsx wires loadState refresh; Pattern: ANY debounced-save editor with a parent-controlled mount lifecycle (Edit/Done toggle, modal open/close, accordion expand/collapse) must (a) flush on unmount cleanup AND (b) propagate save outcomes back to parent state OR re-fetch from server before unmount; cross-references PerItemAnalysisBox + OverallAnalysisBox which share the same RichTextEditor unmount path (the unmount-flush fix benefits them too); their parent consumers (CapturedTextSubsection etc.) have different state-management shapes so the onSaved-callback wiring is W4-specific. **No top-tier corrections-tier slip this session.** **HANDOFF_PROTOCOL header bump only — no new rules drafted this session.** **Schema change** — no; events fired inside the shadow root do not surface to page-level handlers by default, so the per-input event-isolation band-aid shipped in P-45 Build #2 2026-05-22-i (20 events × 4 inputs = 80 listeners on each form open) is no longer needed and has been deleted (the `applyAggressiveEventIsolation` function + its 4 call sites + its 20-line context comment, 62 LOC total); `styles.ts` extracts a new exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant containing the `.plos-cs-form-*` + `.plos-cs-chip-*` rules previously inlined in `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS` and re-interpolates them back via `${FORM_CHROME_CSS}` so the other forms (image / text / url-add) that still mount to `document.body` continue to receive these rules via the host-page `<head>` stylesheet without drift between the two consumption sites. **W#4 Phase-4 director real-Chrome verification deferred a THIRD consecutive session at director directive *"please defer any real world testing"*** — 10-step verification walkthrough preserved verbatim AGAIN in next NEXT_SESSION.md ## Standing carry-overs section so a future session can copy + execute it without re-deriving. **ONE §4 Step 1c forced-picker fired this session (end-of-session next-session scope only — director's session-start directive resolved per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` without a re-confirmation picker since the launch-prompt task was already the recommended default).** Picker offered (A) Bundled W5 + P-47 deploy to main (Recommended) / (B) P-26 below-fold scroll capture Session 1 / (C) P-43 mechanical prevention small fix / (D) question first; Build commit `283d4d1` — 9 files +1258/-32 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; NOT pushed to main since Session 1 is a build session, not a deploy session. **TWO Rule 14f forced-pickers fired at session start** — (1) Session 1 scope: director picked **Full §C.4 scope (recommended)** over Conservative pointer scope + Handler-only this session per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`; **FIRST APPLICATION of the 2026-05-24 Rule 14a tightening** — the §C.4 binding spec was the source of truth; yesterday's NEXT_SESSION.md pointer had defaulted to a more conservative Session 1 scope; Rule 14a directed surfacing the divergence rather than silently picking the pointer's recommendation; director picked the §C.4 binding spec. (2) Navigation surface placement: director picked **Standalone button above the bar (recommended)** over Tab strip + Button inside ColumnVisibilityBar. **ZERO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution** per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`. **TWO new reusable Patterns memorialized.** **Pattern A — "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance"** — when an edit affordance needs the same save-lifecycle shape at TWO scope levels (per-row vs. per-Project), extract parallel components that share structure but differ on the per-scope wire details (PerItemAnalysisBox per-row PATCH `{ analysis }` minimal variant + AnalysisEditor per-Project PUT `{ contentJson }` full variant both wrap the same `RichTextEditor`; both have the same Saving/Saved/Failed status-indicator structure; both use a generation-counter ref for stale-response protection); pairs with W2 S1's "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" Pattern (2026-05-25) + W2 S3's "OverallAnalysisBox parallel component" Pattern (2026-05-27) as meta-shape — when a shared per-action lifecycle (debounced persist + status indicator + retry on error) is needed at N scopes (per-row / per-category / per-Project), each new scope adds a thin parallel component that wraps the same editor primitive; the save-lifecycle structure stays uniform via shared property shape, while per-scope wire details (method, body, scope key) parameterize via props. **Pattern B — "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer"** — §A.5 mentions `generateHTML` for non-editor read views; AnalysisReadView instead uses `RichTextEditor readOnly=true` (the wrapper supports a `readOnly` prop since W2 S1); rationale: identical typography between edit and read modes without a duplicate render path; the editor's overhead is one ProseMirror instance per mount, negligible for a single per-Project doc; a separate static renderer would risk style drift between the two paths; worth memorializing as a reusable Pattern for future read-view/edit-view siblings. **Implementation-detail observation (informational; NOT a slip):** §C.4 names the editor wrapper `TipTapEditor.tsx` but the wrapper W2 S1 actually shipped is named `RichTextEditor.tsx`; the rename was captured in W2 S1's §B 2026-05-25 entry; today consumed the actual filename; no §A amendment needed. **Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO** the entire session — no `prisma db push`; consumes the existing `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` Prisma model from W1's 2026-05-24 schema (already deployed via 2026-05-23-c W2 deploy). informational, not a slip-of-this-session; captures (a) the W1 path-divergence finding (W1 scaffolded the per-record `CapturedReview` route at the nested path `urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts` likely because it followed the collection route's path shape unreflexively; but the precedent for per-record PATCH/DELETE for the other 3 capture types lives at the shallow path — `text/[textId]` / `images/[imageId]` / `videos/[videoId]` — since the record ID is globally unique within the workflow and the deeper path adds no security; the divergence was a W1 oversight that this session detected at session start + resolved within-session via the architectural move); (b) the architectural-move resolution executed within this session (delete the W1 nested stub; ship the new shallow per-record route; collection route stays at the deeper path matching collection-route precedent — `urls/[urlId]/text/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/images/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/videos/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts` — because parent-URL ownership lookup naturally lives at the deeper collection path); (c) the new reusable Pattern memorialized: **"When a foundation workstream scaffolds a 501-stub at a path that diverges from the precedent shape of the sibling capture types, surface the divergence at the next-workstream session start + execute the path move + commit + announce in the build commit; treat W1 path divergences as informational findings to fix not blockers."** This pattern keeps the per-record surface uniform across capture types at low cost (the path move costs one file delete + one file add + zero schema changes since W1's stub never had any production traffic). Pairs with the design doc §B 2026-05-28 entry's reusable Pattern memorialization "Per-record handler DI-seam precedent extension" — together they cover both the surface-path uniformity (this §Entry's Pattern) + the handler-extraction shape (the design doc §B Pattern). **No top-level corrections-tier slip this session.** **HANDOFF_PROTOCOL header bump only — no new rules drafted this session.** **Schema change** — no; pure code session. Issue 2 = aggressive 20-event stopPropagation band-aid (mousedown + mouseup + click + dblclick + pointerdown + pointerup + keydown + keyup + keypress + input + change + paste + copy + cut + compositionstart + compositionupdate + compositionend + focus + focusin + focusout + blur) applied to ALL FOUR text inputs in `video-capture-form.ts` (newCategoryInput + compositionArea + embeddedArea + tagsInput = 80 listeners total) plus click-handler force-focus with `queueMicrotask` retry to override page-level focus-stealing — Build #8's keystroke-only defense on newCategoryInput alone was insufficient because the bug fires at the FOCUS layer (inputs never receive focus in the first place); empirically verified working on Amazon during Phase 4 (the P-27 Bug #11 platform); Issue 3 = `recording-bytes-upload.ts` line 115 PUT Content-Type changed from `input.blob.type || mimeType` (full MediaRecorder MIME with codec params like `video/webm;codecs=vp9,opus`) to `mimeType` (the already-normalized base type like `video/webm`) since Supabase bucket's `allowedMimeTypes` is strict-equal validated against `['video/mp4', 'video/webm', 'video/quicktime']` — codec-laden full MIME doesn't match → 400; codec is still preserved in container metadata + browsers + servers infer codec from container metadata. Phase 1 caught a 4th structural-ceiling issue at FINALIZE call (`'Couldn't save (400): Request body must include clientId + sourceType (EMBED|DIRECT_BYTES) + originalSrcUrl...'`) — root cause was production vklf.com routes didn't have the Build #1a `isVideoSourceType('SCREEN_RECORDING') === true` validator change yet (undeployed code at the schema-change-in-flight boundary); Phase 1 has a structural ceiling because it tests against the LIVE production server which doesn't have the new validator; decision: declare Phase 1 done up to finalize, proceed to Phase 2 → Phase 3, finalize verification deferred to Phase 4 post-deploy where production code carries the new validator. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-22-h (One-hundred-and-thirty-fourth Claude Code session — **W#2 polish P-44 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-h on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — closes the multi-reproduction `wxt build` + `wxt zip` parent-process hang class via build commit `57ed383` (5 files changed +61/-11 — 2 NEW programmatic-API wrapper scripts + `package.json` 4 script rewires + `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` KNOWN ISSUE → wrapper-note swaps). Empirical root cause confirmed: wxt 0.20.25 → Vite 8.0.11 → Rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 (the new Rust-based bundler that replaced Rollup in Vite 8) spawns worker threads via the `@rolldown/binding-linux-x64-gnu` native binding that aren't released when the build promise resolves, leaving Node's event loop with active handles. wxt's CLI awaits the build then relies on natural event-loop drain — which never happens. Confirmed by reading wxt's CLI internals (`node_modules/wxt/dist/cli/commands.mjs`, `core/build.mjs`, `core/builders/vite/index.mjs`) — `isRolldownVersion(vite.version)` returns true for Vite ≥8; Vite 8.0.11's package.json confirms rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 dependency; wxt 0.20.26 + 0.21.0 changelogs reviewed — no relevant upstream fix; Vite upstream issue #18224 documents related pattern. Fix shipped via NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` (16 LOC programmatic-API wrapper — `import { build } from 'wxt'; await build(); process.exit(0);` with browser-flag passthrough + try/catch) + NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (16 LOC same shape calling `zip()` instead of `build()`); both force-exit with `process.exit(0)` after the promise resolves, bypassing the broken event-loop drain entirely. `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` 4 scripts rewired through the wrappers. `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` KNOWN ISSUE blocks replaced with one-paragraph wrapper notes pointing readers at `scripts/wxt-build.mjs` if a hang regresses; deploy.md preserves historical CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-references + adds 2026-05-22-h pointer. Rule 24 scope expansion to `wxt zip` because `wxt zip` internally calls `internalBuild()` from the same `core/utils/building/internal-build.mjs`; symmetric fix is the most-thorough Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation choice. Post-fix verification: `npm run build` cold (after `rm -rf .output`) 4s exit 0 dist valid; `npm run zip` cold 3s exit 0 with 202,601-byte zip artifact valid; warm + 3x rapid-fire builds all GREEN. Pre-fix reproduction confirmed: `rm -rf .output && timeout 45 npm run build` hung past 45s (exit 124 = timeout), dist visible at +6s, process still alive at +36s. Pre-end-of-session /scoreboard ran from `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub`: all 5 checks GREEN at exact baselines (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / extension `npm test` 558/558 / src/lib node:test 590/590 / `npm run build` 57 routes); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention.** `session_2026-05-22-h_p44-wxt-zip-build-parent-process-hang-fix` (PURE LOCAL INFRASTRUCTURE session — THIRD polish-detour in a row ahead of P-45 Build #2's deploy session — 2026-05-22-f P-42 backup-memory hook fix; 2026-05-22-g P-43 absolute-paths discipline; today P-44 wxt build/zip hang fix; no production code touched; no main push; no ff-merge; no Vercel redeploy; no fresh deploy-tagged extension zip; no Rule 9 destructive-op gate fired). Director directive at session start ("please defer any testing in this session and work on the next item that does not require manual testing") forced a pivot from the previously-planned P-45 Build #2 DEPLOY session to a smaller polish item; §4 Step 1c forced-picker DID FIRE between P-44 vs. P-26 vs. "I have a question first." — director picked P-44 per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`. Does NOT close (a.67) RECOMMENDED-NEXT — (a.67) was P-45 Build #2, which remains deferred + unchanged in scope; opens (a.68) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-45 Build #2 (DEPLOY session — same 4-phase shape: Phase 1 dev-time verify on Amazon → Phase 2 /scoreboard GREEN → Phase 3 /deploy orchestration with Rule 9 director-Yes gate + ff-merge → main + push origin/main + Vercel auto-redeploy + ping-pong sync + fresh extension zip → Phase 4 director real-Chrome cross-platform verify across Amazon + Ebay + Walmart + Etsy) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added in this header batch** — the P-44 CLOSING §Entry (see body below at §Entry 2026-05-22-h); informational, not a slip-of-this-session; closes prior CORRECTIONS_LOG references to wxt build hang (2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g) with empirical root-cause confirmation + reusable Pattern for "how to escape a Node CLI tool's event-loop-drain hang via programmatic-API + process.exit(0)"; embeds ONE informational sub-observation — a P-43-bug-class second-reproduction at Claude's typing layer. While running Check 3 of /scoreboard inline (not via the slash-command verbatim), Claude typed `npm test 2>&1 | tail -10` WITHOUT the absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping &&` prefix that the scoreboard.md template specifies; shell ended up at `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub` and got loud-fail "Missing script: test"; caught immediately, re-ran with the absolute cd, got correct 558/558. This is the SECOND in-session reproduction of the same Claude-side discipline slip that P-43's closing entry captured yesterday (2026-05-22-g §Entry sub-observation #2). Empirical root-cause confirmation: added temporary diagnostic instrumentation that wrote raw PostToolUse stdin to `/tmp/plos-hook-debug.log`, triggered a disposable memory-file Write (`_diagnostic_test_p42.md`), captured the actual JSON stdin shape, and confirmed Claude Code's PostToolUse hook stdin uses the field name `.tool_name` — NOT `.tool`. All three P-42 reproductions across 2026-05-20-b, 2026-05-20-c, and 2026-05-21 stemmed from this single field-name mismatch. Three new test files (thumbnail-extraction.test.ts + video-region-record-overlay.test.ts + recording-indicator-overlay.test.ts) + `recording-bytes-upload.ts` line 72 hit TS18048 at the first scoreboard run on the extension tsc check. Fix: non-null assertions `!` on the test files (where the elements are known to exist by construction since the test sets them up) + `?? ''` fallback for the production `split[0]` case in recording-bytes-upload.ts (safer than `!` since split() can theoretically return an empty array on a pathological MIME string even though MediaRecorder won't produce one). Re-ran extension tsc in <5 min; clean pass. This is normal strict-mode-with-new-code, NOT a corrections-tier slip — the strict-mode check caught the unsafe indexed accesses exactly as it was supposed to; the fix is part of the change, not a correction. (2) **Director directive late-session deferred dev-time verify to Build #2.** Verbatim director instruction: *"please defer all testing for later and continue with whatever is next on the roadmap"*. Interpretation: the dev-time happy-path verify on Amazon that was Task #13 of the §C.20 ship checklist becomes Build #2's Phase 1 binding input. Per the 1a precedent for cross-session binding inputs (the 2026-05-22-d session's Phase 2 wiring tasks were closed as binding inputs to 1b rather than orphan TaskList DEFERRED entries since 1b was clearly-named + the wiring was its natural opening step), the verify is captured in NEXT_SESSION.md as Build #2's Phase 1 step + Task #13 marked deleted rather than DEFERRED. Build #2 needs to verify before deploy anyway under Rule 9 + §C.20 step 20 — combining verify + deploy into a single session is more natural than splitting verify into its own session. This is per-protocol handling of a director directive, NOT a corrections-tier slip. (3) **`validateCapturedVideoDraft` bytes-required gate broadened symmetrically to DIRECT_BYTES + SCREEN_RECORDING in `captured-video-validation.ts`.** Prior gate was `if (draft.sourceType === 'DIRECT_BYTES')`; new gate is `if (draft.sourceType === 'DIRECT_BYTES' || draft.sourceType === 'SCREEN_RECORDING')`. Symmetric per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` most-thorough/reliable framing — partial broadening (e.g. only checking size, not MIME) would risk SCREEN_RECORDING rows missing the 100 MB cap or accepting non-webm MIMEs. No test regression because existing tests don't exercise SCREEN_RECORDING; the new branch is additive coverage that will be exercised by the dev-time verify in Build #2. Captured here for traceability + as a forward signal that any future variant-introducing change to validators should consider symmetric broadening as the default. **Schema change** — no; Build #1b is pure wiring code on the existing schema (the SCREEN_RECORDING enum was added in 1a's `prisma db push` and is already live on Supabase as of 2026-05-22-d 14:09 UTC). Rule 14f forced-pickers fired at the correct moments (menu label picker at the start of Phase 1; scope-pacing 1a-vs-1-shot picker mid-Phase-2; Rule 9 destructive-op gate at `prisma db push`); Rule 18 append-only honored (the §C.11-§C.20 deepening was appended below the existing §C.0-§C.10 outline with the original outline untouched); Rule 23 Change Impact Audit ran for code (ADDITIVE) + schema (MIXED — enum value addition reversible at the database layer until consumers depend on it) both confirmed safe at audit time; Rule 9 destructive op gated correctly via AskUserQuestion picker. **Informational observations worth surfacing inline (not §Entries — just observations):** (1) **Rule 14f scope-pacing picker fired correctly mid-Phase-2** — when the original "single Build #1" framing surfaced as risking a 4-5 hour single-shot push past Rule 13's 90-minute trigger (destructive op + wiring + dev verify all in one), Claude offered a 3-way Rule 14f picker (Option A: split 1a foundation + 1b wiring [recommended per most-thorough/reliable]; Option B: continue as single Build #1 single-shot; Option C: narrow MVE). Director picked A. Operational evidence the protocol is working as designed — the scope-pacing picker successfully prevented a long session from spilling into a destructive-op-then-fatigued-wiring-then-rushed-verify failure mode. Pattern worth surfacing: scope-pacing pickers should fire whenever the same session would chain (a) a Rule 9 destructive op + (b) >1 hour of dependent code + (c) a director-cooperation step like dev-time verify, since fatigue on any of the three multiplies risk. (2) **Test regression caught + fixed within the scoreboard pass** — the `VIDEO_SOURCE_TYPES` deepEqual assertion in `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.test.ts:177` failed at the first scoreboard run after the enum tuple was extended from `['EMBED', 'DIRECT_BYTES']` to `['EMBED', 'DIRECT_BYTES', 'SCREEN_RECORDING']`. The assertion held the prior tuple verbatim. Fix: update the deepEqual expected value + add a new `isVideoSourceType('SCREEN_RECORDING')` test case. Re-ran in <2 minutes; baseline confirmed 590/590. This is a normal test-update-with-schema-change, NOT a corrections-tier slip — the failing test caught the schema change exactly as it was supposed to (the test exists specifically as a tripwire for tuple drift); the fix is part of the change, not a correction. (3) **Menu label "Record video for PLOS" locked via Rule 14f picker** — at the picker between "Record video for PLOS" (verb-first) and "Add to PLOS — Record Video" (parallel to existing "Add to PLOS — Captured Video"), director picked verb-first. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-22-c (One-hundred-and-twenty-ninth Claude Code session — **W#2 polish P-27 Captured-videos feature BUILD #9 DEVTOOLS-COOPERATIVE DEBUGGING + P-45 SCREEN-RECORDING DESIGN-PIVOT SESSION on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** Bug #12 Save-fails-Network-unreachable diagnosed in ONE DevTools-cooperative step (root cause: failing fetch URL is `blob:https://www.amazon.com/...` with `net::ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND`; Amazon + Ebay use Media Source Extensions; blob: URLs are unreachable from the background SW; Build #8's `retryOnTransportError` couldn't have ever cleared it because the failure is at the URL-fetchability layer not the transport layer). Director's escape-hatch question at the fix-direction picker surfaced **screen recording as a 4th universal solution** that bypasses the blob: + cross-origin walker + native-controls quirk classes of bugs by going through the user's screen as the source-of-truth. Director chose "skip the short-term fix — design screen recording properly NOW" at the sequencing picker, pivoting Build #9 from a fix-shipping session into a pure design-capture session. 12 P-45 design decisions locked through structured Rule 14f pickers (7 core + 5 default-locks). Rule 24 search ran with zero direct prior treatment found; P-17 region-screenshot infrastructure surfaced as ~50% reusable for the rectangle-drag overlay. **NO code commits this session — pure design + diagnostic work.** No deploy. No schema changes. No Rule 9 trigger. Closes (a.62) RECOMMENDED-NEXT (partial — Bug #12 diagnosed + design pivoted; Bug #11 + Bug #9 deeper-walk + Bug #15 deferred + partially obsoleted by P-45). The CORRECTIONS_LOG canonical purpose is to capture process / methodology / tooling / drift slips Claude makes (with prevention rules to keep them from recurring); today's session ran exactly per protocol — Rule 14f forced-pickers fired correctly (sequencing picker + 7 P-45 core decision pickers); Rule 18 interview-cluster pattern was followed (design first, code later); Rule 24 prior-treatment search ran with zero direct prior treatment found (P-17 region-screenshot adjacency captured as ~50% reusable); Rule 30 + §4 Step 4b template was honored throughout. The design pivot moment is per-protocol and the diagnostic outcome that triggered it (blob: URL class not reachable from background SW) is a product finding belonging in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-22 entry, not in CORRECTIONS_LOG. **Informational observations worth surfacing inline (not §Entries — just observations):** (1) **DevTools-cooperative diagnosis efficiency** — 1 step replaced what would have been a multi-build trial-and-error fix arc on the blob: URL class of bugs. The pattern "Claude narrates one diagnostic step + director performs + reports what they see" surfaced the root cause (visible URL in DevTools Network tab) on the first inspection. For future bugs where code-reading alone is insufficient (especially platform-DOM-quirk + network-fetch + browser-runtime issues), the DevTools-cooperative pattern should be the default first approach BEFORE trial-and-error code fixes. The 4 deferred bugs from Build #8 (#11 input dead + #12 fetch + #9 hover-preview walker + #15 Ebay quirk) all proved this class — code reading + defensive hardening helped some but not all; the failures that needed DevTools all surfaced their root causes once we asked. Consider drafting a future HANDOFF_PROTOCOL rule formalizing "DevTools-cooperative diagnosis as first-class debugging tool" if the pattern proves itself across multiple future debugging sessions. (2) **Escape-hatch question surfaces universal solutions** — at the fix-direction picker after Bug #12's root cause was clear, director asked the open-ended question "is there a way to capture videos that bypasses ALL of these failure classes" instead of jumping to "fix this specific bug." The question surfaced screen recording as a universal solution that bypasses blob: URLs (records through MediaStream of the screen) + cross-origin walker limits (visual capture doesn't need DOM traversal) + native-controls quirks (right-click triggers a separate menu item, not the video element's own controls). The pattern "ask the open-ended escape-hatch question before committing to a narrow fix" can save multi-build fix arcs by surfacing a single universal solution. Worth surfacing in future Rule 14f forced-picker shapes as an explicit "option E: is there an escape-hatch universal solution we haven't considered?" prompt. (3) **Design-pivot mid-debugging is fine when the diagnostic outcome warrants it** — Build #9 started as a debugging session (close 4 deferred bugs) and pivoted mid-session into a design-capture session (P-45 screen recording). The pivot is per-protocol (Rule 18 interview-cluster — design first, code later) and the binding output is the same as any other design session: §C section in the design doc + new ROADMAP polish-backlog entry + next-session pointer rewritten for the first implementation build. The pattern preserves the diagnostic finding (captured in §B 2026-05-22 entry) AND the new design surface (captured in §C). Both binding outputs land in one session without losing context. **Schema change** — no; Build #9 is pure design + diagnostic — no `prisma db push`, no schema edit; SCREEN_RECORDING enum will be added in P-45 Build #1, NOT this session. **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Doc-only this session (safe) — ZERO code changes; only design-doc append-only writes per Rule 18 + a new ROADMAP P-45 polish-backlog entry. Reversible at any point until P-45 Build #1 ships code. No new dependencies. No data risk. Workaround used (both times): `cp` the produced artifact from `.output/` to repo root with the canonical filename; call TaskStop on the hung background task. **Both reproductions in a single session is the strongest evidence yet that P-44 is reliable + recurring** — prior reproductions were one-per-session occurrences with occasional "no-reproduction" sessions sprinkled in (e.g., Build #7 had a clean `npm run zip` exit in <30 sec); today's twin-reproduction confirms the hang is the dominant behavior of `wxt zip` + `wxt build` parent processes in this Codespace environment, not an edge case. **Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — workaround works (the artifacts land correctly; the cleanup adds ~30-60 sec per call); zero production impact; zero user-facing impact. **Reinforces:** P-44 ROADMAP item — the polish item should ship as a priority since the parent-process hang now reliably blocks the canonical "build extension → zip extension → cp to repo root" pattern, requiring per-call workaround overhead. **At this reproduction rate (P-44 now reliable across MULTIPLE same-session invocations), P-44 should ship in the next non-P-27 session window.** **The 2 unfixed bugs + 1 new bug + 1 deeper-walk this session are product bugs (DOM-quirk + network-fetch + Chrome-native-menu issues), NOT corrections-log-tier protocol slips** — they belong in the design doc §B + verification backlog + as TaskList DEFERRED items #10-#13, not as CORRECTIONS_LOG entries. **Three mid-build + verification judgment calls captured in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-21 entry** (per Rule 18 append-only): (1) ship the 5 fixes vs. revert vs. defer — chose ship per §4 Step 1c (Etsy + Walmart + parts of Ebay + Amazon all improved; the surface-level user impact catches even when root cause didn't get exact-fixed); chose pure-fix for Bug #13 `<source src>` fallback where the cause was clear; (3) retry-on-TypeError safe vs. unsafe — chose safe because finalize routes dedupe on clientId server-side (videos/finalize + images/finalize + texts all dedupe; Closes (a.59) RECOMMENDED-NEXT (partial); opens (a.60) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-27 Build #7 (Deploy session — `/deploy` orchestration per `.claude/commands/deploy.md`: pre-deploy `/scoreboard` GREEN → Rule 9 director-Yes gate → ff-merge `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` → push origin/main → Vercel auto-redeploy → ping-pong sync → fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-22-w2-deploy-N.zip`) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** `session_2026-05-22_p27-build-6-playwright-specs` (FIFTH substantive session of the current P-27 Build arc — Builds #2 + #3 + #4 + #5 + #6 all chained sequentially since 2026-05-21). **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added in this header batch:** **P-43 CWD-drift reproduction #4 — `npm test` ran from root instead of extension dir at mid-scoreboard Check 5; caught from "Missing script: test" error; re-ran with absolute-path cd; baseline confirmed 482/482 on re-run; reinforces P-43 ship priority.** During the `/scoreboard` run mid-session, the Bash invocation for Check 5 was `npm test 2>&1 | tail -15` WITHOUT a leading `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping &&`. Each Bash invocation gets a fresh shell, so the `cd` from the prior parallel call (Check 2's extension tsc which did include the absolute-path cd) didn't carry over. Result: npm reported "Missing script: test" from root (root's `package.json` has no `test` script, only `test:e2e:*` Playwright entry points). Caught immediately from the error output (not from a misleading-but-not-erroring count this time — the failure was loud); re-ran the check with proper `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && npm test`; baseline confirmed 482/482 on re-run. **Slip class:** Bash invocation that depends on a specific CWD didn't carry over the prior call's `cd` (each Bash tool call gets a fresh shell — this is documented in the harness behavior); the prevention rule has been "use absolute paths for cd commands that need a specific CWD" since 2026-05-16, but the rule keeps catching real fingers across the P-27 Build arc. **Reproduction #4 in ~2 weeks** across the P-27 Build arc (prior reproductions: 2026-05-20-c parallel-Bash-relative-cd-CWD-race; 2026-05-21 reproduction #2 same parallel-cd-race during /scoreboard execution; 2026-05-21-d reproduction #3 sequential-Bash CWD leak from prior `cd` not being reset). Today's reproduction #4 is loud-fail variant (npm hard-errors instead of producing a misleading-but-not-erroring count); easy to catch from the error output. **Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — caught + corrected before any decision-relying-on-the-wrong-count was made; zero production impact; zero user-facing impact; the actual Build #6 implementation was correct. **Reinforces:** P-43 ROADMAP item — the `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` template polish to convert relative `cd` to absolute paths; the fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template to ALL Bash patterns in any skill or session that depend on a specific CWD. **At this reproduction rate (4 slips in ~2 weeks), P-43 should ship as the very next non-P-27 session.** **Three mid-build judgment calls captured in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-22 entry** (per Rule 18 append-only): (1) DIRECT_BYTES thumbnail assertion strictness — picked "tolerate both paths" per §A.12 NULL-thumbnail fallback; spec tracks `phase2ThumbnailFired` boolean; conditional Phase-2b assertion only when fired; conditional `thumbnailStoragePath` assertion on finalize (present iff Phase 2b fired); Phase-2b DID fire in headless mode on this run; reversible if future Chromium flips that behavior. (2) Embed-platform name capitalization — picked code-truth over branding per Rule 3; form source-kind banner reads `"Recognized as ${capitalize(embedPlatform)}"` per `video-capture-form.ts:182-184` (first-letter-upper from helper's lowercase 'youtube' → 'Youtube' single-capital NOT 'YouTube' camel-case); spec uses `toContainText('Recognized as Youtube')`; if form later changes to 'YouTube' for branding, spec + form code update in lockstep. (3) Right-click capture-phase listener dispatch — distinct from image spec; video orchestrator at `orchestrator.ts:927` reads ONLY `lastRightClickVideoResult` (no `msg.srcUrl` fallback unlike image orchestrator at line 896 which reads `msg.srcUrl || lastRightClickImageSrc || ''`); spec MUST dispatch a real `contextmenu` event on the target FIRST so the capture-phase listener populates the snapshot before the SW→tab open message reads it; implementation: `await page.dispatchEvent('#hero-video', 'contextmenu')` BEFORE the `serviceWorker.evaluate` chrome.tabs.sendMessage retry loop; documented in spec file header comment for future maintainers. **Schema change** — no; Build #6 is pure test code (no `prisma db push`, no schema edit; no shared-types schema changes; no extension source change; no backend code change). **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Additive (safe). New test files + new fixture only; ZERO existing files modified; zero downstream-consumer breakage risk; zero data risk. Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** single-branch session (workflow-2 only; no main push; no ff-merge); workflow-2 entered at `032b484` (Build #5 doc-batch — last commit on branch at session start), Build #6 commit `af0ed00` landed locally + needs end-of-session push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`; main remains at `a754aee`; no ping-pong sync needed since main didn't move. W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** Build #6 tasks ALL COMPLETED; ZERO `DEFERRED:`-prefixed tasks created or open at end-of-session. **Two pushes this session per `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md`:** (1) Build #6 code commit `af0ed00` push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping` (operationally adjacent to the end-of-session doc-batch push; no Rule 9 gate fired — no main push, no Vercel redeploy); (2) end-of-session doc-batch push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **End-of-session:** ONE doc-batch commit covering the 6-doc bundle (ROADMAP + CHAT_REGISTRY + DOCUMENT_MANIFEST + this CORRECTIONS_LOG + NEXT_SESSION + the §B append in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md`). After this doc-batch lands, workflow-2 is 12 commits ahead of main (10 prior + Build #6 + this doc-batch). **FOURTH end-of-session run under the Rule 30 + §4 Step 4b template** (first was 2026-05-21-b Build #3, second was 2026-05-21-c Build #4, third was 2026-05-21-d Build #5); the 3 plain-terms sections at the top of NEXT_SESSION.md + the parent's Personalized Handoff continue per director's standing 2026-05-21 directive.)

The subsequent `npm test` (Check 3) used `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npm test` and worked correctly. But Check 4 — `node --test --experimental-strip-types $(find src/lib -name '*.test.ts')` — used a relative `src/lib` path WITHOUT an explicit `cd` back to root, so it inherited the prior bash call's cwd (`/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping`) and ran the EXTENSION test suite again (482 tests reported, identical to Check 3's 482 — a duplicate-count signature that surfaced the error). Claude caught the slip from the duplicated test count + re-ran Check 1 + Check 4 with explicit absolute paths (`cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npx tsc --noEmit` and `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && node --test --experimental-strip-types $(find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib -name '*.test.ts')`). Re-run reported the correct src/lib count of **589/589**. **Slip class:** sequential-Bash CWD leak from a prior `cd` not being reset (adjacent to but distinct from the 2026-05-20-c + 2026-05-21 parallel-Bash CWD races — those slip classes are parallel races on shared CWD state; this slip class is sequential-Bash CWD leak from a prior bash call's `cd` persisting into the next call's relative path). Reproduction #3 in ~2 weeks across the P-27 Build arc; the recurring pattern confirms the CWD-leak class is reliably reproducible across sessions despite the prevention rule being documented since 2026-05-16. **Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — caught + corrected before any decision-relying-on-the-wrong-count was made; zero production impact; zero user-facing impact; the actual Build #5 implementation was correct — only the scoreboard reporting was briefly misleading. **Reinforces:** P-43 ROADMAP item — the `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` template polish to convert relative `cd` to absolute paths; the fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template to ALL Bash patterns in any skill or session that depend on a specific CWD. (3) Extension-side type-tightening — matched the new wire contract end-to-end per Rule 3 ("code is the ultimate source of truth"); runtime behavior unchanged. **Schema change** — no; Build #5 is pure code (no `prisma db push`, no schema edit; the only data-shape change is a new additive `CapturedVideoWithUrls` shared-type interface paralleling `CapturedImageWithUrls`; the wire-shape upgrade from `CapturedVideo[]` to `CapturedVideoWithUrls[]` is a strict superset). **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Additive (safe). The catch reinforces Rule 3 ("code is the ultimate source of truth"). **Slip class:** path-paraphrase drift in NEXT_SESSION authoring (adjacent to but distinct from the doc-vs-code drift entries captured 2026-05-17 + 2026-05-15 + 2026-05-19 — those caught semantic-doc-drift; this catches sibling-reference-shape path-paraphrase drift specifically in the launch-prompt's Pre-build read list section). Prevention nudge for future NEXT_SESSION authors: verify file paths via `ls` / `find` when listing reference shapes; do NOT loose-paraphrase paths from memory. **Severity:** both INFORMATIONAL — caught + corrected before any production impact; surface the operational lessons for future Claude sessions. **Schema change** — no; Build #4 is pure extension code (no `prisma db push`, no schema edit; no new API routes; no shared-types schema changes — the new `listCapturedVideos` api-client helper consumes the GET .../videos route shipped in Build #2). **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Additive (safe). strengthens the case for P-43 to ship soon and the fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template to ALL parallel-Bash patterns in any skill or session); (ii) `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook did NOT auto-mirror the new feedback-memory file — P-42 reproduction #3 (NEW memory file `feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md` was MISSING from `.codespace-backup/memory/` entirely + the updated `MEMORY.md` was STALE in the backup mirror — 3201 bytes from yesterday vs. source 3515 bytes from today; manually mirrored both via `cp` before the end-of-session commit; **three reproductions across three consecutive memory-write sessions** confirms the Layer-1 (Mechanical) gap is reliably reproducible + that director's memory is unsafe across any future Codespaces rebuild until P-42 ships). **Sub-observation under (ii):** background-task output capture wrote 0 bytes on two consecutive backgrounded `npm run build` attempts (`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/*.output` were 0-byte despite EXIT=0 confirmed via subsequent inline re-run); possibly a harness redirect/buffer issue specific to `npm run` commands that themselves spawn child processes; workaround: use explicit `> /tmp/file.log 2>&1` redirect with a known-stable path + run synchronously. Informational; not blocking. **Slip class:** (i) parallel-Bash-relative-cd-CWD-race; (ii) PostToolUse-hook-not-firing-on-memory-write. **Severity:** both INFORMATIONAL — caught + corrected before any production impact; surface the operational lessons for future Claude sessions. **Schema change** — no; Build #2 is pure code (API routes + shared-types + tests). **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Additive (safe). New optional API routes + new optional shared-type interfaces + new type guards + new tests. Zero existing-data risk; zero downstream-consumer breakage risk (no consumers exist yet for the new routes). Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** single-branch session (workflow-2 only; no main push; no ff-merge); workflow-2 entered at `e3f344e`, Build #2 commit `7093f2e` landed locally + needs end-of-session push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`; main remains at `a754aee`; no ping-pong sync needed since main didn't move. W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 4 tasks total — all 4 closed as `completed`. Zero open `DEFERRED:` items at session end. **NEW Rule 30 + §4 Step 4b template extension shipped this session** per director's verbatim 2026-05-21 standing directive: *"From here on, for every next session, I want you to also tell me in simple terms what we will do in the session and summarize what was done in the session and what we will do in the next session. I also want you to check and tell me what work is pending according to the roadmap in simple terms."* New operational-memory file `feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md` saved + indexed in MEMORY.md (both manually mirrored to `.codespace-backup/memory/` after P-42 reproduction #3). **Two pushes this session per `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md`:** (1) Build #2 code commit `7093f2e` push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping` (operationally adjacent to the end-of-session doc-batch push); (2) end-of-session doc-batch push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **End-of-session:** ONE doc-batch commit covering the 7-doc bundle (ROADMAP + CHAT_REGISTRY + DOCUMENT_MANIFEST + this CORRECTIONS_LOG + NEXT_SESSION + HANDOFF_PROTOCOL + CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER + the §B append in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md`).)

**Previously updated:** 2026-05-20-c (One-hundred-and-twenty-first Claude Code session — **W#2 polish P-27 Captured-videos feature BUILD SESSION #1 SHIPPED at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` — commit `c8fa639` lands the schema migration (`npx prisma db push` for new `CapturedVideo` table + new `VideoSourceType` enum + `video-category` added to `VocabularyEntry.vocabularyType` String column comment allowlist — NOT a Prisma enum per Build #1 mid-build refinement) + new Supabase Storage bucket `competition-scraping-videos` (private + 3-MIME allowlist; bucket-level 100 MB cap REJECTED by Supabase Global File Size Limit → deferred to dashboard offline step; size enforcement falls to app-layer per design doc §A.11 two-layer client + server pattern) + new helper modules + 41 new node:test cases. BUILD session — NOT a deploy; no main push; no Rule 9 gate; no Vercel redeploy; no fresh extension zip. Closes (a.54) RECOMMENDED-NEXT (partial); opens (a.55) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-27 Build #2 (API routes + shared-types extensions for route request/response shapes) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`.** `session_2026-05-20-c_p27-build-1-schema-bucket-helper`. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added in this header batch** — Parallel Bash `cd` shell-state collision during /scoreboard run (4 Bash calls fired in parallel, two using relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping`; shell state shared in a way the harness manages; one `cd` landed inside the extension dir first, then the second parallel Bash's relative `cd` errored trying to go into `extensions/competition-scraping/extensions/competition-scraping`; same collision caused a parallel `find src/lib -name '*.test.ts'` to silently look at the EXTENSION's `src/lib` and return 428 cases instead of expected 577 — looked correct because 428 happens to match the extension test count baseline coincidentally). **Slip class:** parallel-Bash-relative-cd-CWD-race. **Root cause:** the Bash tool's "working directory persists between commands" applies to sequential commands; parallel calls have non-deterministic ordering and can race on shared CWD state. The official Bash-tool guidance is "use absolute paths and avoid `cd`" — this incident is a concrete case where ignoring that guidance produced a misleading-but-not-erroring scoreboard result (the 428 count looked correct but was wrong on a different filesystem). **Impact:** caught during /scoreboard verification + re-run with absolute paths produced the correct 577/577 count; zero production impact; zero user-facing impact; the actual Build #1 implementation was correct — only the scoreboard reporting was briefly misleading. **Pattern strengthening:** when running multiple Bash calls in parallel that need specific CWDs, use absolute paths (`cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...`) instead of relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping`. Suggested follow-up: update `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` to use absolute paths throughout so the skill template itself doesn't carry the relative-cd footgun (worth a small templating polish item). **Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — caught + corrected before any decision-relying-on-the-wrong-count was made; no production impact; surfaces the operational lesson for future Claude sessions running parallel Bash with relative `cd`. **Schema change** — yes, additive (new `CapturedVideo` table + `VideoSourceType` enum + new `video-category` String comment-allowlist value); applied to live Supabase DB via `npx prisma db push`; schema-change-in-flight stayed "Yes" during the push window and flips back to "No" at end-of-session AFTER the Build #1 commit landed + the schema is verified-applied + no rollback needed; downstream API routes don't exist yet to break. **Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** Additive (safe). New optional table + new enum + new vocab string-value + new pure-function helpers + new SDK-only storage helpers. Zero existing-data risk; zero downstream-consumer breakage risk (no consumers exist yet for the new table). Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** single-branch session (workflow-2 only; no main push; no ff-merge); workflow-2 entered at `a754aee`, Build #1 commit `c8fa639` landed locally + needs end-of-session push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`; main remains at `a754aee`; no ping-pong sync needed since main didn't move. W#1 row untouched per Rule 3 ownership. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 9 tasks total — 8 closed as `completed` (#1-#8: Rule 8 pre-flight picker + schema-shape audit + schema write + bucket verify+create + helper wrapper + node:test cases + /scoreboard run + build commit); 1 task still pending — #9 DEFERRED: "Raise Supabase Global File Size Limit to enable bucket-level 100MB cap on competition-scraping-videos" (destination: ROADMAP P-27 polish-backlog entry sub-item; director's offline dashboard step). **Two pushes this session per `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md`:** (1) Build #1 code commit `c8fa639` push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping` (operationally adjacent to the end-of-session doc-batch push; no Rule 9 gate fired — no main push, no Vercel redeploy); (2) end-of-session doc-batch push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`. **End-of-session:** ONE doc-batch commit covering the 6-doc bundle (ROADMAP + CHAT_REGISTRY + DOCUMENT_MANIFEST + this CORRECTIONS_LOG + NEXT_SESSION + the §B append in `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md`).)

If a design doc claims an asymmetry that doesn't exist in code, future Build sessions waste cycles re-implementing UX that already ships. The loss was Claude's own internal operational memory + dev tooling — meta, not product. **Recovery this session:** all 10 memory files functionally reconstructed at the original path from `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` references (operational rules + cross-references are faithful; any nuance that lived ONLY in the originals is lost). **4-layer protective architecture shipped this session to prevent recurrence:** **MECHANICAL** — new `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook on Edit + Write mirrors every memory-dir write into `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/<basename>` (persistent volume; survives rebuild); wired into `.claude/settings.json` PostToolUse arrays. No Claude memory required; happens automatically. **PROCEDURAL** — new `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 29 "Pre-destructive-container-operation audit" (substantial section inserted between Rule 28 and §4) defining the trigger list (Codespaces: Rebuild Container, devcontainer.json edits, container reset, manual home-dir wipes), the Pre-Container-Audit mechanical checklist, the Rule 14f picker shape (A: backup-first recommended, B: proceed without backup, C: cancel, D: escape hatch), the recovery playbook with file list, the 4-layer architecture cross-reference, scope exceptions; new `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` (executable; one-command audit listing what's outside `/workspaces/` that would be wiped on rebuild — memory dir + global npm packages + ~/.local/bin/ + apt extras + credentials + shell profile + VS Code extensions; output structured for the Rule 14f picker); `docs/CLAUDE_CODE_STARTER.md` Rule 8 augment paragraph naming Codespaces rebuild as a destructive container operation requiring the Pre-Container-Audit per Rule 29. **DETECTION** — new `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` SessionStart hook on matcher=startup verifies the memory dir contains the 10 canonical files; emits 🚨 CRITICAL alert as additionalContext if missing/empty/partial-loss; wired into `.claude/settings.json` SessionStart array alongside existing inject-next-session-pointer hook. **RECOVERY** — new `scripts/restore-memory-from-backup.sh` (executable; idempotent recovery script with `--force` flag; verifies backup exists, refuses to overwrite populated dest without --force, restores files in sequence, reports success + count, falls through to reconstruction-from-HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md instructions if backup is also missing); new `.codespace-backup/` directory in persistent volume with tracked `README.md` + tracked `.gitkeep` + gitignored `memory/` subdirectory containing initial mirror of all 10 reconstructed memory files. **`.gitignore` updated:** gitignore `.codespace-backup/*` except `README.md` and `.gitkeep`. **What would have caught this:** Rule 29's Pre-Container-Audit picker — running `scripts/codespace-rebuild-audit.sh` BEFORE recommending the rebuild would have surfaced the memory directory + the CLI binary + global npm packages + apt extras as "will be wiped" items, triggered the Rule 14f forced-picker, and director would have chosen the backup-first path. **Severity class:** HIGH — distinct from the recurring cwd-leak / wxt-hang / hook-substring-match / API-overload patterns captured prior; this is the first slip where Claude's own persistent operational memory was destroyed AND the slip was not Claude-the-executor's fault but rather Claude-the-prior-session-recommender's fault (the recommendation lacked an audit step). **Closes (a.51) + (a.52) RECOMMENDED-NEXT P-18 fully** — verification PASS captured. **Opens (a.53) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-27 Captured-videos feature DESIGN session.** **NO schema change** — config + tooling + procedural rule only; This time the hang lasted ~12 minutes before I killed the process; dist artifact verified correct via grep for the new helper's signature (`Ne=\`—\`` minified form of the em-dash constant). Three concurrent build attempts also raced earlier in the session due to multiple `npm run build` invocations during my diagnosis of the hang — these wrote into the same `.output/` dir concurrently but exited with code 0 per the notification stream; no corruption observed in the final dist. **Future-session prevention recommendation:** add a `pretest` Playwright hook that runs `wxt build` if `.output/` is older than any `src/` file, AND/OR investigate why `wxt build` doesn't exit cleanly (suspect: dev-server keep-alive after build, or a stdout buffer that's flushed lazily). Captured here for the future-session record so the wxt-build-hang doesn't surprise a fresh session. (2) **Launch-prompt drift caught at session-start (Rule 3 — code wins) — opposite of a slip, exactly the failure mode the start-of-session diagnosis-steps discipline exists to catch.** Launch prompt named `url-add-form.ts` as a second fix site but grep of `productName?\.trim\(\)` across the extension showed that file has no saved-URL `<select>` at all (its only `opt.textContent = entry.value` lines are inside the content-category vocab picker shipped as P-36). Root cause appears to be at the launch-prompt write time 2026-05-19-f: the writer asserted "url-add-form.ts has a similar `<select>` for the existing-URL picker" without grepping the file. Operational principle: when a launch prompt names specific file/line as a fix site, the writer SHOULD grep the pattern in that file to confirm the site exists BEFORE writing the prompt. Not severe enough to warrant a §Entry; captured informationally because it's the THIRD time a session-N launch prompt's "X is already shipped/captured" assertion has been wrong (2026-05-19-d Rule 3 catch on P-16 wrap-async-paths; 2026-05-19-e Rule 3 catch on originalSrcUrl existence; today's session-#28 Rule 3 catch on url-add-form having a saved-URL dropdown). Same root cause as the 2026-05-17 doc-vs-code drift entry: polish-backlog capture at original-flag time didn't do full Rule 24 pre-capture search. **Plus a smaller test-setup ambiguity captured in-session (not a CORRECTIONS_LOG-tier slip):** during P-38 director-verification, all 3 test cases (A/B/C) initially appeared failed; investigation showed director's "1-URL" test combo actually had 2+ URLs (so P-38 correctly skipped pre-select — matched Test B's expected behavior, not Test A's). Pattern lesson for future sideload-verification test-script writes: when the test depends on a specific data-shape preconditions (e.g., "exactly N URLs"), the script SHOULD include a counting step the director performs FIRST to confirm preconditions before running the test proper. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-18 (One-hundred-and-seventh Claude Code session — **W#2 → main deploy session #17 — P-28 (delete URLs with cascade disclosure) + P-27 (delete captured texts/images) ALL DEPLOYED to vklf.com + REAL-INDEPENDENT-WEBSITE FULL VERIFY across Parts A-E + IN-SESSION SCOPE-ADD P-33 row hover-highlight SHIPPED + paint-bug HOT-FIXED + DEPLOYED + VERIFIED.** `session_2026-05-18_w2-main-deploy-session-17-p28-p27-DEPLOYED-FULL-VERIFY-plus-P-33-hover-tackon`. Closes (a.39) RECOMMENDED-NEXT. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added below this batch** — P-33 first-attempt `<tr>`-background-paint slip. Adding hover effects to a table by setting inline `background` on the `<tr>` is unreliable when `<td>` cells render on top (the `<td>` cells' transparency is sometimes painted-over). The codebase already had the established convention of applying hover background to `<td>` cells via CSS (`mt-tbl tbody tr:hover td { ... }` in `mt-table.css`; same pattern in `ast-table.css` + `canvas-table-mode.css`). Original Rule 14e pre-capture grep should have surfaced this prior treatment but the first attempt jumped to inline-on-`<tr>` without checking. First attempt commit `1794eef` shipped + deployed but invisible on production. Hot-fix commit `507f7d6` swapped to `querySelectorAll<HTMLTableCellElement>('td')` to set background on each cell + bumped color to `#21262d`. Pattern lesson: when adding hover effects to a table, **grep `tr:hover` against `*.css` in `src/` FIRST** — if the codebase has an established pattern, follow it. Net cost ~5 min (one extra deploy cycle). Zero production impact (invisible-hover state was harmless; clicks + navigation still worked). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** dual-branch session — pre-deploy + first tack-on scoreboard on workflow-2; ff-merge + deploy + hot-fix on main; ping-pong sync after each main push (two cycles). Schema-change-in-flight stays "No" entire session. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 12 session tasks tracked + all 12 closed; zero `DEFERRED:` items. **End-of-session:** doc-batch commit + push.)

Closes (a.37) RECOMMENDED-NEXT. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added below this batch** — cwd-leak Bash slip recurred TWICE this session (pre-deploy scoreboard + post-merge scoreboard). When a parallel Bash batch contains ANY `cd <subdir>` command, the working directory leaks to subsequent commands in the same batch. Both instances caught immediately via test-count anomaly (334 instead of 509) and recovered with explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub &&` prefix. This is the 4th-or-5th recurrence of the slip class (2026-05-12-c original + 2026-05-13 + 2026-05-14 + 2026-05-15-d + today). Pattern STRENGTHENING from prior entries: when running parallel Bash calls where ANY command uses `cd`, EVERY command in the batch must explicitly prefix with the desired absolute cwd. Net cost ~30 seconds; zero production impact. **Deploy mechanics:** clean ff-merge `3443971..f18e146` of 4 commits onto main; rebase no-op (workflow-2 linearly 4 commits ahead since deploy #15's ping-pong sync); pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboards both GREEN (tsc / extension tsc / `npm run build` 52 routes / src/lib node:test 509/509 / extension `npm test` 334/334 / Playwright 64/64); Rule 9 deploy-gate via AskUserQuestion picker → director picked Yes; Vercel auto-redeployed cleanly. **Director walkthrough on real Independent Website URL:** P-32 multi-file-drop ✅ (warning text "2 files dropped — only the first will be used" + first image previewed) + 4 P-29 area spot-checks ✅ (URL add / text add / image drag / image fetch-by-URL all saved cleanly, zero regression from P-31's DI refactor). **HEADLINE: P-29 manual-add area now fully live on vklf.com + end-to-end regression coverage across UI mechanical (P-30 Playwright) + API route-handler integration (P-31 node:test +62 cases) + real-website director walkthrough.** **§4 Step 1c forced-picker fired** at end-of-session — director picked **(a.38) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = W#2 P-28 + P-27 paired build session** (delete URLs with cascade + delete captured texts/images). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** dual-branch session — pre-deploy verification on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`, ff-merge + deploy phases on `main`, ping-pong sync. Closes (a.36) RECOMMENDED-NEXT. **ONE NEW INFORMATIONAL §Entry added below this batch** — original Rule 14f forced-picker for P-31 DI seam shape was shown (Option A per-route closure factory / B module-level pure function / C three-layer adapter) without first verifying whether `next/server` could be imported under `node --test --experimental-strip-types`. Director picked Option A. Mid-flight probe revealed `next/server` is NOT in Next's `package.json` `exports` field — Node ESM cannot resolve `import { NextRequest } from 'next/server'`. The original Option A premise (tests construct `new NextRequest(...)`) was not viable. Surfaced honestly mid-flight + ran a second Rule 14f forced-picker (A' RequestLike contract in factory / B switch to pure function / C add vitest+next plugin) → director picked Option A' (RequestLike contract; Next types only at the route boundary). Net cost ~3 min. Pattern lesson: future Rule 14f pickers involving test-runner constraints should verify the constraint BEFORE the picker fires, not after. Concrete operational test — when about to surface a Rule 14f picker that involves a test-runner mechanism (node:test / vitest / playwright / etc.), run a 30-second probe of the smallest viable case BEFORE drafting the picker; if the probe surfaces a constraint, the picker's option set + recommendation marker change. **HEADLINE P-31 DELTA: src/lib node:test went from 447/447 → 509/509 in a single session** — exactly +62 new P-31 cases (16 urls + 15 url-text + 16 images-finalize + 15 fetch-by-url) well over the launch-prompt's "30-40" target. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-14 (Ninety-fifth Claude Code session — **W#2 polish session #18 — P-23 Amazon main-image right-click context-menu fix SHIPPED at code level on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`** — `session_2026-05-14_w2-polish-session-18-p23-amazon-right-click-context-menu-SHIP`. **1 NEW INFORMATIONAL entry this batch** prepended at top of §Entries — Playwright capture-phase listener-attach race caught + fixed before commit. Pattern lesson: content-script tests that exercise capture-phase event listeners (`document.addEventListener(..., { capture: true })`) must attach the listener BEFORE the `data-plos-cs-active=1` signal Playwright keys off — placing the listener after async init produces a false-negative test failure even when the production code path is correct. Fix: hoist the listener attach to the TOP of `runOrchestrator` (before any awaits), add `detachContextMenuListener()` calls to each early-return path. No production impact — caught + recovered same-session via a clean source re-architecture. **P-23 ship verification scoreboard all GREEN:** ext tsc clean; ext `npm test` 334/334 in 3.6s (was 323; +10 new `findUnderlyingImage` unit tests + 1 refactor); root Playwright extension project 31/31 in 1.3 min (was 29; +2 new specs — positive overlay-shield + negative plain-text); ext build clean in 1.42s; content.js 62,437 → 63,038 bytes (+601 bytes). Real-Amazon browser verification DEFERRED to next W#2 → main deploy session #13. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** session stayed on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` end-to-end; pull-rebase clean at session start (workflow-2 ↔ origin/main at same commit post-deploy-#12 ff-merge); schema-change-in-flight stays "No"; W#1 row untouched per Rule 3. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 6 session tasks tracked + all closed; zero `DEFERRED:` items at session end. **End-of-session:** ONE commit containing code + tests + doc batch (single-commit shape per launch-prompt framing); push deferred per Rule 9 deploy-gate principle.)

(ii) Subsequent grep on docs/ROADMAP.md from a different cd'd dir errored "No such file or directory"; recovered via absolute paths. Yesterday's §Entries lesson captures the canonical fix (prefer absolute paths over `cd`; for backgrounded commands, redirect to file rather than pipe through filters); today's recurrence shows the lesson is not yet internalized — but the recovery path is fast and the slips don't compound. **Decision: no new §Entries entry needed** — adding a third identical entry would add noise without new insight. **Previously updated:** 2026-05-14 (Ninety-second Claude Code session — **W#2 → main deploy session #11 — Module 2 region-screenshot gesture DEPLOYED + cross-platform FULL VERIFY across all 4 platforms** — `session_2026-05-14_w2-main-deploy-session-11-region-screenshot-DEPLOYED-FULL-VERIFY`. **2 NEW INFORMATIONAL entries this batch** — both process observations / operational-pattern captures with no production impact; entries inserted at top of §Entries section below. (i) **Launch-prompt staleness pattern** — director's pasted launch prompt said "deploy session 5 + 6 (both unverified on vklf.com)" but main had drifted: session 5's deploy + full verify had already completed on main earlier (commit `907a363` (a.24-verify-COMPLETE) and commit `bd7b39a` deploy-#10). The launch prompt was generated by `docs/NEXT_SESSION.md` on the workflow-2 branch BEFORE main got the verification-complete entry; on session start the drift surfaced during the mandatory start-of-session sequence's `git log origin/main..HEAD` step. Caught before any code work; surfaced to director cleanly via Rule 14f forced-picker with 3 options (reconcile + deploy session 6 only [recommended] / cherry-pick session 6 only / pause + revise scope). Director picked recommended Option A. Lesson: at start of every W#2 → main deploy session, the FIRST git command after branch verification should be `git log origin/main..workflow-2` AND `git log workflow-2..origin/main` to surface parallel-branch divergence BEFORE reading the launch prompt's framing as authoritative. The launch prompt is a snapshot; the branch state is the truth. (ii) **Doc-batch-empty-on-rebase pattern** — during the rebase of 7 workflow-2 commits onto origin/main, commit `68fd93d` ((a.24-a) P-22 slice 2 end-of-session doc batch) became EMPTY after taking `git checkout --ours` for all its conflict files. Reason: main's parallel (a.24-verify-COMPLETE) session had already prepended doc entries that fully subsumed the (a.24-a) session's intended entries (same "Last updated" slot in CHAT_REGISTRY + ROADMAP + DOCUMENT_MANIFEST; (a.24-a)'s content was already covered by HEAD's more-recent (a.24) entry). `git rebase --continue` silently dropped the empty commit. Similarly the merge commit `8c39f20` (Merge main into workflow) was naturally collapsed by the non-merge rebase. Lesson: parallel-branch doc-batch commits often become empty/duplicate when rebased onto the receiving branch's parallel doc-batch; this is the EXPECTED clean operational pattern. Take `--ours` (keep HEAD's already-updated docs); `git rebase --continue` will silently drop empties. Future deploy sessions facing similar divergence should expect 0–2 empty commits dropped per rebase, treat as routine, document in the deploy doc batch as "N commits dropped (M empty after --ours; rebase naturally collapsed K merge commits)." **All 2 entries INFORMATIONAL** — no production impact; the rebase + deploy + verification all succeeded cleanly. **Cross-references:** ROADMAP W#2 row Last Session 2026-05-14 prepended + (a.24) flipped ✅ DONE + new (a.25) RECOMMENDED-NEXT P-20 design session; CHAT_REGISTRY new top entry; DOCUMENT_MANIFEST per-doc flags; COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG new "Deploy session #11" section; NEXT_SESSION.md rewritten for P-20 design session. **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** dual-branch session — rebase on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`, ff-merge + verification on `main`. Pull-rebase clean at session start. Schema-change-in-flight stays "No". W#1 row untouched per Rule 3. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 6 session tasks tracked + all 6 completed; P-26 captured + closed after destination ROADMAP entry written; zero open `DEFERRED:` at session end. **Four pushes this session:** force-push origin/workflow-2 (rebased); local ff-merge; push origin/main (deploy push, Rule 9-approved via deploy describe); end-of-session doc-batch push to origin/main (covered by deploy-approval scope per `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md`).)

**Previously updated:** May 14, 2026 (Ninetieth Claude Code session — **(a.23) Walmart Phase 2 PASS + P-22 Playwright slice 1 SHIPPED.** ONE INFORMATIONAL entry (Low severity) added this batch — see below: **Playwright `context.route` LIFO matching gotcha — caught before first test run via reasoning, fixed without wasting a test cycle.** While building the P-22 image-capture happy-path spec, I added a catch-all route for `**://*.vklf.com/api/.*` AFTER several specific routes (listCompetitorUrls, listVocabularyEntries, requestUpload, finalize) to defensively 404 any unmocked vklf.com paths so unmocked fetches wouldn't hit production or hang on real-network fall-through. Realized BEFORE running the spec that Playwright `context.route` matches handlers in REVERSE registration order (most recently registered wins). The catch-all registered LATER would therefore intercept the listCompetitorUrls / vocab / requestUpload / finalize paths FIRST — returning 404 for everything specific — making the test useless. Re-organized: catch-all + bare `/api/projects` mock registered FIRST, then specific path routes registered AFTER so the specifics take priority. Test passed on first real run with the corrected ordering. **Lesson:** Playwright's `context.route` LIFO matching is documented but easy to forget when reasoning about route layering. The mental model "specific routes win over catch-alls" is correct in concept but inverted from registration order. When using `context.route` for layered mocking, register catch-alls FIRST and specifics AFTER so the most-recently-registered (= specific) routes match first. Caught self via reasoning rather than test feedback — saved a test-debug cycle. **No process slip — this is an INFORMATIONAL entry capturing the lesson for future Playwright route-mocking work.** Cross-references: `tests/playwright/extension/image-capture.spec.ts` (the slice 1 spec; comments in the route blocks document the LIFO concern explicitly so future readers don't re-trip on it); HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 27 (the forced-picker rule that brought us to Playwright); the prior 2026-05-12-c CORRECTIONS_LOG entries on Playwright harness-setup slips (different sub-class of Playwright lesson but same parent theme: extension-context testing has more gotchas than vanilla Playwright). **Multi-Workflow per Rule 25:** dual-branch session — verification on `main`, P-22 code on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. Schema-change-in-flight stays "No". W#1 row untouched. **TaskList sweep per Rule 26:** 14 session tasks, 13 completed, 1 `DEFERRED:` task (Phase 3 cross-platform manual smoke; destination VERIFICATION_BACKLOG Phase 3 table + ROADMAP (a.24) options; closed at sweep). Zero open `DEFERRED:` at session end. Cross-references in addition to above: ROADMAP W#2 row Last Session 2026-05-14 + (a.23) ✅ PARTIAL DONE + (a.24) RECOMMENDED-NEXT options + P-22 polish entry SLICE 1 SHIPPED; CHAT_REGISTRY new top row; DOCUMENT_MANIFEST per-doc flags + this-session summary; COMPETITION_SCRAPING_VERIFICATION_BACKLOG.md Phase 2 ✅ PASS + Phase 3 ❌ DEFERRED + new (a.23) section; COMPETITION_SCRAPING_DESIGN.md §B P-22 test-shape decisions.)
