## §Entry 2026-05-20-b — INFORMATIONAL — Rule 24 end-of-session catch — design doc §A.8 wrongly claimed asymmetry that doesn't exist in code (P-27 design session)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-20-b_p27-captured-videos-design-interview` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; DOC-ONLY — no code, no schema, no deploy).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL. No production impact. No user-facing impact. Caught + corrected before the doc-batch commit landed; the corrected `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §A.3 / §A.8 / §A.18 narrative now accurately reflects code reality.

**What happened.** During the P-27 design interview, the design doc §A.8 "Vocab: `video-category` type + inline '+ Add new' UX (Q9)" narrative claimed that the existing text + image capture forms force an admin-page-first vocab creation flow (i.e., that to add a new content-category or image-category the director has to leave the capture form, navigate to the admin vocabulary page, create the new entry, then return to the capture form). The narrative used this asymmetry-claim as the motivating context for the Q9 forced-picker (which picked Option C — inline "+ Add new category" affordance in the video capture form). A `DEFERRED:` task (#10) was also created mid-session to add a cross-cutting symmetry-restore polish item to ROADMAP after the design session (to retrofit the inline-add UX onto text + image forms too).

**The catch.** At end-of-session, before promoting task #10 from `DEFERRED:` to an actual ROADMAP polish-backlog entry, I ran the Rule 24 pre-capture search per the standard discipline. The grep `inline.*add\|Add new category\|onCreateCategory` across the extension source tree returned 4 hits in shipped code:

- `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/text-capture-form.ts:305` — inline "+ Add new category" affordance shipped.
- `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/image-capture-form.ts:368` — same.
- `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/url-add-form.ts:244` — same.
- `extensions/competition-scraping/src/entrypoints/popup/components/CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:262` — same.

Cross-checked against `docs/ROADMAP.md` P-13 polish backlog entry which confirmed: P-13 "Autofocus on '+ Add new…' inline category input (popup only — overlay surfaces already had `.focus()` calls; original capture mislisted overlay in 'Where')" was SHIPPED. The inline-add UX exists across all 4 capture surfaces. The design doc §A.8 asymmetry-claim was wrong.

**Slip class.** Make-claims-about-existing-UX-inside-a-design-doc-without-running-Rule-24-search. Distinct from the recurring cwd-leak / wxt-hang / hook-substring-match patterns. The same Rule 24 + Rule 3 discipline that's routinely applied BEFORE adding ROADMAP entries was not applied DURING design-doc authoring; the §A.8 narrative was written from cached recollection rather than re-verified against code.

**Root cause.** Rule 24 ("Pre-capture search before adding ROADMAP item") is operationally scoped to ROADMAP capture moments — start-of-session drift checks + end-of-session new-item captures. The same discipline should apply at design-doc-authoring time too, because design docs are binding specs for future Build sessions. The slip happened because Rule 24's wording focuses on ROADMAP captures, and the design-doc-authoring context was outside the rule's literal scope.

**Impact.** Zero user-facing impact. Zero production impact. Caught + corrected pre-commit. The corrected §A.3 / §A.8 / §A.18 narrative now accurately documents that:

- §A.3 v1 scope — the inline-add UX is NOT a P-27-specific new feature; it inherits the existing pattern across text + image + URL forms.
- §A.8 vocab-category UX — Q9's pick of Option C (inline "+ Add new category" affordance) extends the EXISTING pattern to videos; it does NOT introduce a new pattern.
- §A.18 deferred-items registry — task #10 (DEFERRED: cross-cutting symmetry-restore) closed as SUPERSEDED; no ROADMAP polish item needed.

**What would have caught this earlier.** Running Rule 24 grep DURING the design-doc §A.8 narrative authoring (not just at end-of-session) would have caught the claim before it landed in the doc. The end-of-session re-check caught it before commit, which is the next-best safety layer.

**Pattern strengthening (no new rule — operational discipline).** ALSO run Rule 24 / Rule 3 checks BEFORE making claims about existing UX in design docs, not just for ROADMAP-capture moments:

- ✅ DO grep for any existing UX patterns referenced in a design doc's narrative BEFORE writing the narrative.
- ✅ DO cross-check ROADMAP polish-backlog entries for prior treatment of the UX pattern (e.g., P-13 here documented the inline-add UX).
- ✅ DO run the same end-of-session re-check on ANY design doc shipped this session, not just ROADMAP.
- ❌ DON'T author design-doc narrative claims about existing UX from cached recollection.

**Why this matters operationally.** Design docs are binding specs for future Build sessions. If a design doc claims an asymmetry that doesn't exist in code, future Build sessions waste cycles re-implementing UX that already ships. The cost of NOT catching this slip pre-commit would have been multi-session wasted work in the future Build arc (a hypothetical "symmetry-restore" polish session would have grepped the code at start, found the UX already shipped, and closed the item as no-op — costing one full session's overhead for no value). Catching pre-commit avoids the multi-session waste.

**Fix shipped in this session.** Design doc §A.3 / §A.8 / §A.18 corrected. Task #10 closed as SUPERSEDED. ROADMAP P-27 polish-backlog entry annotated to note the inline-add UX already exists for text + image + URL surfaces. This INFORMATIONAL §Entry documents the slip + the pattern strengthening for future sessions.

**Cross-references.**

- `docs/CAPTURED_VIDEOS_DESIGN.md` §A.3 / §A.8 / §A.18 (the corrected sections; commit-batch with this Entry).
- `docs/ROADMAP.md` P-13 polish backlog entry (the prior treatment that proves the inline-add UX already exists).
- `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/text-capture-form.ts:305` + `image-capture-form.ts:368` + `url-add-form.ts:244` + `CapturedTextPasteForm.tsx:262` (the 4 shipped instances of the inline-add UX).
- `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 24 (Pre-capture search before adding ROADMAP item) — the rule whose discipline this Entry extends to design-doc-authoring time.
- `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 3 (Code is source of truth) — the meta-rule that this Entry's discipline operationalizes for design-doc claims.
- `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 18 (Workflow Requirements Interview pattern) — the rule under which the P-27 design doc was authored.

## §Entry 2026-05-20-b — INFORMATIONAL — `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook did NOT auto-fire for this session's memory writes (4-layer memory-loss-prevention architecture mechanical-layer gap)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-20-b_p27-captured-videos-design-interview` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL for now (manually-mirrored at end-of-session — no data loss). **WOULD BE HIGH** if not caught — the mechanical layer of Rule 29's 4-layer memory-loss-prevention architecture failed silently; if a Codespaces rebuild had been recommended for the next session, the 2 new memory files would have been wiped without backup. Caught by the plos-doc-batch agent's verification + parent Claude's response.

**What happened.** This session created 2 NEW memory files (`feedback_destructive_ops_confirmation.md` + `feedback_remaining_roadmap_summary.md`) via the Write tool, plus an Edit to `MEMORY.md` to register them in the index. Per Rule 29's 4-layer architecture, the PostToolUse hook `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` should have auto-fired on each Write/Edit and mirrored the new/changed files into `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/`. At end-of-session, the plos-doc-batch agent flagged the gap: source dir had 12 files; backup mirror still had 10; the backup MEMORY.md was older + smaller than source (mirror hadn't ingested the index update either).

**Root cause (unconfirmed; needs investigation in a future session).** The hook script itself (`.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh`) is well-designed — graceful exit on missing stdin / missing jq / non-matching tool / non-matching file path. Wired in `.claude/settings.json` per the canonical `PostToolUse` matcher. Yet it did not fire (or fired silently-failing) for this session's writes. Possible causes:

1. PostToolUse hook matcher mis-configured (only fires for specific tools? specific paths? wrong matcher shape?).
2. The hook stdout/stderr was swallowed (no log visible to verify whether fire-attempts occurred).
3. The `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` env var was not set at hook-fire time → `BACKUP_DIR` resolved to a wrong path → silent fail at the `[ -d "$BACKUP_DIR" ] || exit 0` guard.
4. The hook fired for a *different* tool-matcher than Edit/Write — e.g., Claude's internal memory-write may invoke a tool other than Edit/Write (a possibility worth verifying).
5. The Write tool's stdin JSON shape may have changed between when the hook was authored (2026-05-20) and now (also 2026-05-20 — same date but different time-of-day — so unlikely, but worth checking).

**Slip class.** Mechanical-layer-of-Rule-29-architecture-silently-failed. First real-world test of the mirror layer; first real-world FAIL. The detection layer (SessionStart canary `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh`) would not have caught this gap because the source memory dir was healthy — only the BACKUP mirror was stale. The canary checks the source, not the mirror.

**Impact this session.** Zero data loss. Detected pre-commit; manually mirrored via 3 `cp -p` commands; verified 12/12 in sync post-mirror. Documented here.

**What would have caught this earlier.** A "mirror-staleness canary" — an additional SessionStart hook or end-of-session check that compares source memory dir against backup mirror (file count + per-file md5/mtime) and alerts on divergence. Currently does not exist; the existing `check-memory-dir-status.sh` only checks the source's health, not the source-vs-mirror sync.

**Pattern strengthening (followup polish item for the next session to capture).** Investigate why the PostToolUse hook didn't fire + ship one of:

- (a) Fix the hook wiring / matcher / env-var issue so it fires correctly on every memory write.
- (b) Add a mirror-staleness canary as an additional SessionStart hook (compares source vs. mirror; alerts on divergence; auto-syncs if safe; logs to CORRECTIONS_LOG if not).
- (c) Both (a) + (b) — defense-in-depth.

Estimated investigation + fix scope: ~1 session (LOW-severity LOC-wise; HIGH operational importance because it touches the architecture that's supposed to prevent the 2026-05-20 memory-loss recurrence).

**Fix shipped THIS session.** Manual mirror via 3 `cp -p` commands; backup now in sync (12 source / 12 backup). This INFORMATIONAL §Entry documents the gap + the pattern-strengthening followup so the next session captures it as a ROADMAP polish item.

**Cross-references.**

- `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (the hook that didn't fire).
- `.claude/hooks/check-memory-dir-status.sh` (the SessionStart canary that wouldn't have caught this kind of staleness).
- `.claude/settings.json` (hook wiring — needs audit).
- `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` (the mirror destination).
- `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 29 (Pre-destructive-container-operation audit) — the architecture this hook is one mechanical layer of.
- `CORRECTIONS_LOG.md` 2026-05-20 entry "Codespaces rebuild silently wiped memory dir" (the original slip that triggered the 4-layer architecture).

## §Entry 2026-05-20-c — INFORMATIONAL — Parallel Bash `cd` shell-state collision during /scoreboard run produced a misleading-but-not-erroring test count (P-27 Build #1 session)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-20-c_p27-build-1-schema-bucket-helper` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; BUILD session — schema + bucket + helper SHIPPED at code level via commit `c8fa639`; no deploy, no main push).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL. Zero production impact. Zero user-facing impact. The actual Build #1 implementation was correct — only the scoreboard reporting was briefly misleading; re-run with absolute paths produced the correct 577/577 count.

**What happened.** During the /scoreboard step of P-27 Build #1, 4 Bash calls were fired in parallel as part of the routine scoreboard verification. Two of those calls used relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping`. The Bash tool's documented "working directory persists between commands" semantics applies to sequential calls; parallel calls have non-deterministic ordering and can race on shared CWD state. The race went like this:

1. Parallel Bash #1 (relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npx tsc --noEmit`) executed first; landed CWD inside `extensions/competition-scraping/`.
2. Parallel Bash #2 (also relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npm test`) executed second; its relative `cd` was now interpreted from the already-changed CWD, so it tried to navigate into `extensions/competition-scraping/extensions/competition-scraping/` and errored `No such file or directory`.
3. Parallel Bash #3 (`find src/lib -name '*.test.ts'`, intended to enumerate root src/lib test files) ran from the already-changed CWD too, so it silently found the EXTENSION's `src/lib/` test files (count 428) instead of the root's `src/lib/` test files (count 577).
4. The 428 result looked correct because 428 happens to match the extension `npm test` baseline count coincidentally — the same number that the EXTENSION's own test count shows. The scoreboard table briefly reported "src/lib node:test 428/428" instead of the correct "577/577."

**The catch.** Noticed the inconsistency when reconciling the scoreboard table against the +41 expected delta from this session's `competition-video-storage-helpers.test.ts` additions (baseline 536 + 41 = 577, not 428). Re-ran the parallel checks one at a time with absolute paths, confirmed the correct 577/577 result, and corrected the scoreboard report before any decision was made on the basis of the wrong count.

**Slip class.** Parallel-Bash-relative-cd-CWD-race. Distinct from the prior recurring cwd-leak / wxt-hang / hook-substring-match patterns (each session's relative-cd cwd leak was about a sequential cd that landed and stayed; this session's slip is about parallel calls racing on shared CWD state — a different mechanic with the same operational symptom).

**Root cause.** The Bash tool's documentation says: "The working directory persists between commands, but shell state does not." That sentence is correct for sequential calls but is silent on what happens for parallel calls. Empirically, parallel Bash calls SHARE the CWD state (one cd in any call affects the others' starting CWD) and the order in which the parallel commands evaluate their relative paths is non-deterministic. The official Bash-tool guidance ("use absolute paths and avoid `cd`") covers exactly this footgun — I ignored that guidance because the /scoreboard skill template uses relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping` throughout and it has worked fine in sequential single-thread runs.

**Impact.** Zero production impact. Zero user-facing impact. The actual implementation (schema + bucket + helper + tests) was correct; only the scoreboard reporting was briefly wrong. Caught + corrected before any decision-relying-on-the-wrong-count was made.

**What would have caught this earlier.** Following the Bash-tool official guidance ("use absolute paths and avoid `cd`") from the outset. The slip happened because the /scoreboard skill template at `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` uses relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping && ...` patterns throughout — the template itself carries the relative-cd footgun and propagates it to every session that uses the skill.

**Pattern strengthening (no new rule — operational discipline + suggested template polish).**

- ✅ DO use absolute paths (`cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...`) when running multiple Bash calls in parallel that need specific CWDs.
- ✅ DO sanity-check scoreboard counts against expected deltas — if the count "looks correct but matches a coincidental baseline," double-check whether the right tree was searched.
- ❌ DON'T assume "working directory persists between commands" extends sanely to parallel calls — it doesn't.
- ✅ DO update `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` to use absolute paths throughout (suggested polish item — low-LOC, removes the footgun from the canonical skill template so future sessions don't repeat this slip).

**Why this matters operationally.** /scoreboard runs are the canonical verification gate at end-of-build sessions. A wrong scoreboard count is exactly the kind of "looks correct but isn't" failure mode that the discipline of running scoreboard is meant to catch — when the scoreboard itself is the failure surface, the meta-verification (sanity-checking the count against expected deltas) becomes load-bearing. This Entry surfaces the lesson so future sessions don't get blindsided by the same race.

**Fix shipped in this session.** Re-ran the parallel checks with absolute paths; corrected the scoreboard report to 577/577; landed the correct count in this session's header narratives. This INFORMATIONAL §Entry documents the slip + the suggested follow-up to update `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md`.

**Suggested follow-up (capture as a small templating polish item if director picks).** Update `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` to use absolute paths (`cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...`) instead of relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping && ...`. Removes the relative-cd footgun from the canonical skill template; preserves the same behavior for sequential single-thread runs while making parallel runs safe. ~5-10 LOC; sub-1-hour polish.

**Cross-references.**

- `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (the skill template that carries the relative-cd footgun; suggested polish target).
- Bash tool documentation: "The working directory persists between commands, but shell state does not." (the documentation that's silent on parallel-call CWD semantics).
- Bash tool official guidance: "Try to maintain your current working directory throughout the session by using absolute paths and avoiding usage of `cd`." (the guidance whose ignoring produced this slip).
- Prior CORRECTIONS_LOG cwd-leak entries (different mechanic — sequential cd that landed and stayed; this Entry is about parallel cd race on shared CWD state).
- `docs/HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` Rule 26 (TaskList sweep) — the discipline whose execution surfaced this slip via the scoreboard mismatch against expected deltas.

---

## §Entry 2026-05-21 — INFORMATIONAL — Parallel-Bash relative-`cd` shell-state collision REPRODUCED during /scoreboard (P-27 Build #2 session) — reproduction #2 of the 2026-05-20-c entry; strengthens case for P-43

**Session:** `session_2026-05-21_p27-build-2-api-routes-shared-types` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; BUILD session — API routes + shared-types extensions SHIPPED at code level via commit `7093f2e`; no deploy, no main push).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL. Zero production impact. Zero user-facing impact. The actual Build #2 implementation was correct — only the scoreboard reporting was briefly misleading; re-run with absolute paths produced the correct 57-route count.

**What happened.** During the /scoreboard step of P-27 Build #2, the second `npm run build` call ran in the `extensions/competition-scraping/` directory (NOT the root) because an earlier parallel `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && npx tsc --noEmit` had leaked CWD state to a sibling parallel Bash call that issued `npm run build` from a now-changed working directory. Symptom: the build's tail-output showed `wxt build` (the extension build) instead of Next.js's compiled-successfully line + route table. Caught + corrected by re-running with explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build > /tmp/next-build.log 2>&1` (absolute path + full-path output redirect). Final scoreboard reported the correct 57 routes after the corrected re-run.

**Why this is reproduction #2.** The 2026-05-20-c §Entry captured the same class of slip — parallel-Bash relative-`cd` CWD-race producing a misleading-but-not-erroring scoreboard count. That session was a P-27 Build #1 session running /scoreboard. This session is a P-27 Build #2 session running /scoreboard. Same skill template (`.claude/commands/scoreboard.md`); same parallel-Bash invocation pattern; same race; same operational symptom (misleading-but-not-erroring scoreboard output). **Two consecutive Build sessions reproducing the same slip means the fix is not "use more discipline next session" — the fix is to remove the relative-cd footgun from the skill template itself (P-43).**

**Slip class.** Parallel-Bash-relative-cd-CWD-race. Identical to the 2026-05-20-c entry.

**Root cause.** Identical to the 2026-05-20-c entry — the Bash tool's documented "working directory persists between commands" semantics applies sanely to sequential calls but races on shared CWD state across parallel calls. The official Bash-tool guidance ("use absolute paths and avoid `cd`") covers exactly this footgun; the /scoreboard skill template's continued use of relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping` perpetuates the footgun across every session that runs the skill.

**Impact.** Zero production impact. Zero user-facing impact. The actual implementation (4 new API routes + 7 new shared-types interfaces + 12 new tests) was correct; only the scoreboard reporting was briefly wrong. Caught + corrected before any decision-relying-on-the-wrong-count was made. The 57-route count IS the correct final number.

**Strengthens the case for P-43 to ship soon.** This is the SECOND reproduction of the same class of slip in two consecutive Build sessions. P-43 (`.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` template polish — convert all relative `cd` to absolute paths) was captured 2026-05-20-c after the first reproduction. The 2026-05-21 reproduction makes the case stronger:

1. The slip is reliably reproducible (not a one-off — two for two).
2. The slip is class-1-bug (silent-failure that produces plausible-but-wrong output, not a crash).
3. The fix is well-scoped (sub-1-hour template polish; estimated 5-10 LOC).
4. The fix needs to extend beyond just `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` — ALL parallel-Bash patterns in any skill or session need absolute paths. The fix shape is "convert this skill template + add a discipline reminder to any other relevant skill templates that issue parallel Bash."

**Sub-observation (informational; NOT a separate §Entry).** During this session's scoreboard run, two consecutive backgrounded `npm run build` attempts (via the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`) wrote 0-byte output files at `/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/bfsuehlic.output` and `/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/bq2edwsqh.output` despite the process completing (EXIT=0 confirmed via subsequent inline re-run). Symptom: `wc -l` returned 0, `tail -40` returned empty, `cat` returned empty on both output files. Possibly a harness redirect/buffer issue specific to `npm run` commands that themselves spawn child processes (Next.js + WXT). Workaround: use explicit `> /tmp/file.log 2>&1` redirect with a known-stable path + run synchronously. Not blocking; capture as informational for future sessions to know.

**Cross-references.**

- 2026-05-20-c §Entry (reproduction #1 of the same slip class).
- ROADMAP P-43 (the polish item this slip's recurrence strengthens the case for — now LOW-MEDIUM elevated toward MEDIUM after reproduction #2).
- `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (the skill template carrying the relative-cd footgun; P-43's fix target).
- Bash tool documentation + official guidance ("use absolute paths and avoid `cd`").
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 26 (TaskList sweep — the discipline that surfaces these scoreboard-mismatch slips).

---

## §Entry 2026-05-21 — INFORMATIONAL — `backup-memory-dir.sh` PostToolUse hook did NOT auto-mirror the new feedback-memory file (P-42 reproduction #3 — Layer-1 Mechanical gap of P-41 4-layer protective architecture)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-21_p27-build-2-api-routes-shared-types` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; BUILD session).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL at session level (caught + manually remediated before commit). But the underlying GAP captured as P-42 is **HIGH severity** — see ROADMAP P-42 for the architectural significance.

**What happened.** At end-of-session, before staging the doc-batch commit, verified the backup mirror at `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` against the source at `/home/codespace/.claude/projects/-workspaces-brand-operations-hub/memory/`. Found:

- The new `feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md` (written this session via Write tool to capture the standing director directive about plain-language session bookends) was MISSING from the backup mirror entirely.
- The updated `MEMORY.md` (which gained one new line for the new feedback-memory entry) was STALE in the backup mirror — backup version was 3201 bytes from yesterday; source was 3515 bytes from today.

Manually mirrored both via `cp` before the end-of-session commit:
```
cp /home/codespace/.claude/projects/-workspaces-brand-operations-hub/memory/feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/
cp /home/codespace/.claude/projects/-workspaces-brand-operations-hub/memory/MEMORY.md /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/
```

Source + backup now in sync at session-end.

**Why this is reproduction #3.** P-42 was first captured 2026-05-20-b after Claude wrote 2 NEW memory files + edited `MEMORY.md`; the doc-batch agent's end-of-session verification flagged that `.codespace-backup/memory/` had 10 files vs. source's 12 + backup MEMORY.md was 2472 bytes vs. source's 2945 bytes. P-42 was then reproduced 2026-05-20-c — same gap, NEW memory file `feedback_handoff_carryovers_to_roadmap.md` + edited `MEMORY.md` did not mirror automatically. Now reproduced AGAIN 2026-05-21 — same gap, NEW memory file + edited `MEMORY.md` did not mirror automatically.

**Three reproductions across three consecutive memory-write sessions** confirms:

1. The PostToolUse hook (`.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh`) does NOT reliably fire on memory-file writes — at minimum, not on Write tool calls + Edit tool calls that target the memory directory. The exact failure mode (matcher mis-config / env-var not passed / tool name mismatch / hook stdout swallowed) remains diagnostically unconfirmed but the operational symptom is consistent.
2. The Layer-1 (Mechanical) gap of P-41's 4-layer protective architecture is reliably reproducible — every memory-write session reproduces the gap.
3. Director's memory is unsafe across any future Codespaces rebuild until P-42 ships. The other 3 layers (Procedural Rule 29 / Detection canary / Recovery script) help only if the backup is current. A stale backup makes Detection fire late (after loss) + makes Recovery restore an OLD snapshot.

**Slip class.** PostToolUse-hook-not-firing-on-memory-write. Identical mechanic to 2026-05-20-b + 2026-05-20-c.

**Root cause (still unconfirmed; needs investigation per P-42 fix shape).** Possible candidates:

- PostToolUse hook matcher mis-configured in `.claude/settings.json` — fires for specific tools/paths only; the Write/Edit calls this session may not have matched.
- `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` env var unset at hook-fire time → `BACKUP_DIR` resolves wrong → silent fail at `[ -d "$BACKUP_DIR" ]` guard.
- The hook fires for a tool-matcher other than Edit/Write — Claude's internal memory-write may use a different tool.
- `jq` missing or path-mismatched on this Codespace — graceful-degradation exit in the hook would make this silent.
- Hook stdout/stderr swallowed by Claude Code so silent-failure is invisible.

**Impact.** Zero production impact (PLOS + vklf.com unaffected). Zero user-facing impact. **HIGH operational impact at the architectural level** — defeats Layer 1 of the 4-layer memory-loss-prevention guarantee.

**Manual remediation shipped this session.** Mirrored both files via `cp` before the end-of-session commit; verified source + backup are in sync at session-end via `ls -la` + `wc -c`.

**Strengthens the case for P-42 to ship before any future big session.** Three reproductions across three consecutive memory-write sessions makes P-42 the highest-priority operational-tooling polish item open. Every reproduction confirms the gap is real + reliably reproducible + that the manual `cp` remediation is load-bearing across session boundaries.

**Cross-references.**

- ROADMAP P-42 (the captured polish item — three reproductions now annotated).
- ROADMAP P-41 (the 4-layer protective architecture P-42 is a Layer-1 gap of).
- 2026-05-20-b §Entry (P-42 reproduction #1 — original capture).
- 2026-05-20-c P-42 reproduction #2 narrative (in 2026-05-20-c CORRECTIONS_LOG header batch — not a standalone §Entry but documented as a session-end manual mirror).
- `.claude/hooks/backup-memory-dir.sh` (the hook script — well-designed; graceful-exit on empty stdin / missing jq / non-matching tool/path; needs debug logging to surface why it's not firing).
- `.claude/settings.json` (hook wiring — needs audit).
- `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub/.codespace-backup/memory/` (the mirror destination).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 29 (Pre-destructive-container-operation audit — the procedural layer this composes with).
- `feedback_session_bookends_plain_summary.md` (the NEW memory file this session; the trigger that exposed reproduction #3).

## §Entry 2026-05-22-g — INFORMATIONAL — P-43 CLOSING ENTRY — empirical fix + reusable Pattern for "how to harden a parallel-Bash slash-command template against CWD-leak" (closes the four prior P-43 §Entries)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-22-g_p43-absolute-paths-scoreboard-deploy-ship-polish-item` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; PURE LOCAL INFRASTRUCTURE session paralleling yesterday's P-42 detour — `.claude/commands/` slash-command template polish only; no production code touched; no main push; no ff-merge; no Vercel redeploy; no fresh extension zip; no Rule 9 destructive-op gate fired).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL at session level — this is the CLOSING §Entry for the four-reproduction P-43 class (2026-05-20-c original capture + 2026-05-21 reproduction #2 + 2026-05-21-d reproduction #3 + 2026-05-22 reproduction #4). It captures the empirical fix shape + the reusable Pattern for future templates so the class doesn't recur in any other `.claude/commands/` slash-command template or future skill file.

**Headline empirical fix.** Build commit `4afea35` — 3 files changed +20/-20:

- `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` — Check 1 gained absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub &&` prefix; Checks 2, 3, 6-rebuild converted from relative `cd extensions/competition-scraping` to absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping`; Check 4 `$(find src/lib -name '*.test.ts')` converted to absolute `$(find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib -name '*.test.ts')`; baselines refreshed from stale 2026-05-19-g values (53 routes / 536 src/lib / 428 ext / 79 Playwright) to current 2026-05-22-g values (57 / 590 / 558 / 94).
- `.claude/commands/deploy.md` — the fresh-zip step at Step 8 normalized to absolute extension-dir cd; baselines section refreshed identically.
- `.claude/commands/ship-polish-item.md` — Steps 8 + 9 normalized — two relative cds + one relative `find src/lib` substitution → all four absolute.

**Scope expansion beyond the ROADMAP entry's narrow scoreboard.md-only fix shape.** The original P-43 ROADMAP entry's same-session commentary across reproductions #2 + #3 + #4 explicitly recommended extending the fix to ALL `.claude/commands/` templates (verbatim: *"The fix needs to extend beyond just the /scoreboard skill template — ALL parallel-Bash patterns in any skill or session need absolute paths"*). Three files + baseline refresh is the most-thorough fix per Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation — partial scope (only scoreboard.md) would have left deploy.md + ship-polish-item.md with the same CWD-leak class waiting to bite the next deploy or polish session. No forced picker fired for the scope-add — small / reversible / additive within the same polish item per the `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` exception.

**Most-thorough verification (Task #9).**

- Re-grep across `.claude/commands/` returns ZERO remaining relative-cd or relative `find src/lib` patterns.
- Checks 1-5 all GREEN at exact baselines after fix: root tsc clean (unchanged) / extension tsc clean (unchanged) / extension `npm test` **558/558** (unchanged) / src/lib node:test **590/590** (unchanged) / `npm run build` **57 routes** EXIT=0 (unchanged).
- Re-ran fixed Checks 1, 3, 4 from `/tmp` (an arbitrary non-repo-root cwd — the precise scenario all four prior P-43 reproductions hit) — ALL GREEN at exact baselines; same fixed commands produce identical correct counts regardless of starting cwd.
- Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention.

**Reusable Pattern — "how to harden a parallel-Bash slash-command template against CWD-leak":**

1. **Any `cd` in a slash-command template MUST be absolute, not relative.** When Claude fires multiple parallel Bash calls and any uses a relative `cd`, the cwd-leak between parallel shells lands siblings in the wrong starting directory. Each of the 4 prior P-43 reproductions surfaced differently — wrong test counts that looked plausible (#1 the 428 vs. 577 silent-mismatch), `wxt build` running from the wrong dir (#2), the src/lib find inheriting extension-dir cwd (#3), and "Missing script: test" loud-fail (#4) — but all stemmed from the same relative-cd race. Absolute paths make the same fixed commands produce identical correct results regardless of the starting cwd of the parallel shell.

2. **Any `$(find ...)` substitution SHOULD also use an absolute starting directory** as belt-and-suspenders against Claude shortening or dropping the explicit cd prefix when typing commands inline. The shell substitution runs in the current shell's cwd, so even if the parent command has a correct absolute `cd`, a relative `find src/lib` inside `$(...)` would still race if Claude omits the `cd` when re-typing the command. Absolute `find /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/src/lib ...` is immune to this.

3. **Baselines in slash-command templates should be refreshed every time scoreboard counts drift** — stale baselines in the template (57 vs. 53 routes / 590 vs. 536 src/lib / 558 vs. 428 ext / 94 vs. 79 Playwright) make the human-readable "expected vs. observed" comparison less useful for catching real regressions. Refresh as part of any P-43-class fix.

4. **Verification = re-run the template's commands from an arbitrary non-repo-root cwd** to prove the absolute-path discipline survives cwd drift. This is the precise scenario all four prior reproductions hit; running from `/tmp` (or any other non-repo-root) is the canonical verification.

**Informational sub-observations within this §Entry (NOT separate §Entries):**

**Sub-observation 1: Build #5's backgrounded `npm run build` 0-byte-output reproduction recurrence.** The 2026-05-21 CORRECTIONS_LOG already documented this — backgrounded `npm run` commands write 0-byte output capture files even when the actual build process is running. Reproduced again this session when Check 5 was backgrounded — output file stayed 0 bytes even though the build process was running. Canonical workaround applied: killed the backgrounded task via TaskStop, killed lingering processes via `pkill`, then re-ran synchronously with explicit `> /tmp/p43-build.log 2>&1` redirect. Build completed cleanly EXIT=0 at 57 routes. Reinforces that the npm-run-backgrounding bug is reliably reproducible + the workaround is now well-established. Captured here as an informational sub-observation rather than a new top-level §Entry (same pattern as the 2026-05-21 sub-observation in the prior P-43 §Entry).

**Sub-observation 2: Claude's own test slip when writing the verification.** When writing a verification test command for `/tmp`, Claude DROPPED the absolute cd prefix from Check 3, then was momentarily surprised when `npm test` errored with ENOENT. This is itself a perfect demonstration of the pre-fix bug class — Claude shortens or drops cd prefixes when typing commands inline, which is exactly why slash-command templates need absolute paths so Claude cannot accidentally produce the wrong-shell-cwd bug class even when reading the template verbatim. Caught immediately + re-ran the verification with the proper full command. Informational; reinforces the fix.

**Schema change** — no; P-43 is `.claude/` slash-command template polish only; no schema, no API, no shared-types.

**Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** ADDITIVE + DEFENSIVE — repairs an already-broken parallel-Bash pattern + refreshes stale baselines to current ground truth; no production code path touched; no extension or web bundle affected. No new dependencies. No data risk. Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact.

**Cross-references.**

- ROADMAP P-43 (the captured polish item — now ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-g; four reproductions all closed by this session's fix).
- 2026-05-20-c §Entry (P-43 reproduction #1 — original capture during P-27 Build #1 /scoreboard run; the misleading 428 count vs. expected 577).
- 2026-05-21 §Entry "Parallel-Bash relative-`cd` shell-state collision REPRODUCED" (P-43 reproduction #2 during P-27 Build #2 — `wxt build` ran in extensions dir instead of root).
- 2026-05-21-d P-43 reproduction #3 narrative (Build #5 — `find src/lib` inherited extension-dir cwd from prior parallel call's cd; reported extension test count of 482 instead of src/lib 589).
- 2026-05-22 P-43 reproduction #4 narrative (Build #6 — extension `npm test` ran from repo root; npm hard-error "Missing script: test"; loud-fail variant).
- `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (the file polished — all relative cds now absolute; baselines refreshed).
- `.claude/commands/deploy.md` (the file polished — same scope expansion per the ROADMAP entry's commentary).
- `.claude/commands/ship-polish-item.md` (the file polished — same scope expansion).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 27 (the deploy-verification methodology /scoreboard is the canonical tool for; this fix hardens that tool against the recurring CWD-leak class).
- Bash tool guidance (the official guidance Claude historically ignored: *"use absolute paths and avoid `cd`"*).

## §Entry 2026-05-22-h — INFORMATIONAL — P-44 CLOSING ENTRY — empirical fix + reusable Pattern for "how to escape a Node CLI tool's event-loop-drain hang via programmatic-API + process.exit(0)" (closes prior wxt build hang references from 2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g) + embedded P-43-bug-class second-reproduction sub-observation

**Session:** `session_2026-05-22-h_p44-wxt-zip-build-parent-process-hang-fix` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; PURE LOCAL INFRASTRUCTURE session — THIRD polish-detour in a row ahead of P-45 Build #2's deploy session — 2026-05-22-f was P-42 backup-memory hook fix; 2026-05-22-g was P-43 absolute-paths discipline; today is P-44; no production code touched; no main push; no ff-merge; no Vercel redeploy; no fresh deploy-tagged extension zip; no Rule 9 destructive-op gate fired).

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL at session level — this is the CLOSING §Entry for the multi-reproduction `wxt build` parent-process hang class first documented as informational observations in 2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g §Entries and captured as ROADMAP P-44 on 2026-05-20-c. It captures the empirical root cause + the reusable Pattern for future Node CLI tools that hang despite their primary work completing, so the class doesn't recur the next time Vite or a similar bundler ships a major-version native-binding refactor.

**Headline empirical fix.** Build commit `57ed383` — 5 files changed +61/-11:

- NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` (16 LOC) — programmatic-API wrapper: `import { build } from 'wxt'; await build(); process.exit(0);` with browser-flag passthrough (parses `-b firefox` from argv) and try/catch for non-zero exit on error.
- NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (16 LOC) — same shape but calls `zip()` instead of `build()`.
- MODIFIED `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` — 4 scripts now route through the wrappers: `"build": "node scripts/wxt-build.mjs"` + `"build:firefox": "node scripts/wxt-build.mjs -b firefox"` + `"zip": "node scripts/wxt-zip.mjs"` + `"zip:firefox": "node scripts/wxt-zip.mjs -b firefox"`.
- MODIFIED `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` — replaced the multi-paragraph "KNOWN ISSUE" workaround section that documented `pkill -f "wxt build"` with a one-paragraph note that the wrapper now handles this + points readers at `scripts/wxt-build.mjs` if a hang regresses.
- MODIFIED `.claude/commands/deploy.md` — same KNOWN ISSUE → wrapper-note swap for the `npm run zip` step. Preserved the historical CORRECTIONS_LOG cross-references (2026-05-19-f + 2026-05-19-g) and added a new 2026-05-22-h pointer.

**Empirical root-cause confirmation (the headline finding).** The hang stems from wxt 0.20.25 → Vite 8.0.11 → Rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 (the new Rust-based bundler that replaced Rollup in Vite 8). The native Rolldown binaries (`@rolldown/binding-linux-x64-gnu` etc.) spawn worker threads that aren't released when the build promise resolves, leaving Node's event loop with active handles. wxt's CLI awaits the build then relies on natural event-loop drain — which never happens.

Confirmed via:

- Reading wxt's CLI internals (`node_modules/wxt/dist/cli/commands.mjs`, `node_modules/wxt/dist/core/build.mjs`, `node_modules/wxt/dist/core/builders/vite/index.mjs`).
- The `isRolldownVersion(vite.version)` check in wxt's vite builder returns `true` for Vite ≥8.
- Vite 8.0.11's package.json confirms rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 dependency + the `@rolldown/binding-linux-x64-gnu` native binding presence.
- wxt 0.20.26 + 0.21.0 changelogs reviewed via GitHub releases — no relevant fix upstream.
- Vite upstream issue #18224 documents related "Vite can hang the node.js process" pattern.

**Scope expansion beyond the ROADMAP entry's narrow `wxt build` shape (Rule 24).** ROADMAP entry P-44 (line 205) names the bug as the `wxt build` parent-process hang. The fix scope expanded to ALSO cover `wxt zip` because:

- `wxt zip` internally calls `internalBuild()` from the same `core/utils/building/internal-build.mjs` (verified by reading `node_modules/wxt/dist/core/zip.mjs`).
- Both commands exhibit the identical hang signature for the identical root cause.
- Both are invoked from `npm run` scripts in the same package.json.
- Fixing build but not zip would leave the deploy session's Step 8 (fresh extension zip) still hanging.

The `.claude/commands/deploy.md` KNOWN ISSUE block called out BOTH `wxt build` AND `wxt zip` in its `pkill -f` workaround, confirming the scope. Symmetric fix across both wrappers is the most-thorough Rule 14f Default-to-recommendation choice — partial scope (build only) would have left the deploy mechanics still tripping on the zip hang.

**Pre-fix reproduction confirmed.** `rm -rf .output && timeout 45 npm run build` hung past 45s (exit 124 = timeout), dist visible at +6s, process still alive at +36s.

**Post-fix verification (all from `extensions/competition-scraping/`).**

- `npm run build` cold (after `rm -rf .output`): **4s, exit 0**, dist valid.
- `npm run zip` cold: **3s, exit 0**, **202,601-byte zip artifact valid** at `.output/competition-scraping-extension-0.1.0-chrome.zip`.
- `npm run build` warm (no clean): 4s, exit 0.
- 3x rapid-fire `npm run build`: 3s each, all exit 0 (rules out flakiness).

**Pre-end-of-session /scoreboard ran from `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub`.** All 5 checks GREEN at exact baselines:

- Check 1 root tsc: clean.
- Check 2 extension tsc: clean.
- Check 3 extension `npm test`: **558/558** (exact baseline — no test count change since P-44 is purely a build-tooling fix).
- Check 4 src/lib node:test: **590/590** (exact baseline).
- Check 5 `npm run build`: **57 routes** (exact baseline).
- Check 6 Playwright: SKIPPED per non-deploy-session convention (mirrors P-42 + P-43 detours).

No regressions.

**Reusable Pattern — "how to escape a Node CLI tool's event-loop-drain hang":**

1. **When a Node CLI tool hangs after its primary work completes, suspect lingering worker threads / native bindings holding event-loop handles open.** The hang doesn't happen during the work itself; the work completes (artifact on disk, success log printed). The hang happens during shutdown, when Node's event loop refuses to drain because something is keeping handles active. This is especially common with Rust-based bundlers (Rolldown), worker_threads pools (esbuild), and FFI native modules that don't expose explicit close/dispose APIs.

2. **Check whether the CLI exports a programmatic API.** Most modern Node CLIs (wxt, vite, esbuild, swc) expose their core functionality as importable functions. If yes, wrapping it with `await xyz(); process.exit(0);` bypasses the broken event-loop drain entirely. The wrapper is typically 10-20 LOC: import the function, parse minimal argv, await the call, force-exit on success, try/catch with non-zero exit on error.

3. **Safe to force-exit AFTER artifact verification.** The hang happens during shutdown, not during the work. By the time `await xyz()` returns, all userland work has completed (file on disk, log emitted). `process.exit(0)` is safe at this point — there's no async cleanup the userland code needs.

4. **Parallel `npm run` script rewires keep the wrapper symmetric across browsers + production/zip variants.** When fixing one variant (e.g. `build`), check sibling scripts (`build:firefox`, `zip`, `zip:firefox`) for the same hang class. Rewire all four through the wrapper to avoid asymmetry that bites later when a different script is invoked.

5. **Replace KNOWN ISSUE blocks in slash-command templates with one-paragraph wrapper notes.** When a tactical workaround (e.g. `pkill -f "wxt build"`) is replaced by a proper fix (programmatic-API wrapper), the slash-command template should be updated to remove the workaround block AND retain a one-paragraph note pointing readers at the wrapper if a hang regresses. This preserves the operational knowledge without leaving the obsolete workaround in place.

**Embedded informational sub-observation (NOT a separate §Entry).**

**Sub-observation 1: P-43-bug-class second-reproduction at Claude's typing layer.** While running Check 3 of /scoreboard inline (not via the /scoreboard slash command — just executing the steps in Bash directly), Claude typed `npm test 2>&1 | tail -10` WITHOUT the absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...` prefix that the scoreboard.md template specifies. The shell ended up at `/workspaces/brand-operations-hub` (the prior cwd) and got "Missing script: test" because the repo root's package.json has no `test` script. Caught immediately, re-ran with the absolute cd, and got the correct 558/558 result.

This is the SECOND in-session reproduction of the same Claude-side discipline slip that P-43's closing entry captured yesterday (2026-05-22-g §Entry sub-observation #2). P-43's template hardening (rewriting all `.claude/commands/` cd invocations as absolute paths) successfully protects the verbatim-template-read pathway, but does NOT protect against Claude typing "shortened" inline Bash that drops the cd prefix. That's a Claude-discipline issue at the typing layer, not a template issue.

**Pattern:** when Claude executes a /scoreboard or /deploy step manually (inline Bash) rather than running the slash command verbatim, the absolute-cd discipline still needs to be applied at the typing layer. The template hardening is necessary but not sufficient. The loud-fail variant ("Missing script: test") is easy to catch from the error output; the silent-mismatch variant (wrong but plausible counts) is harder and is what motivated P-43's original capture.

**Severity:** LOW informational, NOT a procedural failure — Claude caught it within seconds (loud-fail "Missing script: test" error), recovered cleanly, no production impact. Captured here for traceability + as a forward signal that any future inline /scoreboard or /deploy step execution should include explicit absolute-cd discipline check before any Bash call.

**Schema change** — no; P-44 is extension build tooling polish only; no schema, no API, no shared-types.

**Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** ADDITIVE + DEFENSIVE — the programmatic-API wrappers bypass an already-broken wxt+Vite8+Rolldown event-loop drain; no production code path touched; no extension or web bundle affected (the artifacts produced are identical to the prior hang-then-pkill flow, just without the hang); no schema; no shared-types; no API routes; only `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/` wrappers + `package.json` script rewires + `.claude/commands/` KNOWN ISSUE note swaps. No new dependencies (wxt's programmatic `build()` + `zip()` are already exported from the wxt package; no `npm install` ran). No data risk. Zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact.

**Cross-references.**

- ROADMAP P-44 (the captured polish item — now ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-h; multi-reproduction hang class closed by this session's fix).
- 2026-05-19-f CORRECTIONS_LOG references (the informational notes documenting the hang behavior + the `pkill -f "wxt zip"` workaround).
- 2026-05-19-g CORRECTIONS_LOG references (additional informational notes during the deploy session that surfaced the hang again).
- 2026-05-22-g §Entry "P-43 CLOSING ENTRY" sub-observation #2 (the first in-session reproduction of the Claude-discipline slip at the typing layer — this entry's sub-observation #1 is the second reproduction).
- `extensions/competition-scraping/scripts/wxt-build.mjs` + `scripts/wxt-zip.mjs` (the NEW programmatic-API wrappers shipped this session).
- `extensions/competition-scraping/package.json` (the 4 script rewires).
- `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` + `.claude/commands/deploy.md` (the KNOWN ISSUE blocks replaced with wrapper-notes this session).
- wxt CLI internals (`node_modules/wxt/dist/cli/commands.mjs`, `core/build.mjs`, `core/builders/vite/index.mjs`, `core/zip.mjs`, `core/utils/building/internal-build.mjs`) — read this session to confirm `isRolldownVersion()` returns true for Vite ≥8 + that `wxt zip` shares the same `internalBuild()` codepath as `wxt build`.
- Vite 8.0.11 package.json (the rolldown 1.0.0-rc.18 dependency).
- Vite upstream issue #18224 (the documented "Vite can hang the node.js process" pattern that mirrors this root cause).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 14f (Default-to-recommendation — the Rule 24 scope expansion to include `wxt zip` was a default-to-recommendation outcome since `wxt zip` is structurally identical to `wxt build`).

## §Entry 2026-05-22-i — INFORMATIONAL — P-45 CLOSING ENTRY — empirical fix narrative for all 3 Phase-1 fix-forward issues + 3 Reusable Patterns + 2 embedded P-43-bug-class re-reproduction sub-observations (closes the P-45 screen-recording arc end-to-end)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-22-i_p45-build-2-deploy-with-phase-1-fix-forward` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` then ff-merged to `main` then ping-ponged back).

**Type:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closes the P-45 screen-recording arc with empirical fix narrative for all 3 Phase-1 issues + 3 reusable Patterns memorialized for future sessions. Embeds 2 sub-observations about P-43-bug-class reproductions at Claude's inline-typed Bash layer — both LOW informational, both caught + recovered within seconds.

**P-45 ROADMAP entry:** flipped to ✅ **DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-22-i** this session.

**Build commit:** `ee8c79d` — `W#2 polish P-45 Build #2 — Phase 1 fix-forward (Issues 1+2+3) — 2026-05-22` — 3 files changed +78/-30.

**Deploy mechanics:** ff-merge `d4a2940..ee8c79d` brought 11 commits to `main` as a single fast-forward (Build #1a + 1a-doc + Build #1b + 1b-doc + P-42 + P-42-doc + P-43 + P-43-doc + P-44 + P-44-doc + today's Build #2 fix-forward). 39 files changed +5067/-200 against main. Rule 9 deploy gate fired once for `git push origin main` via AskUserQuestion picker; director Yes approved. Vercel auto-redeploy fired post-push. Ping-pong sync brought `workflow-2-competition-scraping` back even with `main` at `ee8c79d`. Fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-22-w2-deploy-33.zip` (202.75 KB) at repo root via `npm run zip` in 2.2s — exits cleanly thanks to P-44's wxt-zip.mjs wrapper.

**Schema-change-in-flight flag:** FLIPPED **YES → NO** at deploy completion. SCREEN_RECORDING enum value has been live on Supabase since 2026-05-22-d 14:09 UTC; production routes on vklf.com now read + accept it as of this deploy.

### Phase 1 dev-time verify on Amazon surfaced THREE FIXABLE ISSUES + ONE STRUCTURAL CEILING — empirical narrative

**Issue 1 — Chrome Tab tab missing in `getDisplayMedia` share dialog (1-line production-wrapper fix).** When the director clicked "Record video for PLOS" + drew a rectangle on Amazon, Chrome's "Choose what to share" dialog opened — but the **Chrome Tab** option for the current tab was missing entirely; only Window + Entire Screen tabs showed. **Root cause:** `getDisplayMedia` accepts a `selfBrowserSurface` option that defaults to `'exclude'` on Chrome — which hides the requesting tab from the share dialog so users don't accidentally share the page they're recording from. For our use case the requesting tab IS what the user wants to share (we're capturing the video element on the same tab the recording was triggered from), so the default behavior actively prevents the happy path. **Fix shape:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/screen-recording/record-controller.ts createProductionDeps` wrapper now sets `selfBrowserSurface: 'include'` when invoking `navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia()`. 1-line change in the production wrapper only — DI deps untouched so existing 29 + 5 = 34 unit tests on record-controller continue passing (tests inject a fake `getDisplayMedia` so the wrapper's option doesn't reach them).

**Issue 2 — All four text inputs dead on Amazon (P-27 Bug #11 carry-over; aggressive event-isolation band-aid).** After Issue 1 was fixed + the recording flow completed + the form opened with the recording attached, ALL FOUR text input fields on the form (Add new category / Composition / Embedded text / Tags) refused to accept keyboard input on Amazon. This is the same Bug #11 class P-27 Build #8 attempted to address with a keystroke-only defense on newCategoryInput alone — but Build #8's defense was insufficient because **the bug fires at the FOCUS layer, not the keystroke layer**: Amazon's page-level handlers steal focus before any keystroke can reach the input, so the keystroke-only defense was operating on inputs that never had focus to begin with. **Fix shape:** `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/video-capture-form.ts` now applies an aggressive **20-event stopPropagation band-aid** to all four text inputs — every event in {mousedown, mouseup, click, dblclick, pointerdown, pointerup, keydown, keyup, keypress, input, change, paste, copy, cut, compositionstart, compositionupdate, compositionend, focus, focusin, focusout, blur} gets a `stopPropagation()` listener registered on each input (4 inputs × 20 events = 80 listeners total). Plus a click-handler force-focus loop with `queueMicrotask` retry so when the user clicks into an input + Amazon's page-level handler tries to steal focus on the next microtask, the force-focus runs after and wins. **Empirically verified working on Amazon during Phase 4** — director typed metadata into all 4 form fields successfully on the Amazon platform that was P-27 Bug #11's canonical reproduction.

**Issue 3 — `recording-bytes-upload.ts` PUT 400 from Supabase (1-line Content-Type normalization).** After the form filled successfully and director clicked Save, the smart-client orchestrator's Phase 2 PUT to Supabase's signed-URL bucket returned 400. **Root cause:** `recording-bytes-upload.ts` line 115 was using `input.blob.type || mimeType` for the PUT `Content-Type` header — which serves the Blob's full MediaRecorder MIME including codec params (`video/webm;codecs=vp9,opus`). Supabase Storage validates Content-Type strict-equal against the bucket's `allowedMimeTypes` list (`['video/mp4', 'video/webm', 'video/quicktime']`); codec-laden MIME doesn't strict-equal any of those entries → 400 reject. **Fix shape:** changed line 115 to use the already-normalized `mimeType` variable (the base type stripped of codec params via the `normalizeBlobMime` helper that Build #1b added). The encoded codec is still preserved in the bytes themselves — browsers + servers infer codec from container metadata when playing back; stripping codec from the Content-Type header doesn't affect playability.

**Issue 4 — Structural-ceiling finalize 400 (NOT a fix-forward target this session; deferred to Phase 4 post-deploy).** When director clicked Save after the recording bytes uploaded successfully, the FINALIZE call to vklf.com returned `'Couldn't save (400): Request body must include clientId + sourceType (EMBED|DIRECT_BYTES) + originalSrcUrl; DIRECT_BYTES additionally requires capturedVideoId + videoStoragePath'`. **Root cause:** production vklf.com routes didn't have the Build #1a `isVideoSourceType('SCREEN_RECORDING') === true` validator change yet — Build #1a's schema migration shipped the enum value to Supabase 2026-05-22-d, and Build #1a's API route + validator broadening shipped to `workflow-2-competition-scraping` the same session, but the code was undeployed on `main` until today. The error message proves the LIVE production validator was still rejecting `SCREEN_RECORDING` as an unknown sourceType. **This is the schema-change-in-flight state playing out at the finalize layer** — Phase 1 has a structural ceiling because it tests against the LIVE production server which doesn't have the new validator. **Decision:** declare Phase 1 done up to finalize (all 3 fixable issues verified up to the finalize step), proceed to Phase 2 → Phase 3, finalize verification deferred to Phase 4 post-deploy where production code carries the new validator. The deploy decision was informed by this awareness rather than blocked by it.

### Reusable Patterns memorialized

**Pattern A — "How to escape a Supabase signed-URL bucket validation 400 via Content-Type normalization."** When a Supabase Storage bucket has `allowedMimeTypes: ['video/mp4', 'video/webm', 'video/quicktime']` (strict-equal validated), any Content-Type with codec params (e.g., `video/webm;codecs=vp9,opus` from MediaRecorder) → 400. Fix: normalize the MIME to its base type before sending. Use the normalized base MIME for the PUT Content-Type header; codec info is preserved in container metadata so playback isn't affected. Worth surfacing in future Supabase Storage integrations where any MediaRecorder / VideoCapture path produces codec-laden MIMEs.

**Pattern B — "How to dampen P-27-Bug-#11-class page-level focus-stealing via aggressive event isolation."** Keystroke-only defenses fail when the bug fires at the FOCUS layer (input never receives focus → keystroke-level defense is moot). Aggressive 20-event stopPropagation × N inputs + click-handler force-focus with `queueMicrotask` retry works as empirical band-aid. **Limitations:** 80 listeners per form is heavy DOM machinery; carries hidden risk for any future video-form feature that needs to PROPAGATE events upward (e.g., a future "click outside form to dismiss" behavior would now have to be wired with capture-phase listeners on the backdrop instead of bubble-phase on document). Proper long-term fix is Shadow DOM mount of the form's backdrop+content (Shadow DOM doesn't bubble events into the page DOM by default; isolation is at the boundary, not per-listener). Captured as P-47 polish item for the Shadow DOM refactor; LOW priority since band-aid works for current scope.

**Pattern C — "How to verify a feature whose server-side code is undeployed at Phase 1."** When the design has a chained dependency between client-side code (already shipped to a feature branch + dev-sideloadable) and server-side code (not yet deployed to production), recognize the structural limit at Phase 1: dev-time verify can only validate up to the boundary where production code is reached (in this case, the FINALIZE API call). Document the chained dependency in the design doc + the launch prompt so the next session is aware. Defer the unverifiable step to Phase 4 post-deploy where production code carries the new server-side change. The deploy decision is informed by this awareness rather than blocked by it — Phase 4 verification then explicitly validates the deferred step. Worth surfacing in any future feature where schema-change-in-flight + a new validator both ship in the same arc; the validator is the natural Phase 4 verification target rather than a Phase 1 blocker.

### Embedded informational sub-observations (LOW; NOT slips)

**Sub-observation #1 — P-43 bug class re-reproduction at Claude's inline-typed Bash layer (FIRST occurrence pre-deploy Check 5).** Pre-deploy /scoreboard Check 5 first attempt ran `npm run build` from the cwd left by the prior `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npm test` call, producing the **EXTENSION build** instead of the Next.js build. Output content was `Building chrome-mv3 for production` (extension build's signature output) and route count when grep'd = `0 routes`. **Caught immediately from output content** — `0 routes` is structurally impossible for the Next.js build which has 57 routes — and recovered by re-running with absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build`. LOW informational. Reinforces the P-43 + P-44 closing entries that Claude's inline-typed Bash shortcuts don't benefit from the `.claude/commands/*.md` template hardening; only the verbatim-template-read pathway does.

**Sub-observation #2 — P-43 bug class re-reproduction at Claude's inline-typed Bash layer (SECOND occurrence post-merge Check 5).** Post-merge /scoreboard Check 5 first attempt hit the same cwd-leak from the parallel `cd extensions/competition-scraping && npm test` call run a few seconds earlier. Output: `0` routes again. Caught immediately, re-run with absolute cd produced 57 routes. LOW informational. Two reproductions in a single deploy session suggests the inline-Bash discipline gap is still real but the recovery is fast + non-destructive (loud-fail symptoms, easy to spot). The pattern remains: **template hardening protects verbatim reads; Claude's inline-typed shortcuts remain vulnerable to cwd drift between parallel Bash calls.** Both reproductions on Check 5 specifically (the `npm run build` route count check) suggests the route-count grep is a reliable canary — `0 routes` from an extension-build cwd is structurally impossible and surfaces the slip immediately.

Embeds 3 informational sub-observations — all LOW informational, none corrections-tier.

**Build commit:** `caad82a` — `W#2 polish P-46 Workstream 1 — Schema + API route shells + shared-types — 2026-05-24` — 21 files +781/-11 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (NOT pushed to main since Workstream 1 is a build session, not a deploy session).

**Deploy mechanics:** none for production code — Workstream 1 is a build session, not a deploy session. ONE Rule 9 gate fired during the session for `npx prisma db push` (AskUserQuestion picker; director Yes approved); the schema migration ran in 1.32s with zero data loss + additive only. No `git push origin main`; no ff-merge; no Vercel auto-redeploy; no ping-pong sync since main doesn't move; no fresh extension zip since the extension isn't touched.

**Schema-change-in-flight flag:** FLIPPED **NO → YES** at `npx prisma db push` completion. New tables + columns + enum live on Supabase; production routes on vklf.com still unaware. Flag stays YES until the future deploy session ships the schema-aware code to vklf.com.

### What landed — empirical narrative

**3 new Prisma models** — `CapturedReview` (per §A.1b — fields: id / clientId / competitorUrlId / starRating Int / body Text / reviewerName String? / reviewDate DateTime? / capturedAt + analysis JSON), `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` (per §A.4 — fields: projectId unique / contentJson + naming-convention drift: lastEditedBy + lastEditedAt + createdAt rather than the schema-wide addedBy + updatedAt; implemented per §A.4 literal text per Rule 18 frozen), `UserTablePreferences` (per §A.3 — fields: userId / projectId / columnVisibility Json / columnWidths Json / fontSize Int / rowOrder Json + composite unique on (userId, projectId)).

**8 new `CompetitorUrl` columns** — `type String?` / `description1 String? @db.Text` / `description2 String? @db.Text` / `price String?` / `competitionScore Int?` (1-100 per §A.7) / `scrapingStatus ScrapingStatus @default(INCOMPLETE)` / `overallCompetitorAnalysis Json @default("{}")` (TipTap document per §A.5) / `overallAnalyses Json @default("{}")` (denormalized per-category bag per §A.11; shape `{ "text": <TipTap>, "image": <TipTap>, "video": <TipTap>, "reviews": <TipTap> }`).

**1 new `analysis` JSON column on each of CapturedText / CapturedImage / CapturedVideo** — per-item TipTap document for the per-item Analysis text box per §A.5.

**1 new Prisma enum** — `ScrapingStatus { INCOMPLETE, COMPLETE }` — drives the bidirectional mirror between the URL detail page Scraping Status toggle and the Competition Data table Status column per §A.8.

**Deliberate inconsistency-resolution on `CapturedReview.analysis`:** §A.1b's literal text said `Text?` but §A.11 consolidated list said `Json @default("{}")`. Implemented as `Json @default("{}")` matching §A.11 + matching CapturedText/Image/Video on this field per §A.5's standing "per-item Analysis is always TipTap rich-text" pattern. No director-confirmed override needed since both readings were already on the design doc; informational flag captured in `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-24.

**4 new API route shells** scaffolded as 501 Not Implemented stubs that Workstreams 2-4 will fill in: CapturedReview CRUD (`GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/projects/[projectId]/captured-reviews[/...]`) / Comprehensive Analysis read+write (`GET/PUT /api/projects/[projectId]/comprehensive-analysis`) / User Table Preferences read+write (`GET/PUT /api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]`) / per-row PATCH handlers for the new `CompetitorUrl` columns + per-item `analysis` JSON on CapturedText/Image/Video. Each route exports a typed handler that returns 501 with a body shape matching the future contract.

**Shared-types extensions** in `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.ts` — new wire shapes for `CapturedReview` / `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` / `UserTablePreferences` / `ScrapingStatus` enum + new optional fields on `CompetitorUrlWire`. Type guards/discriminators added where needed.

**12 new node:test cases** for the new shape-validation helpers — these reject misshapen data at the trust boundary so future code can rely on them. Test count 590 → 602 (+12).

### Reusable Pattern memorialized — "Workstream Foundation Build Bundle"

**When a workstream's scope is well-specced in advance via the design doc's §A + §C subsections, the schema delta + API route shells + shared-types extension + node:test coverage can bundle in a single build session as long as ALL of the following hold:**

- (a) **Schema is purely additive** — new tables empty; new columns nullable or defaulted; new enum value drives a new column with a sane default; no DROP / DELETE; no destructive migration. Director-Yes Rule 9 gate fires once for `npx prisma db push`; reversible at the schema level via rollback as long as Workstream 2+ hasn't started writing data into the new tables yet.
- (b) **API routes are 501 stubs** — each new route file exports a typed handler that returns 501 with a body shape matching the future contract; downstream workstreams will fill in the implementation. The route shells let Workstream 2+ start importing route URLs from a stable surface immediately.
- (c) **Wire-type extensions are forward-compatible** — new fields optional on request types; new fields populated on response types via mapper extensions; old clients see new fields they don't care about as `undefined`.
- (d) **The existing toWireShape mapper sites are explicitly enumerated and updated** — every place the schema-level model gets converted to wire shape gets the new fields added in this build session. Misses here are caught by Check 1 (root tsc) at /scoreboard.

**Why this matters:** The default planning estimate for a Workstream 1-style foundation in a multi-workstream feature is typically 2-3 sessions (schema delta in session 1, API + shared-types in session 2, tests + verification in session 3). When ALL four conditions above hold, the work compresses into 1 session because there's no UI to wire, no real-world walkthrough, no Rule 9 deploy gate (the schema-migration Rule 9 gate is its own thing + already fires within this session), and no schema-vs-code drift to reconcile across boundaries (the schema, the wire types, and the route stubs all land in one commit so the truth is identical across all three layers).

**Tag this "Workstream Foundation Build Bundle" as a session shape worth recognizing in future Workstream 1-style sessions** — applies to any feature where (a)+(b)+(c)+(d) hold. Future Workstream 1-style sessions can use this Pattern as a planning lens: if all four conditions can be met, plan 1 session for the foundation, not 2-3.

### Embedded informational sub-observations (LOW; NOT slips)

**Sub-observation #1 — P-43 bug class re-reproduction at Claude's inline-typed Bash layer (1×).** During /scoreboard Check 5, Claude ran `npm run build` from a cwd that had drifted to `extensions/competition-scraping/` (from prior Check 2 + Check 3 commands that explicitly cd'd there). Result: the extension's build ran instead of the Next.js build (~2-second extension build with dist artifacts at `extensions/.../.output/chrome-mv3/`). Recovered immediately with explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build`. LOW informational. Same shape as the prior reproductions captured in 2026-05-22-i + 2026-05-22-h sub-observations; reinforces that the absolute-cd discipline from P-43 protects verbatim-template-read pathways but not Claude's inline-typed shortcuts. No corrective action needed beyond the existing P-43 + P-44 + P-45 discipline notes.

**Sub-observation #2 — Workstream 1 came in under estimate (1 session vs. 2-3 planned).** §C.1 of the design doc explicitly allowed Sessions 1+2 to be combined if scope landed cleanly — "if schema delta + API route shells + shared-types extension + node:test coverage all bundle into a single session, that's fine." This session is the empirical proof that the combine-Sessions-1+2 case works for Workstream 1. Sessions 2-3 originally allocated for Workstream 1 are reabsorbed into Workstream 2's runway. NOT a corrections-tier slip — documenting as informational so future estimate calibrations note that "schema + API shells + shared-types + node:test" can bundle in one session if the schema itself is well-specced. May tighten future estimates for similar foundation workstreams.

**Sub-observation #3 — Convention disagreement noted at §A.3's API path (deferred to §B refinement candidate, not a slip).** §A.3's literal path `/api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]` differs from PLOS's general convention of auth-derived userId paths (e.g., `/api/extension-state` derives userId from session; `/api/projects/[projectId]/extension-state/highlight-terms` does the same). Implemented per §A.3 literal text per Rule 18 (§A is frozen at end-of-interview). When Workstream 3 implements the route's body (vs. this session's 501 stub), the decision is EITHER (a) enforce `auth.userId === params.userId` at the auth check so the literal-text path stays + the security model matches the rest of PLOS, OR (b) refactor the path to `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/table-preferences` matching the auth-derived userId convention. Captured inline in the route stub's header comment as a tombstone marker so Workstream 3's session reader catches it immediately. NOT a corrections-tier slip — both readings are valid; deferral to Workstream 3 is correct per the design doc's §A frozen-state.

### What landed — empirical narrative

**`src/lib/rich-text/tiptap-helpers.ts` (NEW, 92 LOC pure helpers)** — exports `EMPTY_TIPTAP_DOC` constant + `isEmptyTipTapDoc` predicate + `normalizeTipTapInput` defensive normalizer + `isValidAnalysisPayload` route-trust-boundary guard (rejects null / arrays / primitives; accepts only object-shaped TipTap documents). **Deliberately free of `@tiptap/*` imports** so `node:test` can load them without browser-only ProseMirror dependencies; this is the same isolation pattern that lets `src/lib/shared-types/*.ts` load under node:test without dragging in React.

**`src/lib/rich-text/tiptap-helpers.test.ts` (NEW)** — 20 new node:test cases covering all four helpers' happy paths + edge cases (empty doc detection / array rejection / null rejection / primitive rejection / nested-object acceptance / forward-compatibility for unknown TipTap node types). Test count 602 → 622 (+20).

**`src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/RichTextEditor.tsx` (NEW, 317 LOC)** — platform-shared TipTap editor wrapper. Minimal toolbar: Bold / Italic / bullet list / numbered list / link. Forward-compatible `variant: 'minimal' | 'full'` prop — `'minimal'` is wired this session (per-item Analysis boxes); `'full'` reserved for Workstream 4 Comprehensive Analysis page (the per-Project rich-text doc that hosts the synthesis of all platforms + competitors). Debounced on-change save (500ms default) + onBlur flush + SSR-safe via `immediatelyRender: false` (per TipTap's Next.js compatibility guidance). The wrapper itself doesn't persist — the consumer wires up the save mechanism via props.

**`src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/PerItemAnalysisBox.tsx` (NEW, 174 LOC)** — one-captured-item Analysis box that owns the per-row save lifecycle. Renders the RichTextEditor (variant='minimal') + a status indicator ("Saving…" while a save is in-flight; "✓ Saved" once it lands; "Save failed — retry?" on error). Reusable for Captured Image / Video / Review in later Workstream 2 sessions by passing a different `apiUrl` prop (per the "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" reusable Pattern memorialized below).

**`UrlDetailContent.tsx` MODIFIED** — `CapturedTextSubsection`'s render switches from a 5-column HTML table to a **vertical card list** per the Rule 14f forced-picker outcome (see below). New `CapturedTextCard` + `CapturedTextSortControl` helpers handle the card-layout rendering + the now-out-of-table sort affordance. Removed the now-unused `SortableHeader` + `textCellStyle`. **Layout precedent set for Captured Image / Video / Review** per-item Analysis in subsequent Workstream 2 sessions.

**`src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/text/[textId]/route.ts` MODIFIED** — PATCH allowlist extended to accept `analysis` field; validates via `isValidAnalysisPayload` at the trust boundary (rejects null / arrays / primitives — only object-shaped TipTap documents pass). FIRST REAL IMPLEMENTATION behind a Workstream-1-era extension surface — the wire-type's `analysis?: Record<string, unknown>` shipped in Workstream 1; this session lands the route-handler half + the UI half together so the round-trip works on the workflow-2 branch even though the deploy is later.
