### Rule 14f forced-picker outcome — visual layout of per-item Analysis on Captured Text

**Picker question:** "How should the per-item Analysis text box render on Captured Text rows in the URL detail page?"

**Options surfaced (4) with ASCII mockups previewed:**

- **Option A — Card layout (replace the table) (RECOMMENDED).** Each captured text becomes a vertical card with metadata row at top + body text below + Analysis editor below that. Most space for the rich-text editor; layout precedent that propagates cleanly to Image / Video / Review which already render gallery-style.
- **Option B — Expandable row (current table stays).** Click a chevron to expand a row into a full-width Analysis editor below; collapse to restore table view. Preserves the current 5-column table but adds a click-cost to access the editor.
- **Option C — Sixth column.** Add a sixth column with a compact Analysis preview + an edit affordance. Table stays uniform but the editor lives off-screen for most rows.
- **Option D — Inline sub-row.** A second row beneath each text row holds the Analysis editor inline. No click-cost but the table loses uniform row-height.

**Director picked Option A — Card layout (replace the table).** This choice **propagates to all 4 capture types** (Text / Image / Video / Review) in subsequent Workstream 2 sessions per §C.2. The remaining 3 capture types already render in gallery/list shapes that adapt naturally to a card-list layout — Captured Image / Captured Video each render as image/video tiles today; subsequent sessions add the Analysis editor below each tile in card-list shape. Captured Reviews (Workstream 2 Session 3 or 4) is greenfield and slots into the same card-list shape from day one.

**Skipped pickers (per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`):** wrapper file location (§A.15 of the design doc already locks W#2-local at `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/RichTextEditor.tsx` — no picker needed); wrapper API shape (props: initial JSON / on-change callback / read-only mode / placeholder / debounce-ms / variant — most-thorough/canonical TipTap wrapper API obvious; skipped per Default-to-recommendation); save mechanism (debounced on-change 500ms + onBlur flush is the canonical Notion / Google-Docs rich-text-editor UX shape; skipped per Default-to-recommendation since the recommendation is the obvious answer).

### Reusable Pattern memorialized — "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction"

**When a per-row edit affordance (rich-text editor / structured form / autosave field) is needed across multiple sibling capture types in a feature, extract a single component that owns the per-row save lifecycle and parameterize per-type behavior via a small props surface.**

The PerItemAnalysisBox extracts the per-row save lifecycle (debounced on-change persist + onBlur flush + status indicator + retry-on-error) into a single component. Each captured item type (Text / Image / Video / Review) consumes the SAME component but passes a different `apiUrl` prop — `/api/.../text/[textId]` for Captured Text today; `/api/.../images/[imageId]` for Captured Image next session; etc. The component itself doesn't know what capture type it's hosting; it just owns the save-lifecycle for the JSON column at the API path the caller provides.

**Why this matters:**

- (a) **One save-lifecycle to maintain.** The "Saving…" / "✓ Saved" / "Save failed" status logic + the debounce + the onBlur flush + the abort-on-unmount semantics all live in ONE component. Future bugs get fixed once; future improvements (e.g., optimistic UI) land once.
- (b) **Layout precedent propagates cleanly.** Because the same component renders on Text / Image / Video / Review, the visual layout (card layout per the Rule 14f picker outcome) propagates by composition rather than copy-paste — Sessions 2-4 of Workstream 2 don't need to re-decide layout for each capture type.
- (c) **API contract uniformity enforced by props.** The `apiUrl` prop has the same shape across capture types — PATCH with `{ analysis: TipTapDoc }` body. The route-handler half (which Workstream 1 stubbed as 501) gets the same one-line extension on each capture type (allowlist `analysis` + validate via `isValidAnalysisPayload`). One pattern, four routes.
- (d) **Trust-boundary guard in shared helpers.** `isValidAnalysisPayload` in `src/lib/rich-text/tiptap-helpers.ts` validates the wire shape at the route trust boundary — rejecting null / arrays / primitives before the data ever reaches Prisma. The same guard runs in all 4 capture-type PATCH routes; future capture types (e.g., the Captured Audio entry on the Workflow #14 roadmap) can adopt the same guard for free.

**Tag this "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" as a reusable Pattern worth recognizing in future per-row-edit-affordance work.** Applies wherever (a)+(b)+(c)+(d) hold across sibling row types. Future workstreams adding click-to-edit on a per-row field across multiple sibling row types can use this Pattern as a planning lens.

### Embedded informational sub-observation (LOW; NOT a slip)

**Sub-observation #1 — Workstream 2 Session 1 estimate-calibration data point.** §C.2 of the design doc estimates Workstream 2 at 3-5 sessions; Session 1's "first slice" framing was "build the shared wrapper + wire per-item Analysis on the simplest capture type (Captured Text)" and that landed cleanly within scope. Build commit `b6e43fe` shipped exactly the planned scope (wrapper component + per-item Analysis box component + Captured Text card-list rewrite + text/[textId] PATCH allowlist extension + 20 new node:test cases). No scope overrun; no fix-forward; no follow-up Workstream-2-Session-1b session needed. Documenting as informational so future estimate calibrations note that a "shared wrapper + first-capture-type wiring + first-route-handler-half" Workstream 2-style session can complete cleanly in 1 session if the wrapper API shape is decided at the start of the session (Default-to-recommendation skipped the API picker) and the layout choice resolves via a single Rule 14f forced-picker.

## §Entry 2026-05-28 — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 2 SESSION 4 CLOSING ENTRY — Workstream 1 path-divergence finding + architectural-move resolution + new reusable Pattern "When a foundation workstream scaffolds a 501-stub at a divergent path, surface the divergence at next-workstream session start + execute the path move within-session"

**Session:** `session_2026-05-28_p46-workstream-2-session-4-captured-reviews-ui` (Claude Code, on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`).

**Type:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closes Session 4 of Workstream 2 of the P-46 implementation arc at code level. Captures (a) the Workstream 1 path-divergence finding (W1's nested `urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts` 501-stub diverged from the shallow precedent the other 3 capture types' per-record routes set — `text/[textId]` / `images/[imageId]` / `videos/[videoId]`); (b) the architectural-move resolution executed within this session (delete the W1 nested stub; ship the new shallow per-record route at `reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts`; collection route stays at the deeper `urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts` matching collection-route precedent); (c) the new reusable Pattern memorialized: "when a foundation workstream scaffolds a 501-stub at a path that diverges from the precedent shape of the sibling capture types, surface the divergence at the next-workstream session start + execute the path move + commit + announce in the build commit; treat W1 path divergences as informational findings to fix not blockers."

**P-46 ROADMAP entry:** annotated this session with Workstream 2 Session 4 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-28 in the Workstream sub-status grid + cross-reference to new design doc §B 2026-05-28 entry. Workstream 2 sub-status updated from "Session 3 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL; Session 4 NEXT" to "Sessions 1-4 of 3-5 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL; Session 5 NEXT per (a.75)".

**Build commit:** `82d390a` — `W#2 polish P-46 Workstream 2 Session 4 — Captured Reviews UI + handler DI seams + path-divergence fix — 2026-05-28` — 9 files +2350/-69 on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (NOT pushed to main since Workstream 2 Session 4 is a build session, not a deploy session).

**Deploy mechanics:** none for production code — Workstream 2 Session 4 is a build session, not a deploy session. ZERO Rule 9 gates fired this session — no schema changes (Workstream 1 already covered all of P-46's schema including the `CapturedReview` Prisma model); no `git push origin main`; no `git reset --hard`; no force-push; no `rm -rf`; no SQL DELETE/DROP/TRUNCATE. The file deletion of W1's nested per-record stub is a path-relocation within a feature branch + replaced by the new shallow route, NOT a Rule 9 destructive op since no production data is touched and no main branch is impacted (the W1 nested stub never had any production traffic; it was a 501 placeholder waiting to be replaced). No fresh extension zip since the extension isn't touched.

**Schema-change-in-flight flag:** STAYS **YES** the entire session (carrying from Workstream 1's 2026-05-24 `prisma db push`); stays YES through subsequent W#2 / W#3 sessions until the future deploy session ships the schema-aware code to vklf.com — the `CapturedReview` Prisma model + the `overallAnalyses.reviews` JSON slot are both already deployed at schema level via Workstream 1; this session lands the route-handler + UI halves together so the round-trip works on workflow-2 even though the deploy is later.

### What landed — empirical narrative

**NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/url-reviews.ts` (~295 LOC)** — DI seam for the captured-reviews-under-URL collection handlers (POST + GET) following the `url-text.ts` P-31 pattern. Includes `CapturedReviewRow` type + `UrlReviewsPrismaLike` minimal Prisma surface + `UrlReviewsHandlerDeps` factory deps; `toWireShape` coercing Date → ISO string and Json → string[] / Record; GET handler with 401 / 404 / 500 happy-path and `orderBy [{ addedAt: 'asc' }]`; POST handler validating `clientId` (non-empty string) + `starRating` (integer in [1, 5]) + `body` (non-empty string) + `tags` (string array) + `reviewerName` (string|null) + `reviewDate` (ISO date string|null|empty→null) + `analysis` (trust-boundary via `isValidAnalysisPayload`) + `source` (defaults to 'manual'); 404 on parent-URL-not-found; 201 happy with default source='manual'; 200 idempotent on Prisma P2002 unique-constraint violation (looks up existing row by `clientId` and returns it); 500 on unhandled errors with `recordFlake`.

**NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/url-reviews.test.ts` (~408 LOC)** — 17 new node:test cases mirroring the `url-text.test.ts` shape: POST 401 / 400 invalid JSON / 400 clientId missing / 400 starRating missing / 400 starRating out of range / 400 starRating non-integer / 400 body empty / 400 tags wrong / 400 reviewDate unparseable / 400 analysis null / 404 parent not found / 201 happy / 200 idempotent P2002 / 500 unhandled + GET 401 / 404 / 200 with orderBy contract.

**NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/reviews-by-id.ts` (~290 LOC)** — DI seam for the per-record captured-review PATCH + DELETE handlers. **Sets a NEW PRECEDENT** — the text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId] per-record routes were direct-shape (no DI seam); this session extracts per-record handlers to a DI seam because the analysis-field trust boundary + the per-field allowlist warrant unit coverage at the handler layer. Allowlisted PATCH fields: `starRating` (integer 1-5) / `body` (non-empty string) / `reviewerName` (string|null) / `reviewDate` (ISO date string|null) / `tags` (string[]) / `analysis` (trust-boundary via `isValidAnalysisPayload`). Ownership check via `findFirst` with the relation filter `competitorUrl: { projectWorkflowId }`. P2025 errors map to 404 (PATCH) or 200 idempotent (DELETE).

**NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/reviews-by-id.test.ts` (~350 LOC)** — 15 new node:test cases: PATCH 401 / 400 invalid JSON / 404 not-found / 400 starRating out-of-range / 400 body empty / 400 analysis array / 400 tags wrong / 400 reviewDate unparseable / 200 happy / 200 analysis-only / 404 P2025 + DELETE 401 / 200 not-found-idempotent / 200 happy / 200 P2025-idempotent.

**MODIFIED `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts` (~82 LOC thin shim; was W1's 501-stub)** — replaces 501-stub with thin shim adapting `makeUrlReviewsHandlers` to NextRequest/NextResponse + CORS, mirroring the text route shim exactly.

**NEW `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts` (~80 LOC thin shim)** — per-record PATCH + DELETE thin shim at the SHALLOW PRECEDENT PATH (text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId] live at the shallow path; W1 scaffolded the per-record review route at the deeper `urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]` shape; this session moves it to the shallow shape so all 4 capture types have uniform per-record path shape).

**DELETED `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]/route.ts`** — W1's nested-path 501-stub. Removed because review IDs are globally unique so the deeper path adds no security and the shallower path keeps the per-record PATCH/DELETE surface uniform across all 4 capture types. The collection route at `urls/[urlId]/reviews/route.ts` keeps the deeper path because parent-URL ownership lookup naturally lives there (matching `urls/[urlId]/text/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/images/route.ts` + `urls/[urlId]/videos/route.ts` precedent).

**NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/CapturedReviewAddModal.tsx` (~479 LOC)** — manual-add modal mirroring `CapturedTextAddModal.tsx` structure but with a 1-5 star-rating picker widget (5 buttons rendered as ☆ / ★ with click-to-select + "Pick 1–5" / "N of 5" inline label) + body textarea + reviewer-name text input + native HTML date input + tags input (comma-separated → string[]). Idempotent on clientId via `crypto.randomUUID()`. Save button disabled until both `starRating` AND `body` are populated.

**MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/url/[urlId]/components/UrlDetailContent.tsx` (+398 LOC; from ~1851 to ~2249 lines)** — threads `CapturedReview` + `ListCapturedReviewsResponse` types through the imports; imports `CapturedReviewAddModal` component; adds `reviewsSlot` state + reviews fetch to the parallel-fetches block (now 6 parallel reads); adds `handleReviewAdded` + `handleReviewDeleted` callbacks (optimistic-remove + rollback shape mirroring text + image); renders `<CapturedReviewsSection>` between `<CapturedVideosGallery>` and the page-bottom `<OverallAnalysisBox>` for `overallCompetitorAnalysis`; NEW `CapturedReviewsSection` function (parent section with sort control + card list + manual-add modal + delete-confirm dialog + Overall Reviews Analysis box at bottom); NEW `CapturedReviewCard` function (per-card render: star-rating display via `StarRatingDisplay` helper + reviewer name + review date + body + trash + tags + addedAt + `PerItemAnalysisBox` below each card via `apiUrl` pointing at `competition-scraping/reviews/[reviewId]` — the new shallow per-record path); NEW `StarRatingDisplay` helper (read-only stars rendered as ★ filled / ☆ unfilled); NEW `CapturedReviewSortControl` function (sort by `addedAt` default or `starRating`; direction asc/desc).

### Rule 14f forced-picker outcome — Session 4 scope-pick

**Picker question:** "Which §C.2-aligned Session 4 scope should land first?"

**Options surfaced (2) with rationale shape captured in §B 2026-05-27 of the design doc:**

- **Option A — Captured Reviews UI (RECOMMENDED).** New `CapturedReviewCard` matching Sessions 1-3's card-list shape + manual-add modal + flesh out the `CapturedReview` CRUD route handlers Workstream 1 scaffolded as 501 stubs. Greenfield card type slots into the same card-list precedent. Recommended because §C.2 originally sequenced this as Session 3 of Workstream 2 (natural next §C.2 step after the Analysis surface arc completion) + Reviews is the only capture type that doesn't yet have UI; landing this fills the last greenfield gap before structural fields.
- **Option B — New URL-level structural fields (ALT).** Type / Description-1 / Description-2 / Price + Scraping Status toggle + remove Sizes/Options UI. Smaller individual changes but touches more existing surfaces. Deferred to Session 5.

**Director picked Option A.** Reviews lands as the last greenfield capture type; structural fields move to Session 5 as the last §C.2 sub-scope remaining for Workstream 2.

**Skipped sub-pickers (per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`):** the architectural move from W1's nested per-record path to the shallow precedent path was a clear "most thorough/reliable" choice per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (the divergence was a W1 oversight that breaks the precedent set by the other 3 capture types; resolving it within-session at zero migration cost was the obvious right call); star-rating widget design was a direct application of the modal precedent (5 buttons rendered as ☆ / ★ is the canonical web star-rating shape; no picker needed); sort-key set was a direct application of the Captured Text sort precedent (`addedAt` default + a secondary key in the type-natural direction — `starRating` here; same shape as Captured Text's `addedAt` + `clientId` secondary).

### Reusable Pattern memorialized — "When a foundation workstream scaffolds a 501-stub at a divergent path, surface the divergence at next-workstream session start + execute the path move within-session"

**The Pattern:** When a foundation workstream (W1 in this case) scaffolds a 501-stub at a path that diverges from the precedent shape of the sibling capture types' equivalent routes, the next-workstream session that consumes the stub should:

1. **Surface the divergence at session start.** At the pre-build read step, when reading the existing 501-stub shape, notice if the path-shape diverges from sibling capture types' equivalent routes. If it diverges, that's a finding worth fixing.
2. **Execute the path move within-session.** Delete the divergent 501-stub; create the new route at the precedent path. The 501-stub had no production traffic + no consumer (it was the stub waiting to be implemented), so the move is a zero-migration-cost rename. No Rule 9 destructive-op gate fires since the deletion is of a never-used 501 placeholder on a feature branch + the new shallow route replaces it.
3. **Commit + announce in the build commit.** Explicitly call out the path-relocation in the build-commit message so future readers see the architectural rationale at the commit level (e.g., "+ an architectural-move fix relocating the per-record review route from W1's deeper `urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]` path to the shallow `reviews/[reviewId]` path matching the text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId] precedent for the other 3 capture types").
4. **Treat W1 path divergences as informational findings to fix not blockers.** The W1 oversight is informational — W1's job was to land the schema + scaffold the stubs as fast as possible to unblock W2-W4; some divergences are inevitable and the right resolution time is at the next-workstream session that consumes the stub. Don't surface this as a CORRECTIONS_LOG slip-of-W1; surface it as an informational finding at W2's session start + resolve it within-session.

**Why this Pattern matters:**

- (a) **Per-record surface uniformity across capture types is high-value.** When Sessions 5+ extend the per-record allowlist on Reviews (e.g., adding a new field), the path-shape uniformity means the same one-line fix-shape that lands on text/[textId] / images/[imageId] / videos/[videoId] also lands on reviews/[reviewId] — no path-shape exception to remember.
- (b) **Zero-migration-cost moves are easy to land in flight.** Because the 501-stub had no consumer + no production traffic, the move is a single commit (1 file delete + 1 file add at the new path); the new path's route ships with its own DI seam + test coverage so the move is also a build-quality upgrade.
- (c) **W1's role is foundation-laying; W2-W4's role is fleshing out.** The Pattern explicitly draws the line: W1 scaffolds stubs (any path-shape errors are informational); W2-W4's first session consuming each stub is the right place to resolve any path-shape errors. This keeps W1 fast (no over-design) without compromising per-record surface uniformity at the end of the arc.
- (d) **Pairs with the design doc §B 2026-05-28 reusable Pattern "Per-record handler DI-seam precedent extension".** Together they cover both the surface-path uniformity (this §Entry's Pattern) + the handler-extraction shape (the design doc §B Pattern). Future workstreams that consume 501-stubs from a foundation workstream can apply both Patterns together at the consuming-session level.

**Tag this Pattern as a reusable lens for future Workstream-N-Session-1-style sessions consuming foundation 501-stubs.** Applies wherever (a)+(b)+(c)+(d) hold.

## §Entry 2026-05-23-b — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 2 SESSION 5 CLOSING ENTRY — Workstream 2 implementation arc COMPLETE at code level + new reusable Pattern "Field-allowlist subset extraction" + date-stamping anomaly informational observation + embedded P-43 cwd-leak re-reproduction

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL (not a slip; closing entry for the fifth + final build session of Workstream 2)
**Schema-change-in-flight flag:** STAYS YES (carrying from Workstream 1's 2026-05-24 `prisma db push`; stays YES through the future Workstream 2 deploy session — flips back to NO at that deploy completion)
**Rule 9 gates fired this session:** ZERO

**What landed:**

Workstream 2's last §C.2 sub-scope shipped end-to-end. The URL detail page gains a row of URL-level structural fields at the top of the URL box (Type / Description-1 / Description-2 / Price — 4 new free-text inputs; Type + Price as compact grid cells; the two Descriptions as full-width multiline rows below the grid with `rows=3` each since they are `db.Text` columns), a Scraping Status toggle as a prominent full-width strip above the field grid (Incomplete / Complete enum via the NEW `EditableEnumField<T extends string>` segmented-control component — single-click optimistic write with error rollback; aria-correct role=radiogroup/radio/aria-checked), and the Sizes/Options UI is gone (full delete per the project rule "If you are certain that something is unused, you can delete it completely"; underlying `CompetitorSize` table data stays in the database per Q6 hide-UI-keep-data; Git history preserves reversibility). The Competition Data table gains a read-only Status pill column (green Complete / gray Incomplete) second-from-left after URL bidirectionally mirroring the URL detail page toggle per Q8 §A.8 — both views read/write the same `CompetitorUrl.scrapingStatus` enum so the mirror lives at the column level rather than at the route level.

The route-handler extension uses a NEW pure trust-boundary helper `extractUrlStructuralFieldsPatch(body)` returning `{ ok: true, patch } | { ok: false, error }` (trim-or-null normalization for the 4 text fields; strict enum-acceptance for `scrapingStatus` via `isScrapingStatus`) extracted from the route into `src/lib/competition-scraping/url-structural-fields-validation.ts` so node:test exercises production code path without Next.js/Prisma. 22 new node:test cases in `url-structural-fields-validation.test.ts` cover empty/null/non-object bodies, trim-or-null normalization per field with non-string coercion + explicit-null pass-through, each `scrapingStatus` enum branch, all-5-set, unknown-key-ignored, omitted-vs-explicit-null discrimination.

The `EditableField.tsx` rewrite is the larger UI delta: `EditableTextField` gains optional `multiline` + `rows` props rendering `<textarea>` with Enter-to-save suppressed via stopPropagation so Enter inserts a newline (Esc still bubbles to the shell to cancel); NEW `FieldShell.readValueStyle` prop for `whiteSpace: pre-wrap + wordBreak: break-word` in multiline read mode; NEW generic `EditableEnumField<T extends string>` segmented-control component over a fixed option set with single-click optimistic write + error rollback — reusable by Workstream 3 for click-to-edit enum cells; NEW style constants `textareaStyle` + `multilineReadValueStyle`. `UrlDetailContent.tsx` removes the Sizes/Options imports + state + render call + function definition + now-unused helpers (`tableStyle` / `thStyle` / `cellStyle` / `formatMoney` were only used by `SizesSubsection` — confirmed via grep before deletion); the parallel-reads block drops from 6 fetches to 5 with a comment annotating the §A.6 hide-UI-keep-data reasoning. `UrlTable.tsx` adds `scrapingStatus` to SortKey union + COLUMNS array with `label: 'Status'` + `filterKey: null` (per-column filtering for Status defers to Workstream 3 with the rest of the table redesign) + matching `<td>` cell with color-coded pill via new `scrapingStatusBadgeStyle(status)` helper.

**NEW reusable Pattern — "Field-allowlist subset extraction":** **When a small subset of PATCH-allowlist fields deserves trust-boundary unit coverage without needing a whole handler DI seam, extract a pure `extractXFieldsPatch(body)` helper that returns an `{ ok, patch } | { ok, error }` discriminator the route spreads onto its Prisma update payload.** Smaller granularity than Session 4's "Per-record handler DI-seam precedent extension" Pattern — covers the spectrum from "whole handler DI seam" (Session 4) to "field-subset pure helper" (this session) to "single-field type-guard" (existing `isScrapingStatus` / `isPlatform`). The benefit: production code is exercised by node:test (the helper is the production path) without paying the boilerplate of a whole DI-seam handler. Cost: a small extra file + a small extra import in the route. Worth it when the field subset has non-trivial normalization or enum acceptance logic that warrants direct test coverage; not worth it when the field is a single primitive validated by an existing type-guard. Pairs with §B 2026-05-25's "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" Pattern + §B 2026-05-27's "OverallAnalysisBox extraction" Pattern + §B 2026-05-28's "Per-record handler DI-seam precedent extension" Pattern — together the four Patterns give Workstream 2 a memorialized extraction-shape vocabulary spanning UI component reuse via `apiUrl` prop / UI parallel component with discriminator prop / handler DI-seam extraction / field-subset pure helper.

**Workstream 2 implementation arc COMPLETE at code level:** Sessions 1-5 of 3-5 estimated all DONE. Workstream 2 came in at the top end of the 3-5 sessions range but no overrun. The four memorialized extraction-shape Patterns across Sessions 1/3/4/5 give the project a small but useful planning lens for future workstreams + future capture types. The next session can be the **Workstream 2 deploy session** (Phase-4 deploy under Rule 9 gate; ff-merge to main carrying Workstream 1's schema commits + Sessions 1-5's UI/route commits as one fast-forward; Vercel auto-redeploy; ping-pong sync; director real-Chrome cross-platform verify of all 5 surfaces; schema-change-in-flight flag flips YES → NO at deploy completion).

Future sessions should continue to ask director at session start when in doubt rather than relying on `date` command output (which reflects the Codespace's system clock, not necessarily director's wall clock).

## §Entry 2026-05-23-c — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 2 DEPLOY CLOSING ENTRY — Workstream 2 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED end-to-end on vklf.com via ff-merge `783abf4..9969427` + Phase-4 6/6 surfaces PASS + new reusable Pattern "Multi-session workstream deploy gate timing" + two LOW informational sub-observations (P-43 cwd-leak double-reproduction + pointer-file off-by-one on main-tip SHA) + calibration data point

**Status:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closing entry for the P-46 Workstream 2 deploy session that ships Workstream 1's schema-aware production code + Sessions 1-5's UI/route handlers to vklf.com end-to-end.

**Headline:** P-46 Workstream 2 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED end-to-end via today's deploy + Phase-4 6/6 surfaces PASS on vklf.com.

**What landed this session (pure orchestration; zero new code):** A canonical 4-phase deploy on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` carrying the full Workstream 1 + 2 implementation arc as one fast-forward.

- **Phase 1 — Pre-deploy /scoreboard:** All 5 checks GREEN at exact Session 5 baselines — root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / 558 ext UNCHANGED / 692 src/lib UNCHANGED / 61 routes UNCHANGED. Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED via Rule 27 picker — director picked SKIP (recommended; the lone `extensions/` file in the bundle is `captured-text-validation.test.ts` +11 lines test-only, doesn't ship in the `.crx` runtime).
- **Phase 2 — Rule 9 director-Yes gate:** AskUserQuestion picker fired ONCE for `git push origin main`; director picked "Deploy now (recommended)".
- **Phase 3 — ff-merge + push + Vercel auto-redeploy + ping-pong sync:** `git checkout main && git merge --ff-only workflow-2-competition-scraping` succeeded clean; ff-merge range `783abf4..9969427` — 49 files +7504/-477 — 13 commits ff'd as one fast-forward (`d364063` design-session doc-batch + `caad82a` Workstream 1 schema build + `fb19314` W1 doc-batch + `b6e43fe` W2 Session 1 build + `9f555d0` S1 doc-batch + `9747f63` S2 build + `070a7ee` S2 doc-batch + `4773b62` S3 build + `64084ae` S3 doc-batch + `82d390a` S4 build + `a8aa37b` S4 doc-batch + `374f1a3` S5 build + `9969427` S5 doc-batch). Post-merge /scoreboard all 5 checks GREEN on main (tree-identity preserved through ff; Playwright SKIPPED per ff-merge-is-pointer-move convention). `git push origin main 783abf4..9969427` succeeded — Vercel auto-redeploy fired. Ping-pong sync: `git checkout workflow-2-competition-scraping && git merge --ff-only main` was a NO-OP (workflow-2 was already at `9969427` from the prior session's doc-batch push); `git push origin workflow-2-competition-scraping` returned "Everything up-to-date".
- **Phase 4 — Director real-Chrome cross-platform verify on vklf.com:** ALL 6 SURFACES PASS clean with zero caveats — (1) Captured Text section vertical card list + per-item Analysis editor PASS (Sessions 1 + 5 combined); (2) Captured Image section same shape PASS (Session 2); (3) Captured Video section same shape PASS (Session 2); (4) Captured Reviews section with manual-add modal + 1-5 star picker + per-item Analysis + Overall Reviews Analysis PASS (Session 4); (5) URL-level affordances 4 new structural text fields Type+Description-1+Description-2+Price + Scraping Status toggle + Overall Competitor Analysis box + Sizes/Options UI gone PASS (Session 5 + Session 3); (6) Competition Data table Status pill column bidirectional mirror PASS (Session 5). Director reported "all surfaces pass" with zero caveats — the cleanest end-of-workstream verification in any P-46 session.

**Closes Workstream 2 + Workstream 1's schema in production code:** Workstream 1's schema (3 new tables `CapturedReview` / `ComprehensiveCompetitorAnalysis` / `UserTablePreferences` + 8 new `CompetitorUrl` columns + 1 new `analysis` JSON column on each of CapturedText/Image/Video + 1 new `ScrapingStatus` enum) shipped via `npx prisma db push` 2026-05-24 + Sessions 1-5's UI/route handlers reading + writing those columns shipped today via the ff-merge + push. Schema-change-in-flight flag FLIPPED YES → NO at deploy completion.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Multi-session workstream deploy gate timing":** When a workstream spans multiple build sessions (here: 1 schema + 5 UI sessions = 6 build sessions across 6 distinct calendar/session-letter dates), the deploy session should land AFTER the LAST build session that contains user-visible UI, not after the schema session. Three reasons:

- (a) Deploying after the schema session alone gives no user-visible value but locks the schema-change-in-flight flag YES across the entire build arc (preventing flag-clear status at every intermediate build session's end-of-session). The flag stays YES the whole multi-session arc anyway under this Pattern (one transition NO→YES at the schema build + one transition YES→NO at the end-of-workstream deploy), but that's the minimal flag-time footprint.
- (b) Deploying after each build session multiplies the deploy-orchestration overhead 6x. Each deploy is ~2 hours (pre-deploy /scoreboard + Rule 9 gate + ff-merge + push + Vercel wait + ping-pong + Phase-4 verify + doc-batch). Six deploys would be ~12 hours of pure orchestration vs. ~2 hours under this Pattern.
- (c) The END-TO-END user flow is verifiable in one Phase-4 walkthrough at the END of the build arc when ALL UI is in place. Verifying after the schema build alone gives no walkthrough (the schema is server-side; nothing to walk through). Verifying after each UI build session would walk through partial UI (Session 2's Captured Image card-list works but the Captured Reviews UI Session 4 ships doesn't exist yet). The end-of-arc Phase-4 verifies the COMPLETE user flow across all 6 surfaces in one continuous walkthrough.

This Pattern pairs with the 2026-05-24 "Workstream Foundation Build Bundle" Pattern (schema + API shells + shared-types in one foundation build session) to give the full multi-session workstream deploy-cadence shape: **ONE foundation build + N UI builds + ONE end-of-workstream deploy.** Reusable by Workstreams 3-5 of P-46 + future multi-session workstreams (W#3-W#14 each likely follow this same cadence if they're well-specced via a frozen §A interview + §C per-workstream outlines like P-46 has).

**Informational sub-observation A — P-43 cwd-leak class re-reproduced TWICE this session:** Same pattern as the 2026-05-22-i precedent. Both pre-deploy /scoreboard Check 5 + post-merge /scoreboard Check 5 ran the EXTENSION build instead of the Next.js root build because the parallel Checks 2+3 cd'd to `extensions/competition-scraping/` legitimately, and Claude's inline-typed Check 5 didn't have the `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub &&` prefix. Caught immediately from grep output of `0` routes (Next.js prints a route table; the extension build prints `Built extension in N s` + zip size) and recovered cleanly both times with absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build`. Same LOW informational shape as the 2026-05-22-i + 2026-05-24 + 2026-05-27 reproductions. Reinforces P-43's standing observation that template hardening (the `/scoreboard` slash-command template uses absolute paths) protects verbatim-template-read pathways but NOT Claude's inline-typed Bash shortcuts. Tightens Claude's start-of-session vigilance for parallel-Bash invocations where one sibling cd's away: when Checks 2+3 cd to `extensions/...`, Check 5's inline-typed Bash MUST carry the absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub &&` prefix. NOT promoted to a separate corrections-tier §Entry — same LOW informational shape as the prior reproductions.

**Informational sub-observation B — Pointer-file off-by-one on main-tip SHA:** NEXT_SESSION.md (written by Session 5's end-of-session) said main was at `ee8c79d` (the 2026-05-22-i P-45 deploy BUILD commit) but actual main was at `783abf4` (the 2026-05-22-i DOC-BATCH which landed via the canonical 3-push pattern's post-deploy ping-pong sync — both main + workflow-2 end up at the doc-batch SHA after the prior session closes). The 13-commit ff-merge count was still correct (the doc-batches `d364063` + `fb19314` + `9f555d0` + `070a7ee` + `64084ae` + `a8aa37b` + `9969427` + the build commits = 13). LOW informational; the deploy proceeded cleanly because the ff-merge from workflow-2 to main worked regardless of which SHA main was at (workflow-2 was descended from both). Pattern reminder for pointer-file writers: when writing NEXT_SESSION.md pointer files, refer to the END SHA after the prior session's full doc-batch push (the "actual main tip"), not the build commit alone. The canonical 3-push pattern in `feedback_approval_scope_per_decision_unit.md` makes the doc-batch the LAST push of the session, so the doc-batch SHA is always the most recent SHA on both branches after a deploy session. Tightens future pointer-file writes to identify the "actual main tip" by inspecting `git log origin/main -1 --format=%H` at the end of the session, not by inferring from the deploy push SHA.

**Calibration data point — Workstream 2 came in at the top end of estimate:** 5 build sessions vs. 3-5 estimated per §C.2. The §C.2 plan was well-specced; no overrun. Combined with Workstream 1's UNDER-estimate (1 session vs. 2-3 planned per §C.1), the total Workstream 1+2 spend is 6 build sessions + 1 deploy session = 7 sessions vs. 4-8 estimated (4 = W1 under-estimate floor 2 + W2 floor 3; 8 = W1 ceil 3 + W2 ceil 5). Right on plan. Useful data point for sizing Workstream 3 (estimated 3-4 sessions per §C.3) — if §C.3 is similarly well-specced, expect 3-4 build sessions + 1 deploy session = 4-5 total. Continued aggregation across Workstreams 3-5 will refine the multi-workstream estimation discipline (a meta-observation worth tracking — the §A frozen interview + §C per-workstream outlines pattern from P-46's design session appears to produce estimates within ±1 session of actual; if W#3-W#5 hold to that, future multi-workstream polish items can use the same pattern with confidence).

**No corrections-tier slip this session.** Two LOW informational sub-observations only; no fix-shape ambiguity; no estimate slip; no process slip. The deploy session ran exactly to the canonical 4-phase /deploy template + the canonical 3-push pattern (deploy push + ping-pong + doc-batch — here the ping-pong was a NO-OP).

## §Entry 2026-05-23-d — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 3 SESSION 1 CLOSING ENTRY — Foundation-workstream path-convention drift surfaced by next-workstream session-start picker + path refactor lands within-session + foundational `UserTablePreferences` plumbing + horizontal `ColumnVisibilityBar` + `PlatformSidebar` removal + calibration data point

**Status:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closing entry for the P-46 Workstream 3 first build session that lands the foundational preferences plumbing + the horizontal column visibility bar + removes the left-side platform sidebar; first of 3-4 estimated Workstream 3 sessions per §C.3.

**Headline:** P-46 Workstream 3 Session 1 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL via build commit `d846a97` (10 files +1369/-224) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. Path-convention refactor caught at session-start + executed within-session; new reusable Pattern memorialized; calibration data point captured.

**What landed this session:**

- **NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/user-table-preferences.ts`** (~340 LOC DI-seam handler factory mirroring the `url-text.ts` / `reviews-by-id.ts` precedent). Exports three pieces:
  - **`extractTablePreferencesPatch(body)`** — strict trust-boundary validator returning `{ ok, patch } | { ok, error }` discriminator. Field allowlist for the 6 editable fields per `WriteUserTablePreferencesRequest`: `columnVisibility` (Record<string, boolean> strict — rejects non-objects, arrays, null, non-boolean values); `columnWidths` (Record<string, non-negative integer> — rejects negatives + non-integers); `fontSize` (∈ [10, 24] integer); `rowOrder` (string[]); `lastUsedSortColumn` (string | null); `lastUsedSortDirection` (∈ {'asc', 'desc', null} via existing `isTablePreferencesSortDirection` guard). Unknown top-level keys silently ignored (additive-future-safe).
  - **`toWireShape(row)`** — coerces Prisma JsonValue columns to the typed wire shapes from `shared-types/competition-scraping.ts` with bad-DB-shape fallbacks (filter-non-boolean-from-bool-map; filter-non-integer-from-width-map; null-on-bad-direction).
  - **`makeUserTablePreferencesHandlers(deps)`** — factory returning `{ get, put }` handlers consuming a `{ prisma, sleep }` deps bag (DI seam for unit testing).
- **NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/user-table-preferences.test.ts`** — 34 new node:test cases covering: empty/non-object body, valid/invalid columnVisibility (incl. array + null + non-boolean rejection), valid/invalid columnWidths (incl. negative + non-integer rejection), fontSize boundary + out-of-range, rowOrder array shape, lastUsedSortColumn null/string, lastUsedSortDirection enum, unknown-key passthrough, full payload, toWireShape coercion + bad-DB defaults + filter-non-boolean-from-bool-map, GET auth-403/404/200/500, PUT auth-403/400-invalid-json/400-bad-shape/200-partial-patch/empty-patch/500-on-throw/null-sort preservation.
- **NEW `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/table-preferences/route.ts`** (~70 LOC thin shim wiring the DI seam to `verifyProjectAuth` + `withRetry` + `recordFlake` + `corsPreflightResponse` / `withCors`). Adapts `verifyProjectAuth`'s `NextResponse` error shape to the DI seam's `HandlerResult` shape via `await auth.error.json()`.
- **DELETED `src/app/api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]/route.ts`** — W1's 501-stub at the old `/api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]` path. Replaced by the new route at the auth-derived-userId convention path.
- **NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/ColumnVisibilityBar.tsx`** (~180 LOC) — horizontal bar with two checkbox groups (Platforms + Columns) separated by a vertical divider. Platforms group: "All Platforms" + 7 platforms (Amazon / Ebay / Etsy / Walmart / Google Shopping / Google Ads / Independent Websites) preserving all 7 from the deleted sidebar; single-select semantics (toggling an active chip resets to 'all'). Columns group: 9 column toggles from `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS`. Owns no state — driven by props. Exports `isColumnVisible(map, columnId)` with missing-key-default-true semantics matching §A.3.
- **NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/url-table-columns.ts`** (~35 LOC) — canonical column registry `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` (id ↔ label for 9 columns matching UrlTable's SortKey) + `TABLE_COLUMN_IDS` Set + relocated `ScopeFilter` type from PlatformSidebar. Stable id strings double as keys in `UserTablePreferences.columnVisibility` JSON.
- **MODIFIED `CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx`** — drops the 220px grid + sidebar layout in favor of a vertical stack (bar above table). Adds: `columnVisibility` state seeded from GET `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/table-preferences` at mount (silent 404/network fallback to empty map); `handleToggleColumn` with 500ms debounced PUT via `prefsTimerRef` (the latest body wins on burst toggles); imports `ScopeFilter` from `url-table-columns` after PlatformSidebar's deletion.
- **MODIFIED `UrlTable.tsx`** — adds optional `columnVisibility` prop; computes `visibleColumns` via `useMemo` filtering `COLUMNS` with missing-key-default-true semantics; extracts cell renderers into a `Record<SortKey, (row) => ReactNode>` map so the tbody iterates `visibleColumns.map((col) => cellRenderers[col.key](row))` without duplicate visibility branches per column. Trash-button column stays unfiltered (row-action surface, not a data column).
- **MODIFIED `src/lib/shared-types/competition-scraping.ts`** — doc-comment updates on `ReadUserTablePreferencesResponse` + `WriteUserTablePreferencesRequest` to reflect the new auth-derived path. Wire shapes themselves unchanged (W1 already shipped them correctly).
- **DELETED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/PlatformSidebar.tsx`** — replaced by `ColumnVisibilityBar`.

**Rule 14f forced-pickers fired this session (two):**

1. **Session-start scope-pick** (Workstream 3 Session 1 scope) — director picked "Preferences plumbing + checkbox bar (recommended)" (§C.3 Session 1 spec) over "New columns + click-to-edit foundation (alt)" and "Combine both". Recommended path because Sessions 2-3 build click-to-edit/drag-resize on top of this plumbing; landing it first means no double-rewriting of persistence later.

2. **Path-convention refactor** (table-preferences API URL) — director picked "Refactor — match platform convention (recommended)" over "Keep current path, add ownership check". W1 explicitly flagged this in the stub's comments: *"When Workstream 3 implements this route, enforce 'auth.userId === params.userId' at the auth check OR refactor to /api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/table-preferences (auth-derived userId). Surface this as a §B refinement candidate before the implementation lands."* Refactor chosen for most-thorough/reliable per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` — removes the special-case URL shape, removes the auth.userId === params.userId double-check, matches extension-state + every other W#2 project-scoped route.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Foundation-workstream path-convention drift surfaced by next-workstream session-start picker":** When a foundation workstream (here: P-46 Workstream 1) scaffolds a route at a path that diverges from the platform's convention (here: `/api/users/[userId]/table-preferences/[projectId]` vs. the auth-derived `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/...` shape every other W#2 route uses), the foundation session SHOULD leave a clear "surface before next workstream lands" comment in the 501-stub body (which W1 did, verbatim quoted above). The next workstream's session-start SHOULD fire a Rule 14f forced-picker on the path before implementation lands — surfacing the divergence as a binary "refactor to convention" vs. "keep current path with ownership check" picker. Most-thorough/reliable per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` is virtually always the refactor (removes the special-case URL shape; removes the auth.userId === params.userId double-check; matches sibling routes for callsite uniformity), unless production traffic exists at the old path (here: zero — W1's stub returned 501; no rows in `UserTablePreferences`). The refactor cost is one file delete + one file add at the new path + one shim implementation — minimal at next-workstream time, growing-cost as workstream consumers pile on the old path.

This Pattern **pairs with the 2026-05-28 Session 4 "W1 path-divergence finding + architectural-move resolution" Pattern** (nested per-record path `urls/[urlId]/reviews/[reviewId]` vs. shallow precedent path `reviews/[reviewId]`). Together they cover both **URL-shape drift** (this Pattern — userId in path vs. project-scoped auth-derived) + **nesting-depth drift** (Session 4's Pattern — nested vs. shallow per-record). Both share the meta-shape:

- **Detect:** at the foundation-workstream → next-workstream transition.
- **Surface:** as a Rule 14f forced-picker at next-workstream session start.
- **Resolve:** within-session via a path move (delete + new + shim) + announcement in the build commit message.
- **Memorialize:** in both the operational-log layer (CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry — what + why) + the design-doc layer (§B append — pairs with §A.3 / §A.X reference).

Reusable by Workstreams 4-5 of P-46 + future multi-workstream polish items.

**Calibration data point — Workstream 3 Session 1 sized correctly:** Session 1 of 3-4 estimated Workstream 3 sessions landed cleanly within scope. The §C.3 Session 1 spec was: (a) `UserTablePreferences` read/write at the Competition Data page level; (b) horizontal checkbox bar combining platform filters + per-column show/hide controls; (c) delete the left-side PlatformSidebar. All three landed; the path-convention refactor was an in-scope bonus catch (cost: one file delete + one file add + ~70 LOC shim — small relative to the ~1369 LOC session total). Sessions 2-3 build click-to-edit/drag-resize/font-size on top. If §C.3 Sessions 2-4 are similarly well-specced, expect 3-4 build sessions + 1 deploy session = 4-5 Workstream 3 total (matching the §C.3 estimate). Useful data point continuation of the calibration discipline started in the 2026-05-23-c §Entry (Workstream 2 came in at top end of 3-5 estimate; combined W1+W2 = 7 sessions vs. 4-8 estimated).

**No corrections-tier slip this session.** All decisions resolved via Rule 14f pickers; no fix-shape ambiguity; no estimate slip; no process slip. The path-convention catch was a planned in-scope deliverable (W1 explicitly flagged it for next-workstream surfacing) — not a slip but a successful execution of the documented hand-off mechanism.

**Cross-references:**

- `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-23-d (the design-doc twin of this §Entry; captures the path-convention refactor + Pattern memorialization at the design-doc layer per Rule 18 §B append-only).
- `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §C.3 (Workstream 3 implementation outline; Session 1 spec executed cleanly this session; Sessions 2-4 remaining).
- `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §A.3 (server-side per-user `UserTablePreferences` Prisma model keyed by (userId, projectId) — binding decision Q3; consumed this session via the new auth-derived-userId route).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 9 (NOT fired this session — pure build session; no destructive ops).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 14f forced-picker (fired TWICE — session-start scope-pick + path-convention refactor mid-session).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 18 (§A of `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` stays frozen; §B 2026-05-23-d is the new append).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 22 (pre-build read list executed at session start; §C.3 + §A.2 + §A.3 + §A.8 + §B 2026-05-24 through 2026-05-23-c + the existing `UrlTable.tsx` / `EditableField.tsx` were the binding inputs).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 23 Change Impact Audit (ADDITIVE + UI-only + ROUTE-PATH-REFACTOR — no schema; no data risk; zero downstream W#1 / W#3 cross-tool impact).
- HANDOFF_PROTOCOL Rule 25 (Multi-Workflow — single-branch session; main unchanged at `9969427`; W#1 untouched per Rule 3).

## §Entry 2026-05-24 — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 3 DEPLOY CLOSING ENTRY — Workstream 3 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED end-to-end on vklf.com via initial ff-merge `51e68f8..c727da9` + 5 in-session fix-forwards `f8293f1` → `0703174` → `712efa0` → `7358963` → `ac45737` + 6/6 Phase-4 verifies PASS + NEW reusable Pattern "Phase-4 verification fix-forward cascade in a single deploy session" + MEDIUM informational Rule 18 spec-capture gap observation with mechanical prevention added to working methodology + LOW informational P-43 cwd-leak ~9-times re-reproduction across the day + LOW informational date-stamping anomaly continuation

**Status:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closing entry for the P-46 Workstream 3 deploy session that converted Workstream 3's three build sessions into 6 production deploys to vklf.com in one calendar day; 11 distinct UI issues surfaced at Phase-4 director verification + all 11 resolved + all 11 re-verified PASS on vklf.com after fix-forward #5; director's exact end-of-session words: *"pass"*. P-46 Workstream 3 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-24 on vklf.com end-to-end.

**Session:** `session_2026-05-24_p46-workstream-3-deploy-and-five-fix-forwards`
**Branch:** `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` (ping-pong sync 6 times across the day)
**Deploy events:** 6 — initial ff-merge `51e68f8..c727da9` + fix-forward #1 `f8293f1` + fix-forward #2 `0703174` + fix-forward #3 `712efa0` + fix-forward #4 `7358963` + fix-forward #5 `ac45737`
**Total pushes today:** ~14 — 6 deploy pushes to `origin/main` (each gated via AskUserQuestion Rule 9 picker) + 6 ping-pong syncs to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping` + 1 end-of-session doc-batch push + 1 end-of-session ping-pong
**Director verdict at session end:** *"pass"* — all 11 Phase-4 issues PASS on vklf.com after fix-forward #5

**(a) Workstream 3 ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED end-to-end on vklf.com — full deploy timeline:**

1. **Initial deploy** — pre-deploy /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN at baselines (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / 558 ext UNCHANGED / 744 src/lib UNCHANGED / 61 routes UNCHANGED); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per Rule 27 (no `extensions/` source files in ff-merge bundle); Rule 9 director-Yes gate fired via AskUserQuestion picker; director picked "Deploy now (recommended)"; ff-merge `51e68f8..c727da9` carrying 6 commits clean; post-merge /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN on main; push to origin/main; Vercel auto-redeploy fired; ping-pong sync to workflow-2 clean. Phase-4 verify on vklf.com surfaced 5 issues → fix-forward #1.

2. **Fix-forward #1** — commit `f8293f1` 5 files +336/-177 — addressed Phase-4 issues 1-5 in one bundle: (Issue 1) extended column resize handles via ResizeObserver on the table + applied `height: tableHeight` on the handle so the line extends past the header showing a faint full-height column line during drag; (Issue 2) page-level full-width layout — removed `maxWidth: 1080px` + `margin: auto` on page.tsx `<main>` so the page consumes the viewport width; (Issue 3) table horizontal scroll — table `width` changed from `100%` to `max-content` + `minWidth: 100%` so the overflowX:auto wrapper scrolls horizontally when columns overflow; (Issue 4) relocated font-size stepper from ColumnVisibilityBar to UrlTable toolbar as bare `+`/`-` buttons (no `Npt` value display + no label); (Issue 5) multi-select platform checkboxes — Platform[] state shape with `?platforms=X,Y` URL convention + All Platforms select-all/deselect-all toggle + legacy `?platform=X` backwards compat in the URL-detail-page viewer (treats single-value `?platform=` as a one-element array on the new state shape). Director re-verified all 6 surfaces (5 fixed + the originally-passing-but-needed-re-confirmation column resize behavior) — all PASS.

3. **Fix-forward #2** — commit `0703174` 1 file +54/-6 — addressed Phase-4 Issue 6 (sticky table header + sticky horizontal scrollbar): table wrapper changed to `overflow: auto` + `maxHeight: calc(100vh - 200px)` + `minHeight: 400px`; all 3 `<th>` variants (drag-handle / sortable / non-sortable) got `position: sticky; top: 0; zIndex: 3; background: #0d1117` via shared `stickyHeaderStyle` constant; `ColumnResizeHandle` zIndex bumped from 2 to 4 to render above the sticky thead so the drag handle isn't covered. Phase-4 verify PASS.

4. **Fix-forward #3** — commit `712efa0` 3 files +105/-83 — addressed Phase-4 Issues 7-9: (Issue 7) director-specified column order — `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` + `COLUMNS` reshuffled in lockstep to: Category · Type · Sponsored · Product Name · Brand Name · Description 1 · Description 2 · Results Rank · Price · Product Stars · # Reviews · Seller Stars · Seller Reviews · Competition Score · URL · Status · Added On; (Issue 8) `↗` Open button relocated INSIDE Product Name cell with `e.stopPropagation()` so the cell still click-to-edits the text but the inline link opens the URL detail page in a new tab; the row-actions column shrunk from 88px to 52px containing only trash; the standalone `rowOpenButtonStyle` + `handleOpenClick` + `onOpenClick` prop all removed; NEW `inlineProductNameOpenButtonStyle`; (Issue 9) URL detail page full-width — `UrlDetailContent.tsx` `<main>` `maxWidth: 1080px` + `margin: auto` changed to `maxWidth: 100%` with `24px` side padding; inner content caps intentionally untouched (480px breadcrumb / 320px image / 480px video player). Phase-4 verify PASS.

5. **Fix-forward #4** — commit `7358963` 3 files +49/-12 — addressed Phase-4 Issue 10 (Platform column at very left of table): new `'platform'` entry at position 0 in `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` + `COLUMNS` + `ColumnSortKey` union + `cellRenderers`; renders friendly label via `PLATFORM_LABELS` lookup; `PLATFORM_LABELS` moved from `ColumnVisibilityBar` local const to `url-table-columns.ts` exported shared const so both the bar checkbox row + the table cell renderer use a single source of truth; column read-only display matches `addedAt`'s server-stamped read-only precedent; checkbox auto-appears in Columns visibility bar since the bar iterates `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS`. Phase-4 verify PASS.

6. **Fix-forward #5** — commit `ac45737` 1 file +80/-19 — addressed Phase-4 Issue 11 (Status column click-to-cycle): NEW internal `StatusCycleCell` sub-component replaces `InlineEnumCell` dropdown for `scrapingStatus`; one-click toggle between `INCOMPLETE`/`COMPLETE` with optimistic update + error rollback; `saving`-disabled prevents double-click pileup; bidirectional mirror with URL detail page's `EditableEnumField` Scraping Status toggle still works via the same shared `CompetitorUrl.scrapingStatus` PATCH; removed `InlineEnumCell` import + `SCRAPING_STATUS_OPTIONS` const since `scrapingStatus` was the only `InlineEnumCell` caller in this file. Phase-4 verify PASS. **Director's verbatim end-of-session words: "pass"** — all 11 Phase-4 issues confirmed PASS on vklf.com.

**(b) NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Phase-4 verification fix-forward cascade in a single deploy session":**

When Phase-4 director verification surfaces multiple issues post-deploy, fix-forward in-session rather than deferring; each fix-forward becomes its own build commit + own Rule 9 gate + own Phase-4 reverify cycle; the session ends with N deploys total. Today's session set the new high-water mark: 6 deploys (initial + 5 fix-forwards). Pairs with the P-45 Build #2 2026-05-22-i fix-forward Pattern (1 initial + 1 fix-forward) — today extends to N≥5 fix-forwards in one session showing the pattern scales.

**When to use:** any deploy session where Phase-4 verify surfaces multiple issues that are scoped + reversible + UI-only.
**When NOT to use:** if any issue requires schema change OR significant new code OR director shifts scope away from current workstream → defer to next session instead. The pattern depends on each fix being small enough that the cost of an extra `tsc + build + push + Vercel-redeploy + Phase-4-reverify` cycle is small relative to the cost of capturing the issue + scheduling a follow-up session for it.

Today's 11-issues / 5-fix-forwards execution validated the pattern's scaling: every fix-forward landed in under 30 minutes from issue surfacing to PASS verification; no fix-forward needed to be reverted; each one's tsc + build re-verification stayed at 61 routes UNCHANGED; full scoreboard was acceptably skipped per fix-forward (no new tests, no new routes, no new dependencies). The pattern composes with the "Multi-session workstream deploy gate timing" Pattern (memorialized 2026-05-23-c) — together they define the full multi-session-workstream deploy cadence: ONE foundation build + N UI builds + ONE end-of-workstream deploy (which may itself include N fix-forwards in cascade).

**(c) MEDIUM informational — Rule 18 spec-capture gap surfaced + fixed-forward in-session:**

Director's column-order specification for the Competition Data table existed in director's intent but was never echoed into binding docs (§C.3 of `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` or a §B refinement entry). The Session 2 (2026-05-23-e) implementation defaulted to "additive append" semantics — the 9 pre-P-46 columns kept their pre-existing positions (URL / Brand Name / Product Name / Sponsored / Product Stars / # Reviews / Category / Added On / Actions) and the 8 new ones were appended at the end (Type / Description 1 / Description 2 / Price / Competition Score / Results Rank / Seller Stars / Seller Reviews) without asking. The default felt natural for an additive change so no Rule 14f picker fired. The gap surfaced at Phase-4 verification of the initial deploy + fix-forwards #1-#2 — director re-specified the column order verbatim: "Category · Type · Sponsored · Product Name · Brand Name · Description 1 · Description 2 · Results Rank · Price · Product Stars · # Reviews · Seller Stars · Seller Reviews · Competition Score · URL · Status · Added On." Fix-forward #3 (commit `712efa0`) reshuffled both `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` + `COLUMNS` in lockstep to match.

**Mechanical prevention added to working methodology:** any UI-shape spec given mid-build (column order, button position, layout decision, sort default, etc.) must be echoed into binding docs + read back per Rule 14a BEFORE implementation lands. The capture point: at session-start when reading the design doc's §C.X session-spec block + the most-recent §B entries, scan for any sequencing/ordering/positioning language; if any is implicit-but-not-explicit, fire a Rule 14f picker on the canonical spec BEFORE writing code. This is a tightening of Rule 14a from "the design doc is the source of truth" to "the design doc is the source of truth; if any aspect of the implementation feels like 'the default should be obvious,' explicitly check that the default is captured in binding docs first."

Capture this prevention as a footnote in this §Entry; it ports to all future workstream UI sessions where any visual ordering matters.

**(d) LOW informational — P-43 cwd-leak class re-reproduced ~9 times today across the day:**

Across pre-deploy /scoreboard + post-merge /scoreboard + the various single-check tsc/build sanity passes between fix-forwards, the P-43 cwd-leak class re-reproduced approximately 9 times (rough count; the high frequency reflects the unusually high number of sanity-check passes in a 6-deploy session). Same Pattern as the 2026-05-22-i + 2026-05-24 + 2026-05-27 + 2026-05-23-c + 2026-05-23-e + 2026-05-23-f reproductions: when Check 3 (extension `npm test`) cd's to `extensions/competition-scraping/`, the cwd leaks into Check 5's `npm run build` call. Caught + recovered every single time with the absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` template form. Pattern stable; P-43's template-hardening fix protects verbatim-template-read pathways but NOT Claude's inline-typed shortcuts (the high count today reflects that Claude reaches for the shortcut form more often when running many sanity-check loops in a row).

LOW informational; immediate detection + recovery every time; no impact on scoreboard results; no impact on deploy results. Counted as a single ongoing observation per the prior session's framing rather than each reproduction being a separate finding.

**(e) LOW informational — Date-stamping anomaly continuation:**

Calendar date today per director confirmation is **2026-05-24**. The 5 fix-forward commit MESSAGES used suffix labels `2026-05-23-g` through `2026-05-23-k` continuing yesterday's `-f` sequence (which itself was the prior session's suffix). Git commit TIMESTAMPS in the actual git history are correct (2026-05-24); only the human-readable labels in commit MESSAGES diverged. Same anomaly class as noted in the prior 2026-05-23-b §Entry. **Pattern:** when many sessions occur in close succession across calendar-day boundaries, the `-letter` suffix convention sometimes diverges from the calendar date — Claude treats "this session is the Nth after the prior session" as more salient than "this session is on calendar day X." Today's session particularly amplified the divergence because the 5 fix-forwards were operationally adjacent to the previous day's Session 3 in Claude's narrative-flow framing.

LOW informational; not corrections-tier; no remediation needed beyond this capture. Header bumps + ROADMAP polish entry + design doc §B entry + NEXT_SESSION.md + this CORRECTIONS_LOG §Entry all use the correct calendar date 2026-05-24 (the in-doc dating discipline is intact; only the in-message commit labels diverged).

**What landed this session:**

- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/url-table-columns.ts`** — adds `defaultWidth: number` to `TableColumnDef` + per-column defaults (URL=280, Description-1/2=240, Product Name=220, etc.) + NEW constants `MIN_COLUMN_WIDTH = 60` / `MAX_COLUMN_WIDTH = 600` / `FONT_SIZE_MIN = 10` / `FONT_SIZE_MAX = 24` / `FONT_SIZE_DEFAULT = 14` (the font-size trio mirrors the handler's validator constants per Session 1's `extractTablePreferencesPatch`) + NEW `resolveColumnWidth(map, column)` helper.

- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx`** — adds three new state slices (`columnWidths` / `fontSize` / `rowOrder`) alongside Session 1's `columnVisibility` + extends the seed-from-GET effect to fill all four fields from `UserTablePreferences` + NEW handlers `handleColumnResize` / `handleFontSizeChange` / `handleRowReorder` all sharing the existing `prefsTimerRef` 500 ms debounced PUT (a burst of drag events from any control coalesces into one network write on idle).

- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/ColumnVisibilityBar.tsx`** — adds a third group "Text size" at the far right of the bar with − / Npt / + stepper buttons disabled at FONT_SIZE_MIN / FONT_SIZE_MAX bounds + the supporting styles (`fontSizeGroupStyle`, `stepperStyle`, `stepperButtonStyle`, `stepperValueStyle`).

- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/UrlTable.tsx`** — major rewrite (+629/-178 region; bulk of session diff):
  - Splits the `SortKey` type into `ColumnSortKey | 'manual'`; 'manual' mode respects `rowOrder` from `UserTablePreferences`, otherwise the existing comparator-based sort wins.
  - Wraps `<tbody>` in `<DndContext sensors={sensors} collisionDetection={closestCenter} onDragEnd={handleDragEnd}>` + `<SortableContext items={...} strategy={verticalListSortingStrategy}>`.
  - `PointerSensor` with `activationConstraint: { distance: 4 }` so per-cell click-to-edit (Session 2) isn't hijacked by drag activation on the row's drag handle.
  - Each `<tr>` becomes a NEW `SortableUrlRow` sub-component that uses `useSortable({ id: row.id })` + applies `CSS.Transform.toString(transform)` style + threads `attributes` onto the row + spreads `listeners` onto the leading drag-handle button.
  - Adds `<colgroup>` with explicit widths per column (drag-handle column 32px + visible columns from `resolveColumnWidth(...)` + actions column 88px); switches table to `tableLayout: 'fixed'` so colgroup widths bind exactly.
  - Adds NEW `ColumnResizeHandle` sub-component — drag handle absolutely positioned at the right edge of every `<th>`; `onPointerDown` captures + tracks `pointermove` (computing delta + clamping to MIN/MAX) + commits on `pointerup`; optimistic per-pointermove commit so the table re-renders width live during the drag.
  - Applies `effectiveFontSize` as `fontSize: ${effectiveFontSize}px` on the `<table>` element so every cell scales together.
  - `handleDragEnd` computes the new id order from the sorted display, preserves any rowOrder IDs not in the current sorted set (e.g., rows filtered out by the search box), calls `onRowReorder` with the merged order, sets `sortKey` to `'manual'` so the user-imposed order sticks visually.
  - Adds NEW `tableColumnDefByKey(key)` helper to resolve a column id to its `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` entry for width lookup.

- **MODIFIED `package.json`** — adds three @dnd-kit dependencies (`@dnd-kit/core`, `@dnd-kit/sortable`, `@dnd-kit/utilities`).
- **MODIFIED `package-lock.json`** — auto-updated; 4 packages added net (3 direct + 1 transitive `@dnd-kit/accessibility`).

**Rule 14f picker disposition:**

ONE Rule 14f forced-picker fired — session-start scope-pick. Director picked "Bundle all 3 controls (recommended)" over "Ship just font size + drag-to-reorder, defer column resize" and "Ship just column resize, defer font size + drag-to-reorder." Recommended option per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (most thorough — all 3 share the same `UserTablePreferences` plumbing + debounced PUT lifecycle, so bundling avoids re-deriving the same patterns 2-3 times across sessions). ZERO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution — library choice (`@dnd-kit` over alternatives), MIN/MAX clamping bounds, sort-mode disambiguation ('manual' vs. existing column sort), and "preserve rowOrder ids not in current sorted set" semantics were all clear "most thorough/reliable" defaults per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Shared debounced-mutation lifecycle reused across an N-control surface":**

When a foundation session wires a debounced PUT lifecycle for ONE field of a shared per-user prefs row, the next session can land N additional controls without re-deriving the batching logic by routing every new control through the SAME timer ref + the SAME flush callback (with a partial body specific to that control). The 500 ms debounce coalesces bursts from ANY of the N controls into ONE network write; the latest mutation always wins because each handler clears the timer before resetting it. Today's session validated this Pattern by routing 3 new controls (column resize / row reorder / font-size change) through Session 1's `prefsTimerRef` + `flushPrefsPut`, producing zero additional debounce code beyond a one-line handler per control. Pairs with W2 S5's "Field-allowlist subset extraction" Pattern as another example of "foundation-session primitive becomes Pattern when reused by N=2+ subsequent sessions." Reusable by any future workstream/session that adds a control to a debounced-PUT-backed prefs row.

**Three new npm dependencies landed (first runtime drag-and-drop library on this layer):**

`@dnd-kit/core` + `@dnd-kit/sortable` + `@dnd-kit/utilities` (with one transitive `@dnd-kit/accessibility`). First time the Competition Data table layer takes a runtime drag-and-drop library; bundle size +~30 KB gzipped one-time cost. The @dnd-kit family chosen as the "most thorough/reliable" default per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` — modern React-first API, pointer-event based, accessibility built-in, MIT license, actively maintained.

**P-43 cwd-leak class re-reproduced ONCE (sixth reproduction overall) — LOW informational:**

During /scoreboard Check 5 the parallel-Bash invocation leaked cwd from Checks 2+3 (which legitimately `cd extensions/competition-scraping/`) into the Check 5 `npm run build` call, producing the EXTENSION build output (`Built extension in 2.432 s`) instead of Next.js routes. Caught immediately from the output content + recovered with the absolute-`cd` template form. Same LOW informational pattern as the 2026-05-22-i + 2026-05-23-b + 2026-05-23-c + 2026-05-23-e reproductions. Reinforces P-43's standing observation that template hardening protects verbatim-template-read pathways but NOT Claude's parallel-Bash inline shortcuts. Recovery cost ~30 seconds; no impact on scoreboard result. Sub-observation only; NOT a corrections-tier slip.

**Calibration data point:**

Session 3 of 3-4 estimated Workstream 3 sessions landed cleanly within the bundled scope. Workstream 3 came in at the LOWER end of the 3-4 estimate (3 sessions vs. 3-4 budgeted), reflecting that:
- Session 1's foundational `UserTablePreferences` plumbing (DI-seam handler + auth-derived-userId route + horizontal bar + sidebar removal) was a one-session bundle as specced;
- Session 2's bundled scope (click-to-edit cell editors + 8 new data columns) fit cleanly in one session because 3 of the 7 needed cell-editor primitives already existed in W2 Session 5's EditableField.tsx;
- Session 3's bundled scope (column resize + drag-reorder + font size) fit cleanly in one session because all 3 controls reused Session 1's debounced PUT lifecycle without re-derivation.

Combined Workstream 1+2+3 calibration: W1 = 1 session (under 2-3 estimate); W2 = 5 sessions + 1 deploy (top end of 3-5 estimate); W3 = 3 sessions (low end of 3-4 estimate); Workstream 3 deploy session pending. Useful data point for sizing Workstreams 4-5.

**Cross-references:**
- `docs/COMPETITION_DATA_V2_DESIGN.md` §B 2026-05-23-f (the design doc §B entry capturing today's Pattern memorialization + scope-pick outcome + impact-on-§A: none/§A stays frozen per Rule 18);
- Prior §Entry 2026-05-23-e (Session 2 closing entry — sets the "In-table inline-cell parallel-component set" Pattern this session builds atop);
- Prior §Entry 2026-05-23-d (Session 1 closing entry — sets the "Foundation-workstream path-convention drift" Pattern + the foundational `prefsTimerRef` plumbing this session reuses for N=3 new controls);
- `docs/NEXT_SESSION.md` (rewritten for the P-46 Workstream 3 deploy session — Phase-4 deploy under Rule 9 gate; ff-merges 6 commits as one fast-forward);
- `package.json` + `package-lock.json` (the 3 new @dnd-kit dependencies + 1 transitive).

**Lesson:** when a foundation session ships a debounced-PUT plumbing for ONE field of a shared prefs row, plan subsequent sessions to route ALL additional controls through the SAME timer ref + flush callback — the 500 ms debounce coalesces bursts from any of the N controls and the resulting code surface is N+1 handlers (not N+1 plumbing). Reuse Sessions 1-2's `prefsTimerRef` + `flushPrefsPut` as the canonical lifecycle anchor for any future P-46 control that writes to `UserTablePreferences`. The "Shared debounced-mutation lifecycle reused across an N-control surface" Pattern is reusable beyond P-46 — any future workstream with a per-user prefs row gets the same shape for free.
