## §Entry 2026-05-23-e — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 3 SESSION 2 CLOSING ENTRY — Click-to-edit cell editors per §A.2 + 8 new data columns + whole-row onClick removed in favor of per-row "↗" Open button + Pattern reusability validated ("Field-allowlist subset extraction" applied to `competitionScore`) + NEW reusable Pattern "In-table inline-cell parallel-component set to URL-detail-page EditableField primitives" + P-43 cwd-leak fifth re-reproduction + calibration data point

**Status:** INFORMATIONAL — not a slip-of-this-session; closing entry for the P-46 Workstream 3 second build session that lands the §A.2 click-to-edit cell editors + 8 new data columns + the UX behavior change replacing whole-row onClick with a per-row "↗" Open button; second of 3-4 estimated Workstream 3 sessions per §C.3.

**Headline:** P-46 Workstream 3 Session 2 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL via build commit `899afd4` (7 files +1414/-91) on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`. Bundled scope (click-to-edit + new columns together) landed cleanly per the session-start Rule 14f picker outcome; one new reusable Pattern memorialized; one prior Pattern validated reusable; one UX behavior change worth surfacing; one LOW informational P-43 re-reproduction; calibration data point captured.

**What landed this session:**

- **NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/InlineCells.tsx`** (~800 LOC compact in-table click-to-edit editor primitives — parallel-component set to the URL detail page's `EditableField.tsx` primitives). Exports:
  - **`InlineTextCell`** — single-click value-to-input transformation; optional `multiline` flag → textarea; commit on blur or Enter; cancel on Escape.
  - **`InlineNumberCell`** — with optional `min` / `max` / `step` / `integer` constraints (the `integer` flag rejects decimal input + clamps to int on parse).
  - **`InlineBooleanCell`** — single-click toggle with checkmark state.
  - **`InlineEnumCell<T>`** — single-click opens an options popover; single-click on an option commits.
  - **`InlineDateCell`** — wraps a native HTML date input with optional `readOnly` for server-stamped timestamps like `addedAt`.
  - **`InlineUrlCell`** — delegates to InlineTextCell with link-coloring + non-empty validation.
  - Save lifecycle uniform across variants: optimistic update + rollback-on-throw + inline error pill until next edit. `e.stopPropagation()` on every cell-click handler so the row's onClick doesn't fire while editing (decoupled from this session's removal of the whole-row onClick — defensive-future-safe even if a parent re-adds it).
- **NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/competition-score-validation.ts`** (~65 LOC trust-boundary helper `extractCompetitionScorePatch(body)` for the new `competitionScore` PATCH allowlist per §A.7 — integer 1-100 nullable; mirrors W2 Session 5's `url-structural-fields-validation.ts` Pattern). Returns `{ ok: true, patch } | { ok: false, error }` discriminator the route spreads onto its Prisma update payload. **Direct application of W2 Session 5's "Field-allowlist subset extraction" reusable Pattern** — validating the Pattern's reusability after one prior application; total Pattern applications in P-46 = 2 (S5's URL structural fields + today's competitionScore).
- **NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/competition-score-validation.test.ts`** — 18 new node:test cases covering: empty body / null body / non-object body (defensive paths return empty patch); integer in range (1, 50, 100) accepted; null explicitly clears column; below-min (0, -5) / above-max (101) / decimal (50.5) / NaN / Infinity / string / boolean / array / explicit-undefined all rejected with appropriate error messages; unknown keys alongside `competitionScore` ignored. src/lib count 726 → 744 (+18, exact match).
- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/url-table-columns.ts`** (+47 LOC) — adds `TableColumnDataType` discriminator (`'url' | 'text' | 'text-multiline' | 'number-integer' | 'number-decimal' | 'boolean' | 'enum' | 'date-readonly'`) driving per-cell editor selection in UrlTable; extends `TABLE_COLUMN_DEFS` registry with 8 new column defs; total registry now 17 columns.
- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/UrlTable.tsx`** (+263 LOC / -109 LOC) — major rewrite. Extends `SortKey` union from 9 keys to 17. Extends `COLUMNS` array with 8 new entries (5 W1-additive — `type` / `description1` / `description2` / `price` / `competitionScore`; 3 pre-existing-but-unsurfaced — `resultsPageRank` / `sellerStarRating` / `numSellerReviews`). Rewrites `cellRenderers` map to use `InlineCells` components — each cell now renders its column's appropriate editor wired to `onCellSave(row.id, { fieldName: next })`. Adds required `onCellSave` prop. Removes row-level `onClick={handleRowOpen}` (replaced by explicit "↗" Open button in row-actions column). Adds per-row "↗" Open button next to the trash button; both buttons sit in a single 88px-wide row-actions column at the right edge.
- **MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx`** (+44 LOC) — new `handleCellSave` `useCallback` PATCHes `/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]` with the field patch + replaces the local row in `urls` state with the server's authoritative response; throws on PATCH failure so the inline cell can render its error pill. Passed through to `<UrlTable />` as `onCellSave`. Imports `UpdateCompetitorUrlRequest` + `UpdateCompetitorUrlResponse` from shared-types.
- **MODIFIED `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/urls/[urlId]/route.ts`** (+13 LOC) — imports the new `extractCompetitionScorePatch` helper; adds `competitionScore` validation step between the existing structural-fields extraction and the `overallCompetitorAnalysis` allowlist — mirrors W2 S5's helper-extraction pattern.

**Rule 14f forced-picker fired this session (one):**

1. **Session-start scope-pick** (Workstream 3 Session 2 scope) — director picked "Bundle: new columns + click-to-edit together (recommended)" over "Click-to-edit on existing columns only". Recommended because both touch the same cell-renderer map in UrlTable; splitting them across sessions requires rewriting the map twice. The §C.3 Session 2 spec explicitly enumerates both as in-scope.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "In-table inline-cell parallel-component set to URL-detail-page EditableField primitives":** When a feature needs the same edit-affordance shape in two different visual contexts (here: per-cell editing in a dense table row vs. per-field editing in a labeled detail panel), prefer a **parallel-component set** over forcing the existing components to handle both contexts via configuration props. The visual + ergonomic constraints differ enough (label + pencil-button + spaced layout in a panel vs. compact click-on-value + auto-focus + tight padding in a table cell) that the configurations would proliferate. The parallel set keeps each callsite typed against the right shape + lets the two evolve independently.

This Pattern **pairs with W2 Session 3's "OverallAnalysisBox parallel to PerItemAnalysisBox" Pattern** (parallel-component for ANALYSIS surfaces). Together they cover **"parallel-component for visual-context-divergence"** as a meta-shape applicable across both EDIT and ANALYSIS surfaces. Sister patterns in the P-46 catalog: PerItemAnalysisBox extraction (S1) / OverallAnalysisBox parallel (S3) / Per-record handler DI-seam precedent extension (S4) / Field-allowlist subset extraction (S5) / Foundation-workstream path-convention drift (W3 S1) / In-table inline-cell parallel-component set (this session). Reusable across Workstreams 4-5 + future polish items needing the same edit-affordance shape across multiple visual contexts.

**Validation of W2 S5's "Field-allowlist subset extraction" Pattern reusability:** The new `extractCompetitionScorePatch(body)` helper is a direct second application of the Pattern shipped 2026-05-23-b for the 4 URL-level structural fields. Same shape: pure trust-boundary helper returning `{ ok, patch } | { ok, error }`; same call shape from the route (`const result = extract(body); if (!result.ok) return 400; spread patch onto Prisma data`); same test coverage shape (~15-25 node:test cases per helper). Validates the Pattern travels cleanly without per-callsite customization. Total Pattern applications in P-46 = 2 (S5 URL structural fields + today's competitionScore).

**UX behavior change worth surfacing to director:** The whole-row `onClick={handleRowOpen}` is **removed** this session — mandated by §A.2 since every cell is now click-to-edit and a whole-row onClick conflicted with cell-level click. Replaced by an explicit per-row "↗" Open button in the row-actions column (sitting to the LEFT of the trash button; both buttons in a single 88px-wide row-actions column at the right edge of the table). The visual hover-highlight on the row remains (purely cosmetic) but the cursor is no longer pointer-on-the-row; only the explicit buttons are pointer. **Director should test this** — the row-click → URL detail page navigation no longer works; the "↗" arrow icon is the new affordance.

**P-43 cwd-leak class re-reproduced ONCE this session** during /scoreboard execution. Parallel Bash calls for Check 2 (extension tsc, `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && npx tsc --noEmit`) + Check 4 (`npm test`) ran in parallel; the `cd` from Check 2 leaked into Check 4's cwd, causing `npm test` to invoke the EXTENSION's test script (558 tests) instead of falling back to the root's (no test script). Caught from the test-name content (urlsMatchAfterNormalization / buildRecognitionSet are extension tests). Recovered by reading `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` for the canonical absolute-path commands + re-running Check 4 with the explicit `node --test --experimental-strip-types $(find /workspaces/...)` form. **LOW informational** — FIFTH reproduction of the same Pattern (prior reproductions: 2026-05-22-i + 2026-05-23-b + 2026-05-23-c twice during pre-deploy + post-merge scoreboard runs). Reinforces P-43's standing observation that template hardening protects verbatim-template-read pathways but NOT Claude's inline-typed shortcuts. Recovery cost: ~30 seconds + one extra Bash call. **Not promoted to a separate corrections-tier §Entry** — same Pattern as the four prior reproductions; same recovery path; tightens Claude's vigilance for parallel-Bash invocations where one sibling cd's away.

**Calibration data point — Workstream 3 Session 2 sized correctly under bundled scope:** Session 2 of 3-4 estimated Workstream 3 sessions landed cleanly within the bundled scope (click-to-edit cell editors per data type + the 8 bundled new data columns). The pleasant discovery that 3 of the 7 needed cell-editor primitives already existed in W2 Session 5's EditableField.tsx (only 2 truly new editor types needed: InlineDateCell + InlineUrlCell beyond the parallel set) meant the session came in at the bundled scope without scope creep. Bundled-scope Rule 14f picker outcome played out as estimated — landing both in one session avoided the double-rewrite of the cell-renderer map that splitting would have cost. Useful data point continuation of the calibration discipline started in the 2026-05-23-c §Entry. If §C.3 Session 3 (column resize + drag-reorder + font size) is similarly well-specced, expect 3-4 build sessions + 1 deploy session = 4-5 Workstream 3 total (matching the §C.3 estimate); currently at 2 of 3-4 build sessions DONE.

**No corrections-tier slip this session.** All decisions resolved via the session-start Rule 14f picker + default-to-recommendation Patterns; no fix-shape ambiguity; no estimate slip; no process slip. The P-43 re-reproduction is the same LOW informational class as four prior reproductions and is captured here informationally + cross-referenced rather than escalated.

## §Entry 2026-05-24-b — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 4 SESSION 1 CLOSING ENTRY — Comprehensive Competitor Analysis page first build session lands the full §C.4 Session 1 scope at code level + TWO new reusable Patterns memorialized (Pattern A "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance" + Pattern B "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer") + FIRST APPLICATION of the 2026-05-24 Rule 14a tightening (the §C.4 vs. NEXT_SESSION.md pointer divergence on Session 1 scope was surfaced via Rule 14f picker rather than silently picking the pointer's recommendation) + calibration data point

**Session:** `session_2026-05-24-b_p46-workstream-4-session-1-comprehensive-analysis-page`
**Date:** 2026-05-24-b (second session of the day; suffix `-b` per Rule 14)
**Branch:** `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (single-branch session; no main push since Session 1 is a build session, not a deploy session)
**Build commit:** `283d4d1` — 9 files +1258/-32
**Tier:** INFORMATIONAL — no corrections-tier slip this session; closing entry memorializing two new reusable Patterns + the first application of the Rule 14a tightening + a calibration data point.

**What this entry captures (5 sub-points):**

**(a) Session 1 closed at code level for W#4.** First build session of the P-46 Workstream 4 implementation arc (Session 1 of ~2-3 estimated per §C.4). Landed the full §C.4 Session 1 scope end-to-end at code level — NEW `comprehensive-analysis.ts` DI-seam handler factory (~225 LOC mirroring `user-table-preferences.ts` precedent — `extractComprehensiveAnalysisPatch` strict trust-boundary validator + `toWireShape` JsonValue → Record coercion with bad-DB-shape fallback to empty doc + `makeComprehensiveAnalysisHandlers` factory returning GET + PUT; PUT populates `lastEditedBy` from auth-derived `userId` on every upsert; `lastEditedAt` Prisma-managed via `@updatedAt` on update, passed explicitly on create) + 20 new node:test cases bringing src/lib to 764 + W1's 501-stub at `comprehensive-analysis/route.ts` replaced by thin DI-seam shim wrapping `makeComprehensiveAnalysisHandlers` via the `verifyProjectAuthAdapter` pattern + NEW page route `comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (~269 LOC client page route using `useWorkflowContext` + `useParams` + `useRouter`; GET on mount via `authFetch`; 404-tolerant empty-state; Edit/Done toggle; back-button "← Competition Data" at top; last-edited timestamp footer) + NEW `AnalysisEditor.tsx` (~151 LOC parallel-component to W2 S1's `PerItemAnalysisBox`) + NEW `AnalysisReadView.tsx` (~76 LOC; renders via `RichTextEditor readOnly=true`) + extended `RichTextEditor.tsx` `variant='full'` toolbar per §A.5 (H1/H2/H3 + Bold + Italic + Underline + bullet/numbered lists + Link + Code block; `@tiptap/extension-underline` imported + registered for both variants) + standalone navigation button row above `ColumnVisibilityBar` in `CompetitionScrapingViewer.tsx` (+31 LOC) + `@tiptap/extension-underline@3.23.6` promoted from transitive to top-level dep.

**(b) NEW reusable Pattern A memorialized — "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance":**

When an edit affordance needs the same save-lifecycle shape at TWO scope levels (per-row vs. per-Project), extract parallel components that share structure but differ on the per-scope wire details:

- **PerItemAnalysisBox** (W2 S1) — per-row scope; PATCH method; body `{ analysis }`; `variant='minimal'`.
- **AnalysisEditor** (W4 S1, today) — per-Project scope; PUT method; body `{ contentJson }`; `variant='full'`.

Both wrap the same `RichTextEditor`. Both have the same Saving/Saved/Failed status-indicator structure. Both use a generation-counter ref for stale-response protection.

Pairs with:
- W2 S1's "PerItemAnalysisBox extraction" Pattern (2026-05-25)
- W2 S3's "OverallAnalysisBox parallel component" Pattern (2026-05-27)

Meta-shape: when a shared per-action lifecycle (debounced persist + status indicator + retry on error) is needed at N scopes (per-row / per-category / per-Project), each new scope adds a thin parallel component that wraps the same editor primitive — the save-lifecycle structure stays uniform via shared property shape, while per-scope wire details (method, body, scope key) parameterize via props. Three reusable Patterns now compose into this meta-shape across the W2+W4 implementation arc.

WHEN TO USE: when adding a new edit affordance at a new scope (per-row / per-category / per-Project / per-Workflow) that needs the same save-lifecycle structure as an existing per-scope component. WHEN NOT TO USE: if the new scope's wire details (method / body / status semantics) diverge significantly from the existing components — re-using by configuration props fragments the call sites; spinning up a parallel component preserves wire-contract clarity.

**(c) NEW reusable Pattern B memorialized — "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer":**

§A.5 mentions `generateHTML` for non-editor read views. AnalysisReadView instead uses `RichTextEditor readOnly=true` (the wrapper supports a `readOnly` prop since W2 S1). Rationale: identical typography between edit and read modes without a duplicate render path. The editor's overhead is one ProseMirror instance per mount, negligible for a single per-Project doc. A separate static renderer would risk style drift between the two paths. Worth memorializing as a reusable Pattern for future read-view/edit-view siblings.

WHEN TO USE: when a feature has both an edit mode and a read mode for the same rich content (TipTap doc, markdown, etc.) AND the wrapper already supports a readOnly prop AND the per-render overhead is small relative to page load. WHEN NOT TO USE: when the read view fans out across many simultaneous instances on a single page (per-row read-view of N rich-text fields would multiply ProseMirror instances) — in that case the static renderer path is the right choice for performance.

**(d) FIRST APPLICATION of the 2026-05-24 Rule 14a tightening:**

The 2026-05-24 W3 deploy session memorialized the mechanical prevention: any UI-shape spec given mid-build must be echoed into binding docs + read back per Rule 14a BEFORE implementation lands. Today's session is the FIRST APPLICATION of this tightening at a parallel-shape: the §C.4 binding spec named Session 1 scope as the full route handlers + page + edit + read-view scope; yesterday's NEXT_SESSION.md pointer had defaulted to a more conservative scope (handler-only + page route + read-only display). Rule 14a directed surfacing the divergence to director rather than silently picking the pointer's recommendation; director picked the §C.4 binding spec. The pointer-vs-binding-spec divergence is a sibling shape of the column-order spec-capture gap that triggered the 2026-05-24 mechanical prevention — both are mid-arc planning artifacts where the binding spec wins over the more ephemeral document. Today's first application validates the tightening: catching the divergence at session-start added ~5 minutes to the session opening (one extra Rule 14f picker) and avoided a potential mid-session scope expansion / scope contraction friction.

WHEN TO USE: any time the NEXT_SESSION.md pointer + the binding design doc (§A/§B/§C) appear to diverge on session scope, scope boundaries, or implementation shape. WHEN NOT TO USE: when the binding design doc explicitly defers a question to the next session's pointer (the pointer becomes the binding source for that scoped question).

**(e) Calibration data point — Session 1 of 2-3 estimated landed cleanly within scope:**

Session duration ~150-180 min (top end of recommended-scope estimate per the Rule 14f Session 1 scope-pick rationale); no overrun; no fix-forward; all 17 in-session TaskCreate tasks completed cleanly. Two new Patterns surfaced organically during implementation. Continuation of the W1+W2+W3 calibration discipline — total P-46 spend now at 12 sessions (1 W1 + 5 W2 build + 1 W2 deploy + 3 W3 build + 1 W3 deploy + 1 W4 build today) vs. 14-16 estimated trajectory for the full P-46 arc; W4 = ~2-3 sessions per §C.4 (1 down, 1-2 to go); W5 = ~1-2 sessions per §C.5; total P-46 trajectory remains on plan.

## §Entry 2026-05-25 — INFORMATIONAL — P-46 WORKSTREAM 4 SESSION 2 CLOSING ENTRY — Internal-hyperlink TipTap extension + Link-to-URL picker affordance lands the full §C.4 Session 2 scope at code level + NEW reusable Pattern "Custom TipTap extension via `addProseMirrorPlugins` for click interception of shorthand hrefs without a custom Mark type" + TWO Rule 14f session-start picker outcomes + calibration data point (Workstream 4 implementation arc COMPLETE at code level across Sessions 1-2 of ~2-3 estimated)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-25_p46-workstream-4-session-2-internal-hyperlink-tiptap-extension`
**Date:** 2026-05-25
**Branch:** `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (single-branch session; no main push since Session 2 is a build session, not a deploy session)
**Build commit:** `5854eff` — 8 files +786/-1

**What landed (file-by-file matching build commit `5854eff`):**

- NEW `src/lib/rich-text/url-reference-helpers.ts` (~115 LOC pure helpers — `extractUrlIdFromHref` extracts the `<urlId>` from a `#url/<urlId>` href shape returning the matched id OR `null`; `buildInternalUrlPath(projectId, urlId)` returns the canonical detail-page path `/projects/<projectId>/competition-scraping/url/<urlId>/`; `buildInternalUrlHref(urlId)` returns the canonical shorthand `#url/<urlId>` href used inside the editor doc; `filterUrlsByQuery(urls, query)` returns a case-insensitive filtered subset of the URL picker entries; `defaultLinkLabelForUrl(url)` derives a sensible default link text from a URL's product name or seller name or the raw URL; exports `URL_REFERENCE_HREF_PREFIX = '#url/'` constant + `UrlPickerEntry { id, productName, sellerName, url }` interface).
- NEW `src/lib/rich-text/url-reference-helpers.test.ts` (~158 LOC; 19 new node:test cases bringing src/lib to **783 from baseline 764** — exact match with 19 new cases; covers all 5 helpers including malformed-href tolerance, percent-encoded ids, multi-segment ids, empty-query passthrough, and edge cases on the default-label derivation).
- NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/components/UrlReferenceExtension.ts` (~105 LOC TipTap Extension via `addProseMirrorPlugins`; intercepts clicks on `<a href="#url/<urlId>">` in both editable + read-only modes via ProseMirror's `handleClick` prop; extracts urlId via the pure helper from the raw `getAttribute('href')` value — **not** `link.href` which is browser-resolved to an absolute fragment URL that includes the page path; calls `options.onInternalLinkClick(urlId)` callback so the consumer decides how to resolve the click — today's consumer is the editor wrapper which calls `router.push(buildInternalUrlPath(projectId, urlId))`).
- NEW `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/components/LinkToUrlPicker.tsx` (~304 LOC toolbar dropdown component — lazy-loads URL list via `authFetch('/api/projects/<projectId>/competition-scraping/urls')` on first open + caches; case-insensitive search filter via the shared `filterUrlsByQuery` helper; on pick inserts the chosen URL's default label text at cursor position + applies the Link mark with `#url/<urlId>` href via TipTap's `editor.chain().focus().insertContent({...}).setLink({href}).run()` pattern; closes on outside-click; tab/arrow keys navigate the filtered list).
- MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/components/RichTextEditor.tsx` (+90/-1 LOC) — accepts a new optional `projectId?: string` prop; uses `useRouter` from `next/navigation`; stores `projectId` + `router` in refs so the click handler (registered ONCE at editor mount via the Extension's `handleClick`) always sees the current values without stale-closure bugs; registers `UrlReferenceExtension` with `handleInternalLinkClick = (urlId) => { if (projectIdRef.current) routerRef.current?.push(buildInternalUrlPath(projectIdRef.current, urlId)) }` callback; extends `Link.configure`'s `isAllowedUri` to accept `#url/` hrefs (otherwise the Link extension's default URL-shape gatekeeper would reject the non-http href shape and silently strip the mark when the user pastes `#url/<urlId>` content); surfaces `LinkToUrlPicker` in the 'full' toolbar variant only when `projectId` provided (graceful degradation if a consumer wires up the editor without a projectId — the extension still works for any pre-existing `#url/<urlId>` hrefs in the doc, but the toolbar picker hides); inline `<style>` tag with a small `InternalLinkStyles` component emitting CSS scoped to `.plos-rt-editor a[href^="#url/"]::before { content: "🔗 "; ... }` for the visual distinctness signal picked at session-start.
- MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/components/AnalysisEditor.tsx` (+7 LOC) — accepts + passes-through `projectId` prop to the inner `RichTextEditor`.
- MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/components/AnalysisReadView.tsx` (+5 LOC) — accepts + passes-through `projectId` prop to the inner `RichTextEditor` (the read-mode editor also needs the click interception since rendered hyperlinks must navigate in read mode).
- MODIFIED `src/app/projects/[projectId]/competition-scraping/comprehensive-analysis/page.tsx` (+2 LOC) — passes `projectId` (already in scope via `useParams()`) to both `AnalysisEditor` + `AnalysisReadView`.

**TWO Rule 14f forced-pickers fired at session start, both decided by director per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`:**

1. **Session 2 scope.** Three options surfaced: (a) Extension + Link-to-URL picker bundle (most thorough — RECOMMENDED), (b) Extension only this session, picker deferred to a polish-detour, (c) Picker only this session (NOT recommended — picker depends on the extension to render the hyperlinks correctly). Director picked **(a)**. Rationale captured in the picker label: the picker is the user-facing affordance that makes the shorthand discoverable + ergonomic; without it, only users who already know the `#url/<urlId>` syntax can use the feature.

2. **Visual styling of internal hyperlinks.** Three options surfaced: (a) Identical to external hyperlinks — same blue underlined treatment, no distinguishing visual signal, (b) Distinct — small URL icon prefix (🔗) + same blue underlined (RECOMMENDED — keeps consistent typography while adding an unobtrusive signal that the link is internal vs. external), (c) Distinct — different color (would diverge typographically and feel "noisier"). Director picked **(b)**. Implemented via inline `<style>` tag emitted by the editor wrapper, scoped to `.plos-rt-editor a[href^="#url/"]::before { content: "🔗 "; ... }`. The CSS attribute selector `[href^="#url/"]` keys off the href shape — same mechanism used by the Extension's `handleClick` filter; this means the styling + click interception share their criterion + don't drift from each other.

**ZERO Rule 14f sub-pickers fired during execution** per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`. Several design micro-decisions came up + each had a clear "most thorough/reliable" default:

- **Extension vs. custom Mark type:** chose Extension via `addProseMirrorPlugins` (lower-cost + reuses the existing Link mark); a custom Mark would have required a new schema type + custom rendering + custom parsing rules.
- **ProseMirror `handleClick` vs. a per-Mark click handler:** chose `handleClick` at the EditorView level; it intercepts at the right layer + sees the raw DOM node so `getAttribute('href')` returns the unresolved shorthand.
- **Stale-closure prevention:** chose refs for `projectId` + `router` (over a fresh extension registration on every render — which would discard editor state on every prop change).
- **Lazy URL list load:** chose load-on-first-open (over load-at-mount — saves bandwidth on docs the user never edits + on read-only views).

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Custom TipTap extension via `addProseMirrorPlugins` for click interception of shorthand hrefs without a custom Mark type":**

> When a feature needs to recognize + intercept clicks on a non-standard href shape (here: `#url/<urlId>` shorthand), the lowest-cost design is an Extension (not a new Mark) that:
> 1. Adds a ProseMirror plugin via `addProseMirrorPlugins` returning a `Plugin` with `props.handleClick`.
> 2. Reads the raw href via the DOM node's `getAttribute('href')` (NOT `link.href` — that property is browser-resolved to an absolute URL).
> 3. Filters by the shorthand prefix (or any other shape-test) via a pure helper imported from a shared `src/lib/` module.
> 4. Delegates the resolution to a consumer-supplied callback (e.g., `options.onInternalLinkClick(urlId)`).
>
> The existing Link mark is REUSED (just extend `Link.configure`'s `isAllowedUri` to accept the shorthand prefix — otherwise the Link mark's default URL-shape gatekeeper rejects the non-http href and silently strips the mark).
>
> A separate visual signal (CSS attribute selector + `::before` content) handles distinctness without a Mark-level type discriminator. Use the same href-prefix criterion the Extension uses so the styling + click interception don't drift apart.
>
> **WHEN TO USE:** any TipTap doc where you need to recognize + handle a custom href shape but the styling + storage shape of the Link mark are otherwise fine. **WHEN NOT TO USE:** if you need fundamentally different storage shape (e.g., extra attributes beyond href), use a custom Mark; if you need different schema position semantics (e.g., a Node not a Mark), use a Node.

This is the **third W4-arc Pattern**, paired with W4 S1's Pattern A "Per-Project edit-affordance parallel to per-row edit-affordance" + W4 S1's Pattern B "Editor-as-readonly substitutes for a separate static renderer." Together the three Patterns capture the W4 implementation arc's reusable design discoveries — (A) parallel-component shape across scopes, (B) editor-as-readonly substitution, (C) Extension-plus-CSS recognition of shorthand href patterns.

**§A frozen per Rule 18** — today's `#url/<urlId>` shorthand handling consumes §A.4's example syntax (which named the shorthand shape in §A) + §A.5's "custom TipTap extension" guidance (which named the extension shape in §A). No §A amendment.

**Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO** the entire session — no `prisma db push`; pure client-side TipTap extension on top of the W4 S1 editor wrapper + page route + components.

The walkthrough covers: navigation from Competition Data → standalone "→ Comprehensive Competitor Analysis" button → page loads → toggle edit mode → type body content + insert `#url/<urlId>` shorthand via the "Link to URL" toolbar dropdown → toggle Done → click the rendered hyperlink → confirm navigation → back-button returns to Comprehensive Analysis page → final edit-mode toggle. Session-end TaskList sweep: Tasks #1-#7 + #9 completed in-session. Task #8 (Fix-forward Phase-4 issues) deleted at the point director deferred Phase-4 — no fix-forwards happened. Task #10 `DEFERRED: P-46 W4 Phase-4 real-Chrome verification` created + closed in this same end-of-session pass after NEXT_SESSION.md + ROADMAP P-46 entry both received the Phase-4-verify destination capture per Rule 26. ZERO carry-over DEFERRED items leaving this session.

**NEW informational Pattern memorialized — "Truncated picker response → fire clarifying picker, don't silently interpret":**

When director's AskUserQuestion answer renders as a truncated string (e.g., missing the trailing word or character of an answer that's clearly mid-sentence), Claude must NOT silently interpret the truncated text. Instead, Claude fires a clarifying AskUserQuestion picker offering the most likely 3-4 disambiguation options + lets director pick. Today's session validated this Pattern: director's first AskUserQuestion answer rendered as "deploy now but defer any real wor" — Claude could have inferred "deploy now but defer any real work" (= ship the deploy + skip non-deploy work), but the inference space was actually larger (was "real work" Phase-4 verification? fix-forwards? next-session prep? unclear). Claude fired a 4-option clarifying picker; director picked the most-thorough deploy option which was effectively default Rule 9 behavior.

**Why this Pattern matters:** AskUserQuestion picker text is short-form by design (often single phrases or one-liners), so a truncated reply that drops a critical word can flip the meaning entirely (e.g., "deploy now AND defer X" vs. "deploy now BUT defer X" — opposite implications for X). Silent interpretation risks the wrong execution path. Clarifying picker overhead is small (~30 seconds) and the disambiguation has perfect fidelity since director picks the literal text.

**Pairs with Rule 14g** (trust director confirmation when explicit, but ambiguity → clarify rather than assume). **Could become a feedback memory if it recurs** — for now, informational only; one observation insufficient to confirm a recurring pattern.

**LOW informational — TWO P-43 cwd-leak reproductions on Check 5 during /scoreboard execution (multi-reproduction class; 5th+ reproduction overall):**

Both reproductions happened on Check 5 (Next.js `npm run build` route count) in pre-deploy AND post-merge /scoreboard runs. Root cause: parallel Checks 2+3 use `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub/extensions/competition-scraping && ...` which drifts the shell cwd to the extension directory; Check 5's template in `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` has a bare `npm run build` without an absolute-cd prefix, so it picked up the drifted cwd and ran the EXTENSION build (`Built extension in 3.697 s` / `757.27 kB`) instead of the Next.js build (no route table).

Caught immediately from output content (the extension build output is structurally different from the Next.js build output — no `Route (app)` table, different size formatting) + recovered with absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub && npm run build` retry. Final 62-routes result correct after retry.

**This is the 5th+ reproduction in this Pattern class.** Prior history: the 4-reproduction P-43 ship 2026-05-22-g + the 2026-05-23-b §B observation + the 2026-05-23-c P-43 LOW informational + the 2026-05-23-e P-43 fifth re-reproduction + the 2026-05-23-f P-43 sixth re-reproduction + the 2026-05-24 P-43 ~9-times re-reproduction across the W3 deploy session. Pattern stable.

**Mechanical prevention candidate (informational only; NOT shipped this session):** add absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` prefix to ALL Bash commands in `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (specifically Check 5's `npm run build` + route-count grep), not just the extension-rooted Checks 2-3. The prior P-43 template-hardening pass (2026-05-22-g) added absolute cd to Checks 2-3 (the extension-rooted checks that caused the drift) but did NOT add it to Check 5 (the Next.js root check that ABSORBS the drift). Today's reproduction shows the hardening should be bilateral — both the drift-causing checks AND the drift-absorbing checks need absolute paths to make the template robust against any reordering or parallelization changes.

**NOT a top-tier slip** — recovery was immediate + result was correct + no scoreboard or deploy fidelity was lost. Surface as an informational observation flagging that the prior P-43 template-hardening pass missed Check 5 specifically. Candidate for a future P-43-followup polish session, but not blocking any active workstream.

**Reviews modal clientId idempotency bug — opportunistic-polish-during-build-session NEW reusable Pattern:**

W2 Session 4 (2026-05-28) shipped `CapturedReviewAddModal.tsx` with `clientId: crypto.randomUUID()` inlined in `handleSubmit`, defeating the server-side P2002 dedup in `url-reviews.ts` on retries. Each Save click regenerated a new UUID, so the server saw each retry as a new submission rather than a duplicate of the prior failed Save. The server-side P2002 dedup was working correctly — the React component layer was the bug.

Surfaced today during a polish scan of the Reviews surface (originally framed as cosmetic UX polish targets — focus order / chip preview / etc.); a real correctness bug surfaced alongside the cosmetic candidates. **Prioritized the real bug over the cosmetic ones per `feedback_recommendation_style.md` most-thorough/reliable.**

Fix shape: hoisted `clientId` to a `useState` seeded per modal-open + reset on close. On first render of an opened modal, `useState(() => crypto.randomUUID())` initializes a fresh UUID; subsequent re-renders (including re-renders triggered by retry-after-error) reuse the same UUID; closing + reopening the modal triggers a `useEffect` reset that clears the UUID so the next open generates a fresh one. End-to-end: each modal-open session has exactly ONE clientId across all retry attempts, restoring the server-side P2002 dedup's correctness contract.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Opportunistic-polish-during-build-session — when scanning a related surface for polish, real bugs may surface alongside cosmetic candidates; prioritize real bugs":**

When a session's primary build scope is locked (today: P-46 W5 extension URL save form additions) but director permits opportunistic polish on related surfaces (today: scanning the Reviews surface for cosmetic UX targets), real correctness bugs may surface alongside the cosmetic candidates. The Pattern: prioritize real bugs over cosmetic improvements when both surface during opportunistic polish. Today's instance: scanning Reviews modal for "improve focus order" / "improve chip preview" / "improve placeholder text" cosmetic candidates surfaced the clientId regen-on-every-Save correctness bug as the highest-value target. Cosmetic candidates deferred to a future polish session; correctness bug fixed today.

**Why this Pattern matters:** Opportunistic polish is bounded-time work inside a primarily-scoped build session; the polish budget should go to the highest-value target surfaced during the polish scan. Correctness bugs are always higher value than cosmetic improvements (correctness regressions affect users immediately; cosmetic regressions accumulate gradually). The Pattern guides time-budget allocation when multiple polish candidates surface.

**Pairs with:** `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (most-thorough/reliable) — when ranking polish candidates surfaced opportunistically, recommend the most-thorough/reliable target = the correctness bug; defer cosmetic candidates to a future polish session where they're the primary scope.

## §Entry 2026-05-24-d — INFORMATIONAL — P-47 SHADOW DOM REFACTOR SESSION 1 CLOSING ENTRY — Content-script video-capture-form mount strategy refactor from `document.body` + 80-event-listener band-aid to open Shadow DOM root with `FORM_CHROME_CSS` injected inside the shadow + band-aid deletion lands at code level + NEW reusable Pattern "Shadow DOM mount as structural replacement for per-listener event-isolation band-aid" pairing with P-45 Build #2 §Entry 2026-05-22-i Pattern B as the long-term-fix pairing + LOW informational sub-observation on CSS extraction into shared exported constant + template-literal interpolation back into composite constant (single source of truth for both global stylesheet consumers + Shadow DOM root consumer)

**Session:** `session_2026-05-24-d_p47-shadow-dom-refactor-session-1`
**Branch:** `workflow-2-competition-scraping` (single-branch; no main push; build commit `d08f673` sits on workflow branch alongside the 2 prior W5 build commits `3c981be` + `41172f1` all awaiting the next bundled W5 + P-47 deploy session per (a.87))
**Build commits this session:** ONE — `d08f673` — W#2 polish P-47 Shadow DOM refactor Session 1 — replace 80-event-listener band-aid with Shadow DOM mount — 2 files +203/-220 (net -17 LOC)
**Director directive at session start:** *"please defer any real world testing that needs to be done by me and work on the next item otherwise road map for workflow#"* — same standing pattern as prior 2 sessions; resolved per `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md` without a re-confirmation picker since the launch-prompt task (P-47 Shadow DOM refactor Session 1, pure code) was already the recommended default and matched the directive
**Director directive mid-session:** *"please skip anything that requires me to do real world testing in this session"* — directly skipped P-47 Step 8 (empirical Chrome verification on Amazon); captured as DEFERRED task #6 + resolved by routing the verify to next session's bundled deploy Phase-4 step (which covers BOTH W5 URL-form additions AND P-47 Shadow DOM mount since both ship via the same extension form surface)
**Director directive at end-of-session:** picker outcome A (Bundled W5 + P-47 deploy session — Recommended) over (B) P-26 below-fold scroll capture Session 1 + (C) P-43 mechanical prevention small fix + (D) question first
**Status:** P-47 Session 1 ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL 2026-05-24-d. P-47 implementation arc progress: Session 1 of the original 2-3 estimate landed at code level; Session 2 (Cross-platform verification) MERGED into the next deploy session's Phase-4 since the Shadow DOM mount is structural-only with no cross-platform variance expected; Session 3 (DEPLOY) MERGED into the next bundled W5 + P-47 deploy session per (a.87). DEPLOY-PENDING. Phase-4 director real-Chrome verification on Amazon pending — bundles into the next deploy session's Phase-4 step (covers both W5 URL-form additions + P-47 Shadow DOM mount since both ship via the same extension form surface). All other workstream statuses unchanged: P-46 W1-W3 = ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED on vklf.com; W4 = ✅ DEPLOYED-PHASE-4-PENDING (W4 Phase-4 verify deferred a THIRD consecutive session); W5 = ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL — DEPLOY-PENDING.

**Headline outcome:**

Today's session was the P-47 Shadow DOM refactor Session 1 — pure-code structural refactor of the content-script video-capture-form mount strategy from the 80-event-listener band-aid shipped in P-45 Build #2 (2026-05-22-i, commit `ee8c79d`) to proper Shadow DOM isolation. Build commit `d08f673` (2 files +203/-220, net -17 LOC) landed the full refactor. Pre-build + post-refactor /scoreboard both 5/5 GREEN at unchanged baselines.

**What landed this session — empirical narrative:**

The content-script video-capture form (`extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/video-capture-form.ts`) now mounts inside an **open Shadow DOM root** attached to a fixed-positioned host `<div>` in `document.body`, rather than appending the backdrop directly into `document.body`. Events fired inside the shadow root do not surface to page-level handlers by default, so the per-input event-isolation band-aid (P-45 Build #2 2026-05-22-i, 20 events × 4 inputs = 80 listeners on each form open) is no longer needed and has been deleted.

**`styles.ts` changes:**

- New exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant containing the `.plos-cs-form-*` + `.plos-cs-chip-*` rules previously inlined in `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS`.
- `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS` now interpolates `${FORM_CHROME_CSS}`, so the other forms (image / text / url-add) that still mount to `document.body` continue to receive these rules via the host-page `<head>` stylesheet without drift between the two consumption sites.

**`video-capture-form.ts` changes:**

- Import `FORM_CHROME_CSS` from `./styles.ts`.
- Create host `<div>` with `position:fixed inset:0 z-index:999998` + `attachShadow({mode:'open'})`; inject `<style>FORM_CHROME_CSS</style>` inside the shadow root.
- Append backdrop inside the shadow root; append host to `document.body`.
- `destroy()` removes the host (parent of the shadow root) instead of the backdrop.
- `focusNewCategoryInput()` reads `shadow.activeElement` instead of `document.activeElement`, since `document.activeElement` returns the host element for any descendant focused inside the shadow.
- Delete `applyAggressiveEventIsolation` function (62 LOC including the 20-event-array `stopPropagation` loop + the click-handler force-focus with `queueMicrotask` retry) + its 4 call sites + its 20-line context comment.
- Escape-to-close `keydown` listener stays on `document` — keydown events from inside the shadow root compose up through the host into the document tree, so the listener catches them in both cases.

**NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Shadow DOM mount as structural replacement for per-listener event-isolation band-aid":**

When a per-listener band-aid (N events × M elements = N×M listeners on each component open) successfully isolates a component from page-level interference, the structural replacement is a Shadow DOM mount providing the same isolation at the boundary level with zero per-listener overhead.

**Today's instance:** P-45 Build #2 (2026-05-22-i) shipped an aggressive event-isolation band-aid (20 events × 4 inputs = 80 listeners on each video-capture-form open) to solve the page-level focus-stealing problem on Amazon. The band-aid worked empirically — verified on Amazon during P-45's Phase-4 verification — but carried hidden risk for any future video-form feature that needed to PROPAGATE events upward, and the per-listener overhead grew linearly with form-control count. P-47 Session 1 today replaced the band-aid with a Shadow DOM mount: events fired inside the shadow root do not surface to page-level handlers by default, so the isolation is at the boundary rather than per-listener.

**Pairs with P-45 Build #2 §Entry 2026-05-22-i Pattern B** (the band-aid memorialized) as the long-term-fix pairing. The band-aid + Shadow DOM mount form a complete pattern lifecycle:
- **Pattern B from P-45 Build #2 §Entry 2026-05-22-i** memorialized the band-aid: "aggressive page-event-isolation via per-listener stopPropagation across the full input event surface — viable as a localized late-stage fix when a structural refactor would balloon scope and the band-aid stays bounded to a small component surface".
- **Today's Pattern memorialized in this §Entry** memorializes the structural replacement: "when a per-listener band-aid successfully isolates a component from page-level interference, the structural replacement is a Shadow DOM mount providing the same isolation at the boundary level with zero per-listener overhead".

Together these two Patterns calibrate the band-aid → structural-fix arc: ship the band-aid first to unblock the immediate ship; capture the structural replacement as a polish-backlog entry; ship the structural replacement in a later session when scope permits. P-45 Build #2 → P-47 Session 1 is the canonical exemplar.

**Why this Pattern matters:** Many UI-isolation problems present first as "events from the host page interfere with the component"; the band-aid (per-listener stopPropagation across the full event surface of the component's input elements) solves the immediate problem but doesn't generalize — every new input or event type added later must be added to the band-aid arrays manually, and the overhead grows linearly. Shadow DOM mount provides the same isolation at the boundary level — events composed inside the shadow root don't surface to the page unless explicitly bubbled through with `composed: true`. The structural replacement is cheaper at runtime AND less brittle at code-level.

**LOW informational sub-observation — "CSS extraction into shared exported constant + template-literal interpolation back into composite constant":**

When refactoring `styles.ts` to inject CSS rules inside the Shadow DOM root (which doesn't inherit page styles), the natural approach was to duplicate the `.plos-cs-form-*` rules inline inside `video-capture-form.ts`. That would have introduced drift risk: any future change to a form-chrome CSS rule would have to be made in two places (the page-level stylesheet for the image / text / url-add forms that still mount to `document.body`, and the inline copy inside `video-capture-form.ts` for the shadow root).

**The most-thorough/reliable approach per `feedback_recommendation_style.md`:** extract the form-chrome rules to a new exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant; re-interpolate that constant back into `CONTENT_SCRIPT_CSS` via `${FORM_CHROME_CSS}` so the global stylesheet consumers continue to receive these rules via the host-page `<head>` stylesheet; import `FORM_CHROME_CSS` into `video-capture-form.ts` and inject it via a `<style>` tag inside the shadow root.

**The Pattern:** **single source of truth via template-literal interpolation back into composite constant** — both consumption sites (global stylesheet + Shadow DOM root) consume the same exported `FORM_CHROME_CSS` constant; any future change to a form-chrome CSS rule lands in one place and propagates to both sites without drift.

**Why this is informational not a full Pattern:** the technique is a standard refactor pattern (extract-shared-constant + interpolate-back) rather than a novel insight; captured as a sub-observation under the main P-47 entry rather than a standalone reusable Pattern. The Pattern memorialization is for the Shadow DOM mount as structural replacement, not for this CSS extraction.

**Schema change** — no; pure content-script DOM mount refactor; no schema, no API, no shared-types. Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO entire session.

**Per Rule 23 Change Impact Audit:** STRUCTURAL REFACTOR + EXTENSION-ONLY (DOM mount strategy change inside the content script + CSS rule re-injection inside shadow root + deletion of 80-event-listener band-aid; no new schema; no new routes; no new dependencies; no new tests since the refactor is structural-only with no new units of behaviour to cover at the node:test layer). No data risk to existing rows. Zero downstream W#1 cross-tool impact. Form-mount lifecycle isn't unit-tested today; coverage will land if/when this proves useful as a regression bell.

### NEW reusable Pattern memorialized — "Bundled-build-commit deploy under ONE Rule 9 gate"

**Shape:** When N build commits sit on a workflow branch awaiting deploy + each commit already passed /scoreboard GREEN at its own session + all commits are additive + no inter-commit dependencies require fix-forward sequencing, the most-thorough/reliable choice is to ff-merge ALL N commits behind ONE Rule 9 gate rather than splitting into N separate deploy sessions.

**Why it works:**
- (a) Each build commit already passed individual session /scoreboards GREEN — the bundle's combined /scoreboard pre-deploy is operationally equivalent to N sequential individual /scoreboards.
- (b) ff-merge to main is fast-forward only — no merge conflicts possible since the commits are linear on the workflow branch.
- (c) Vercel auto-redeploy fires ONCE per main push regardless of how many commits in the push — so N separate deploy sessions would each pay the ~2-3 minute Vercel build + cache invalidation cost; bundling pays it once.
- (d) Rule 9 deploy gate fires ONCE — director approval cost is constant regardless of N.
- (e) Phase-4 verification can pair walkthroughs from sibling commits when they share the same UI surface (today: W5 + P-47 both ship via the extension form surface — Phase-4 covers both walkthroughs in one session at next-session).

**When to use:**
- N build commits already at ✅ DONE-AT-CODE-LEVEL with /scoreboard GREEN at individual sessions.
- All commits additive (no schema, no breaking changes).
- No inter-commit dependencies requiring fix-forward sequencing.
- Phase-4 verification walkthroughs can be paired OR deferred together.

**When NOT to use:**
- Any commit has fix-forward dependency on another commit (must sequence individually).
- Any commit involves schema-change-in-flight transition (deploy each schema commit individually for cleaner rollback).
- Phase-4 verification needs intermediate sign-off between commits (defeat the bundling purpose).

**Exemplars:**
- **First exemplar — W2 deploy 2026-05-23-c** — carried 5 build sessions + Workstream 1 schema commits as one fast-forward. But single-workstream (all P-46 W2).
- **Second exemplar — today's bundled W5 + P-47 deploy 2026-05-24-e** — first CROSS-WORKSTREAM bundled deploy in W#2 history (P-46 W5 + P-46 Reviews polish + P-47 Shadow DOM are 3 distinct polish items shipped together; P-46 + P-47 are different ROADMAP polish entries).

**Pairs with:**
- §Entry 2026-05-26 W4 deploy Pattern — single Rule 9 gate + Phase-4 deferred — same shape as today's deploy + Phase-4 deferral.
- §Entry 2026-05-24 W3 deploy Pattern — opposite end of the spectrum — 6 fix-forwards in one session demonstrating that when issues surface at Phase-4, fix-forward in-session rather than deferring; today's deferred-Phase-4 means no fix-forward cascade possible this session.

## §Entry 2026-05-24-f — INFORMATIONAL — BUNDLED PHASE-4 VERIFICATION + FIX-FORWARD #1 + PHASE-4 PASS CLOSING ENTRY — P-46 W4 + W5 + P-47 ALL ✅ DONE-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-24-f end-to-end on vklf.com via fix-forward #1 `5ed754e..d38b036` (1 commit; 5 files +292/-94 UI-only) resolving 7 director-observed Phase-4 issues on the W4 page + W5 + P-47 walkthroughs both PASS first-walk on Amazon + NEW reusable Pattern "Bundled Phase-4 verification surfaces issues concentrated in the most-complex newest surface; subsequent surfaces tend to PASS first-walk" + NEW reusable Pattern "Editor save-on-Done race condition (debounced save + unmount cleanup)" + LOW informational sub-observation (UX directive surfaced 3+ months after styling shipped) + NEW P-48 capture per Rule 24 search + calibration data point validating fix-forward-in-session criterion

Today's session demonstrates the alternate branch of the W4 deploy 2026-05-26 Pattern: when Phase-4 happens, fix-forward in-session resolves cleanly when issues match the UI-only + scoped criteria.

**What happened — sub-observation (f) NEW reusable Pattern "Editor save-on-Done race condition (debounced save + unmount cleanup)":**

Issue 5 from today's W4 Phase-4 walkthrough (Done button doesn't save) root-caused to a two-layer bug:

(1) **Layer 1 — RichTextEditor cleanup CLEARS the pending 500ms debounce timer WITHOUT flushing.** When `AnalysisEditor` unmounts on mode='edit'→'read', `RichTextEditor`'s cleanup effect runs; cleanup CLEARED `pendingTimerRef.current` but did NOT call the save callback with the pending content. The 500ms debounce window typically spans the click-Done event, so the latest content was always in `pendingContentRef` when cleanup fired. Result: any content typed within the last 500ms before Done was discarded.

(2) **Layer 2 — page.tsx's `loadState` was frozen at mount.** Even if the save HAD persisted (e.g., a 600ms delay between last keystroke and Done click), page.tsx's `loadState.contentJson` was never refreshed after a successful PUT. So when AnalysisEditor unmounted + AnalysisReadView mounted, the read view rendered from the stale mount-time loadState, showing pre-edit content even when the save succeeded.

**Two-layer fix:** (i) RichTextEditor cleanup flushes `pendingContent` through `onChangeRef` before clearing the timer; (ii) AnalysisEditor exposes `onSaved` callback fired after a successful PUT; page.tsx wires the callback to update `loadState.contentJson` + `loadState.lastEditedAt` so read view shows fresh content.

**Pattern:** ANY debounced-save editor with a parent-controlled mount lifecycle (Edit/Done toggle, modal open/close, accordion expand/collapse) must (a) flush on unmount cleanup AND (b) propagate save outcomes back to parent state OR re-fetch from server before unmount.

Cross-references PerItemAnalysisBox + OverallAnalysisBox (W2 Sessions 1 + 3) which share the same `RichTextEditor` unmount path — the Layer 1 unmount-flush fix benefits them too (any rapid Save → modal-close interaction in those surfaces was vulnerable to the same race). Their parent consumers (CapturedTextSubsection / CapturedImageSubsection / CapturedVideoSubsection / CapturedReviewsSection etc.) have different state-management shapes so the Layer 2 onSaved-callback wiring is W4-specific (W2 sessions used a different state-shape that didn't have the freeze-at-mount bug).

Memorialize for future editor-lifecycle work.

## §Entry 2026-05-25 — INFORMATIONAL — P-48 SESSION 1 (DIAGNOSTIC) CLOSING ENTRY — Screen-recording playback stutter root cause locked via `ffprobe` + EBML inspection of 3 SCREEN_RECORDING webm files in production + `curl` HEAD/Range tests of Supabase Storage delivery — missing WebM duration metadata in MediaRecorder-produced container headers; pre-Session-1 capture-side-bitrate hypothesis empirically FALSIFIED + file path drift corrected; Session 2 fix path locked = `fix-webm-duration` npm dep + ~10 LOC in `record-controller.ts` + NEW reusable Pattern "ffprobe-first diagnostic reveals hypothesis-bust before any code lands — empirical inspection of artifacts in their actual production state preempts speculative implementation" + MEDIUM informational sub-observation on enum-coverage query-bug false-alarm + calibration data point + diagnostic-script housekeeping note (6 throwaway scripts deleted at session end)

**Type of finding:** (a) calibration data point + (b) NEW reusable Pattern + (c) sub-observation with mechanical prevention candidate + (d) calibration data point + (e) operational housekeeping.

**Severity:** informational (no slip-of-this-session; today's session executed exactly the diagnostic shape called for in (a.89) RECOMMENDED-NEXT; the hypothesis-bust + file-path-drift correction are healthy outcomes of the diagnostic protocol).

**When:** `session_2026-05-25_p48-session-1-diagnostic`; pure diagnostic session on `workflow-2-competition-scraping`; no code commit; one push planned at end-of-session (doc-batch) + one ping-pong ff-merge push (operationally adjacent; no Rule 9 gate).

**Where:** Diagnostic surfaces — (a) Supabase Storage `competition-scraping-videos` bucket signed-URL `GET` + `curl -I --range` HEAD checks; (b) downloaded webm Blobs piped through `ffprobe` for format + duration + bitrate inspection; (c) deep EBML inspector via `ffprobe -show_format -of json` confirming `duration: NOPTS`; (d) source code reads on `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/screen-recording/record-controller.ts` (line 42 `VIDEO_BITS_PER_SECOND_DEFAULT = 2_500_000` constant + line 455-458 MediaRecorder construction site).

**What happened — sub-observation (a) P-48 hypothesis empirically falsified — capture-side bitrate cap already shipped; root cause is missing WebM duration metadata:**

The pre-Session-1 working hypothesis captured in the P-48 ROADMAP entry (NEW 2026-05-24-f) read: *"`MediaRecorder` is currently constructed with NO `videoBitsPerSecond` cap, so it defaults to the browser's chosen bitrate (typically 6-8 Mbps+ at 1080p for screen content). The resulting `.webm` files are large + exceed real-time decode budget on the playback device, producing stutters / frame drops during playback."* This was a plausible-sounding hypothesis based on the symptom + the canonical MediaRecorder default-bitrate concern; but it was a guess, not an empirical finding.

Session 1 ran the empirical inspection per the (a.89) RECOMMENDED-NEXT diagnostic protocol:

1. **Read `record-controller.ts:42`** — found `export const VIDEO_BITS_PER_SECOND_DEFAULT = 2_500_000;` (2.5 Mbps cap, exactly what the busted hypothesis said Session 2 needed to add). Read `record-controller.ts:455-458` — found the MediaRecorder constructor already passes the cap. **Hypothesis 50%-busted at this point** — cap was already shipped.

2. **Queried the production DB for recent SCREEN_RECORDING captures** — initial query `where: { sourceType: 'DIRECT_BYTES' }` returned ZERO rows. Reported "no webm rows in DB" to director. Director immediately corrected: *"I've definitely created P-45 screen recordings; they're missing from the DB."* Re-queried `where: { sourceType: { in: ['DIRECT_BYTES', 'URL_REFERENCE', 'SCREEN_RECORDING'] } }` (all 3 enum values) → found 3 SCREEN_RECORDING rows. The initial query filtered on a 2-of-3 enum value and missed the very category we were investigating. (See sub-observation (c) below for the prevention candidate.)

3. **Generated signed URLs for the 3 webm files + curl-HEAD-tested Supabase Storage delivery:**
   - File 1: 0.50 Mbps actual encoded bitrate. `accept-ranges: bytes`. 206 Partial Content on `--range 0-1000000`. Cloudflare edge cache HIT on second request. 124 Mbps throughput Codespace → Cloudflare → file.
   - File 2: 1.35 Mbps actual encoded bitrate. Same delivery characteristics.
   - File 3: 0.67 Mbps actual encoded bitrate. Same delivery characteristics.
   - Capture-side and delivery-side BOTH ruled out empirically. **Hypothesis fully busted at this point.**

4. **Ran `ffprobe` against the downloaded webm Blobs** — every file reported `Duration: N/A` in the format header. Ran `ffprobe -show_format -of json` for the EBML deep-probe — every file's `format.duration` field was `NOPTS` (no presentation timestamp).

5. **Root cause locked:** missing WebM duration metadata in MediaRecorder-produced container headers. This is the canonical MediaRecorder limitation documented in MDN + WebRTC samples + cross-browser bug trackers — the EBML header is written at recording-START before duration is known, and the browser never patches it after `stop()`. The HTML5 video player on vklf.com therefore sees `videoElement.duration === Infinity` on load, which breaks its pre-buffer-planning math (the player can't compute a meaningful pre-buffer target without knowing total duration); the fallback is reactive chunk-fetching which surfaces as visible stutter.

6. **Session 2 fix path locked:** add `fix-webm-duration` npm package as runtime dep in the extension (tiny ~3 KB library — https://github.com/yusitnikov/fix-webm-duration is the canonical implementation). In `record-controller.ts`, after recorder fires `stop` but before `onStopped` emits, `await fixWebmDuration(blob, durationMs)` to inject the JS-known duration into the EBML Segment Info Duration tag, then emit the patched Blob. ~10 LOC in `record-controller.ts` + a few new unit-test cases in `record-controller.test.ts` confirming the patch is applied. ONLY new recordings benefit; the 3 existing webm files in DB keep their broken metadata.

7. **File path drift corrected:** the pre-Session-1 ROADMAP entry referenced `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/screen-recorder.ts`. The actual file is `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/screen-recording/record-controller.ts`. The drift was caused by Claude's pre-Session-1 description being based on the conceptual "where MediaRecorder lives" rather than a verified file-read. ROADMAP entry corrected in this end-of-session doc-batch.

**What happened — sub-observation (b) NEW reusable Pattern "ffprobe-first diagnostic reveals hypothesis-bust before any code lands — empirical inspection of artifacts in their actual production state preempts speculative implementation":**

**Pattern: ffprobe-first diagnostic reveals hypothesis-bust before any code lands.**

**Trigger criteria:** A polish item or bug-fix item has a working hypothesis based on plausible reasoning but no empirical inspection of the actual production artifact. The artifact is available in a queryable production store (DB row / Storage object / log line / cache entry) and a cheap probe exists (`ffprobe` for media; `curl -I --range` for HTTP delivery; `psql EXPLAIN` for DB query plans; `jq .` for JSON shape; `tree` for filesystem layout).

**When to apply:** ALWAYS, BEFORE drafting Session-2-style implementation scope. The diagnostic session should produce empirical evidence that EITHER confirms the hypothesis (proceed to implementation as scoped) OR falsifies it (re-scope implementation to match the empirical root cause).

**Concrete steps:**

1. Read the source code where the artifact is produced — verify the implementation actually does what the hypothesis assumes it does (today's example: `record-controller.ts:42` already had the bitrate cap shipped, so the "uncapped MediaRecorder" hypothesis was already half-busted at step 1).

2. Query the production store for ALL artifacts in the relevant category — when the discriminator is an enum, query ALL enum values OR use `groupBy by <enum-field>` to surface coverage upfront (see sub-observation (c) below for the prevention candidate after today's near-miss on this step).

3. Run the cheapest available probe against each artifact — output a small table summarizing the probe findings.

4. Cross-check delivery layer if the artifact is consumed via network — `curl -I --range` for HTTP, `dig` for DNS, etc.

5. Lock the root cause empirically. If it matches the hypothesis, Session 2 implementation proceeds as originally scoped. If it doesn't, RE-SCOPE Session 2 to match the empirical root cause (today: from capture-side bitrate cap to container-metadata patch).

**Why this pairs with `feedback_recommendation_style.md` most-thorough/reliable:** empirical inspection IS the most-reliable diagnostic. Speculative implementation based on plausible reasoning saves time IFF the speculation is correct; today's example demonstrates the cost when speculation is wrong (Session 2 would have shipped ~30 LOC adding a bitrate cap that's already shipped, and the stutter would have persisted because the real root cause is elsewhere).

**Cross-references:**
- `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (most-thorough/reliable — today's diagnostic embodies this principle).
- §Entry 2026-05-22-i Pattern B (P-45 Build #2 band-aid memorialization — different Pattern shape but same diagnostic-rigor philosophy: the band-aid was shipped only AFTER empirical demonstration that the page-level event interference was real on Amazon).
- §Entry 2026-05-24-f sub-observation (b) "Bundled Phase-4 verification surfaces issues concentrated in the most-complex newest surface" (different Pattern but same calibration-from-empirical-observation philosophy).

**What happened — sub-observation (c) MEDIUM informational — query-bug false-alarm where filtering on a 2-of-3 enum value created a false "data integrity bug" report to director:**

During Session 1 step 2 (querying production DB for recent SCREEN_RECORDING captures), Claude's initial query was `where: { sourceType: 'DIRECT_BYTES' }`. This filter excluded the `SCREEN_RECORDING` enum value (the very category being investigated for P-48). The query returned ZERO rows, and Claude reported to director: "no webm rows in DB — looks like screen recordings aren't being saved." Director immediately corrected: *"I've definitely created P-45 screen recordings; they're missing from the DB."* Re-querying with `sourceType: { in: ['DIRECT_BYTES', 'URL_REFERENCE', 'SCREEN_RECORDING'] }` returned 3 rows immediately.

The false-alarm report was caused by Claude not checking the enum's full value set before filtering. The `sourceType` discriminator in the schema has 3 values; Claude's mental model only retained the first 2 from a prior session's context.

**Mechanical prevention candidate (informational only; NOT shipped this session):** when querying tables with multi-value discriminator enums, query ALL values OR use `groupBy by <enum-field>` to surface enum coverage upfront BEFORE drilling into a subset. Specifically:

```typescript
// PREFER (surfaces enum coverage upfront):
const counts = await prisma.captured.groupBy({ by: ['sourceType'], _count: true });
// THEN drill into the subset:
const rows = await prisma.captured.findMany({ where: { sourceType: 'SCREEN_RECORDING' }, ... });
```

```typescript
// AVOID (silently filters out enum values not in your mental model):
const rows = await prisma.captured.findMany({ where: { sourceType: 'DIRECT_BYTES' }, ... });
```

**Severity:** MEDIUM informational. Not a top-tier slip because (a) director caught it within one round-trip, (b) re-query was immediate, (c) no data loss or schema change involved, (d) diagnostic continued cleanly afterwards. But the false-alarm is high-cost in director attention — a "data integrity bug" report is the kind of thing director takes seriously, so creating one falsely consumes director trust.

**Pairs with §Entry 2026-05-24-c sub-observation (a)** (§C.5 file-list inconsistency — `UrlAddForm.tsx` referenced but file doesn't exist) — same Pattern class of "stale or partial mental model of the production state surfaces as a false report; cheap empirical verification preempts the false report."

**What happened — sub-observation (d) calibration data point — Session 1 ran ~45 min in-session (under the 30-60 min budget):**

Session 1 budget was 30-60 min per the (a.89) NEXT_SESSION.md launch-prompt estimate. Actual in-session time was ~45 min broken down as: pre-build reads ~5 min + branch state verify ~2 min + initial DB query + (false alarm + recovery) ~8 min + re-query + ffprobe inspection on 3 files ~15 min + curl HEAD/Range tests + EBML deep-probe ~10 min + diagnosis paragraph drafting + ROADMAP entry redraft + this §Entry drafting ~10 min (end-of-session doc-batch overhead not counted in Session 1 budget per convention).

**Calibration:** the budget held. Director answered 2 Rule 14f pickers in real-time (the capture-flow scoping picker after the false alarm + the end-of-session next-session picker shape). Four signed-URL + ffprobe + curl/HEAD lookups + 1 EBML deep-probe needed to lock the diagnosis. For future ffprobe-first diagnostic sessions of similar shape, ~45-60 min in-session is the right budget when N=2-3 artifacts need probing.

**What happened — sub-observation (e) operational housekeeping — 6 throwaway diagnostic scripts deleted at session end:**

During Session 1, 6 throwaway diagnostic scripts were created in `scripts/`:
- `p48-list-recent-videos.mjs` — initial DB query (had the enum-value bug).
- `p48-find-screen-recs.mjs` — corrected DB query (the SCREEN_RECORDING-inclusive re-query).
- `p48-find-screen-recs-v2.mjs` — slight refinement adding signed-URL output.
- `p48-get-signed-url.mjs` — Supabase Storage signed-URL generator for one file at a time.
- `p48-dl-webm.mjs` — downloader fetching the signed-URL into a local tmp file for ffprobe.
- `p48-sign-webm.mjs` — alternate signed-URL generator with curl test included.

All 6 scripts were DELETED at session end. No diagnostic-script residue is checked into the repo. Rationale: these scripts were one-shot operational tools, not reusable diagnostic infrastructure. They referenced credentials + Supabase project IDs that would create maintenance overhead if kept around. The DB queries + ffprobe invocations + curl HEAD/Range syntax used are documented for posterity in this §Entry so a future session that wants to re-run can recreate the diagnostic scripts trivially (~10 min reconstruction; cheaper than maintaining the throwaway scripts).

**Future calibration:** for ffprobe-first diagnostic sessions, prefer inline `Bash` commands or shell heredocs over standalone script files when the diagnostic is one-shot. Reserve script files for diagnostic infrastructure that will be re-run (e.g., a `scripts/diagnose-webm-duration.mjs` if the duration-patch fix surfaces a regression and we need to re-probe).
