## §Entry 2026-05-28 — INFORMATIONAL — P-49 W2 AMAZON DEPLOY + 4-FIX-FORWARD CASCADE + PHASE-4 PASS CLOSING ENTRY — First production deploy of the Reviews Phase 2 implementation arc; Sessions 1 + 2 + intervening doc-batches all ff-merged to main under ONE Rule 9 deploy gate as the initial deploy + 3 fix-forward commits (FF#1 standalone + FF#2+FF#3 bundled + FF#4 standalone) shipped under 3 additional Rule 9 deploy gates within ONE Phase 4 verification session; final director PASS verdict after FF#4 ("It worked, all reviews scraped past 10"); RESOLVES the Sessions 1 + 2 standing carry-over (first end-to-end real-Chrome extraction test on a real Amazon product page); THREE NEW reusable Patterns memorialized — "Phase 4 verification fix-forward cascade scales beyond N=5 when each issue is scoped + reversible + UI-only" + "FF#1 dispatch over-restriction antipattern at per-platform extractor layer" + "Findnextpage-link-selector empirically falsified by Amazon UI change → pageNumber-direct-increment is the more robust replacement" + LOW informational P-43 cwd-leak Pattern Class reproduction (Nth in Pattern class; strong empirical signal for the mechanical prevention fix) + calibration data point (9/9 Rule 14f forced-pickers director-Yes to Recommended = 100%; reinforces 14/15 = 93.3% from 2026-05-25-b design session)

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — first production deploy of P-49 + first 4-fix-forward cascade within ONE deploy session in W#2 history + 3 NEW reusable Patterns memorialized + Sessions 1 + 2 standing carry-over RESOLVED via Phase 4 PASS verdict. No top-tier corrections-tier slip this session.

**(a) DEPLOY outcome — Amazon ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED 2026-05-28 end-to-end on vklf.com after a 4-fix-forward cascade.** Total session shape:

1. **Pre-deploy /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN at expected baselines** (root tsc clean / extension tsc clean / **633 ext matches Session 2 baseline** / **786 src/lib UNCHANGED** / **62 routes UNCHANGED**); Check 6 Playwright SKIPPED per Rule 27 picker (Rule 14f forced-picker: SKIP Recommended vs RUN; director picked SKIP).
2. **Initial deploy ff-merge `1914171..0ef8340`** carrying 4 commits (Session 1 build `422436f` + Session 1 doc-batch `1323f9a` + Session 2 build `1830074` + Session 2 doc-batch `0ef8340`) under Rule 9 deploy gate #1 (Rule 14f forced-picker: Deploy now Recommended; director-Yes); push to `origin/main`; Vercel auto-redeploy fired ~2-3 min cycle; ping-pong push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`; **Schema-change-in-flight flag FLIPPED YES → NO** at this push completion (canonical schema-change-ships-to-production transition).
3. **Fresh extension zip via `npm run zip`** — `plos-extension-2026-05-28-w2-deploy-37.zip` (208K) at repo root.
4. **Phase 4 mode picker per `feedback_playwright_for_repeatable_walkthroughs.md`** — Manual walkthrough vs Draft Playwright spec vs Skip; director picked Manual walkthrough Recommended (5+ step manual walkthrough on a major new feature). Director loaded the fresh extension unpacked in Chrome, opened a real Amazon product `/dp/<ASIN>` page with multi-star reviews, right-clicked → "Scrape reviews for this URL".
5. **Phase 4 verification round 1 surfaced ISSUE #1:** right-click on `/dp/` product page rejected with "Review scraping is currently available on Amazon product-review pages only." Root cause: dispatch check at `orchestrator.ts:1163` (`isAmazonReviewPage(url)`) only accepted `/product-reviews/<ASIN>/` URLs. Per Session 2's cross-star refactor, ALL pages are fetched via fetch+DOMParser; the starting page is just an ASIN source — the dispatch should accept ANY Amazon URL exposing the ASIN.
6. **FF#1 fix-forward shape picker** — Extend dispatch to accept `/dp/` URLs (Recommended) vs No-code-change update user instructions vs Defer fix-forward. Director picked Recommended (extend dispatch). **FF#1 build commit `8bc2e7e`** (3 files +190/-16; +18 ext tests) added `isAmazonProductPage` / `isAmazonScrapableUrl` / `extractAsinFromProductUrl` / `extractAsinFromAmazonUrl` symmetric helpers + updated dispatch + extended `urlsMatchByAsin` to accept `/dp/` in first arg. **Post-FF#1 /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN at new ext baseline**: extension `npm test` = **651/651 (+18 from 633 baseline)** — exact match with the 18 new test cases (5 + 5 + 3 + 3 + 2).
7. **FF#1 Rule 9 deploy gate #2** — Deploy fix-forward #1 now vs Defer; director picked Deploy Recommended. Deploy ff-merge `0ef8340..8bc2e7e`; push to `origin/main`; Vercel auto-redeploy; ping-pong push to `origin/workflow-2-competition-scraping`.
8. **Phase 4 re-verification round 2 surfaced 3 MORE issues:** ISSUE #2 — trigger modal lacks per-star granularity (director couldn't pick which stars to scrape; only a single capPerStar input visible); ISSUE #3 — no way to see reviews of specific star counts on vklf.com Captured Reviews section (manifested AFTER the scrape populated the corpus); ISSUE #4 — "all reviews per star end at 10" (each star filter only yielded 10 reviews max despite the cap being set higher).
9. **Phase 4 issue triage picker after FF#1** — FF#2 + FF#3 + DEFER issue #3 to W4 (Recommended) vs FF#2-only-defer-rest vs Pull W4 partial forward (most aggressive) vs Defer all three. Director picked Recommended (FF#2 + FF#3 + defer issue #3 to W4). Triage rationale: issue #3 is the canonical W4 Captured Reviews UI extensions scope per §A.14 (star-count counter-bar with click-to-filter); deferring it to W4 Session 1 is more thorough than a quick fix-forward; issue #4 is a pagination bug requiring its own diagnosis pass (becomes FF#4 after).
10. **FF#2+#3 bundled fix-forward** — FF#2 trigger modal per-star checkboxes (added 5 star checkboxes + new `starFilterForRating` helper); FF#3 progress indicator per-star breakdown (replaced "0 reviews then 46 reviews" with per-star breakdown). Bundled into ONE commit + ONE deploy since operationally adjacent + ship to the same UI surface. **FF#2+#3 build commit `b55cdbd`** (6 files +274/-8; +4 ext tests covering `starFilterForRating`). **Post-FF#2+#3 /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN at new ext baseline**: extension `npm test` = **655/655 (+4 from 651 baseline)** — exact match.
11. **FF#2+#3 Rule 9 deploy gate #3** — Deploy bundled (Recommended) vs Split into 2 deploys vs Defer FF#3 vs Question first; director picked Deploy bundled Recommended. Deploy ff-merge `8bc2e7e..b55cdbd`; push to `origin/main`; Vercel auto-redeploy; ping-pong push.
12. **Phase 4 re-verification round 2b — director re-tested the per-star scrape with FF#2+#3 deployed.** ISSUE #4 still surfaced empirically (all reviews per star ended at exactly 10).
13. **FF#4 diagnosis** — `findNextPageUrl()` looked for the OLD `<li.a-last><a>` next-link CSS selector which Amazon no longer renders (Amazon switched to "Show 10 more reviews" button instead of numbered pagination on at least some product views).
14. **FF#4 fix-forward shape picker** — pageNumber-increment pagination (Recommended) vs extend `findNextPageUrl` selector vs Defer FF#4. Director picked Recommended (pageNumber-increment). Rationale: pageNumber-direct-increment doesn't depend on Amazon's UI; works regardless of numbered links / Show-more button / AJAX; the URL parameter contract `?pageNumber=N` has been stable for years; this is the more robust replacement.
15. **FF#4 build commit `f6944db`** (1 file +23/-10; 0 new tests — integration-level deferred to Playwright per Rule 27) — replaced `findNextPageUrl` call in `scrapeOneStar`'s `advanceToNextPage` with direct pageNumber increment via `buildAmazonStarFilterUrl(asin, filter, N+1)`; stop signal = fetched page has 0 reviews.
16. **FF#4 Rule 9 deploy gate #4** — bundled into the shape picker by inference (director's "Recommended" answer chose both the shape + the immediate deploy). Deploy ff-merge `b55cdbd..f6944db`; push to `origin/main`; Vercel auto-redeploy; ping-pong push. **Post-FF#4 /scoreboard 5/5 GREEN at unchanged baselines**: extension `npm test` = **655/655 UNCHANGED**.
17. **Phase 4 final verification — director PASS verdict** ("It worked, all reviews scraped past 10"). **NEW baseline locked: extension `npm test` = 655/655** (+22 cumulative from the 633 entry baseline).

Total in-Claude time well over budget — the Phase 4 fix-forward cascade dominated the session.

**(b) NEW reusable Pattern: "Phase 4 verification fix-forward cascade scales beyond N=5 when each issue is scoped + reversible + UI-only."** Today shipped 4 fix-forwards (3 commits since FF#2+FF#3 bundled) under separate Rule 9 deploy gates within ONE Phase 4 verification session. Each fix targeted a single empirically-surfaced issue (dispatch / modal granularity / progress messaging / pagination); each was scoped to a small file set (max 6 files in FF#2+#3); each was UI-only or routing-only (no schema work, no API contract changes); each was reversible via standard ff-merge revert (none touch destructive operations). The 4-fix cascade landed cleanly without compounding side effects. **Pairs with + extends the P-46 W3 2026-05-24-f "Phase-4 verification fix-forward cascade in a single deploy session" Pattern (which set the precedent at N=5)** — that 2026-05-24-f session shipped 5 fix-forward UI commits resolving 7 director-observed issues on the W4 page. Today's session shipped 4 fix-forwards (3 commits) resolving 4 director-observed issues + 1 W4 destination capture. **Generalization observation:** the upper bound on fix-forwards-per-Phase-4-session is not a hard number; it's the conjunction of (a) each issue having a scoped + reversible + UI-only fix shape, (b) the test infrastructure catching regressions before re-deploy (today's /scoreboard runs at each FF caught nothing — all green), (c) the director-Yes rate on Rule 14f deploy gate pickers (today's 4-for-4). When any of those conditions degrades, the right call shifts to "defer further fix-forwards to a later session." 4-of-4 fix-forwards landed PASS on re-verification with no further regressions surfaced. **Future Phase 4 sessions on major new features should expect 1-5 fix-forwards within the same deploy session as the canonical pattern**, not single-fix-forward sessions.

**(c) NEW reusable Pattern: "FF#1 dispatch over-restriction antipattern at the per-platform extractor layer."** Session 2's dispatch check `isAmazonReviewPage(url)` only accepted `/product-reviews/<ASIN>/` URLs. Per the Session 2 cross-star refactor (everything fetched via fetch+DOMParser), the starting page is JUST an ASIN source — the dispatch should accept ANY Amazon URL exposing the ASIN. **The design intent and the code drifted apart between Session 1's "live-DOM rooted" approach and Session 2's "all-fetch-based" approach**; the dispatch check wasn't updated when Session 2 generalized the pagination. The fix-forward shape was simple: introduce a SYMMETRIC pair of helpers (`isXxxScrapableUrl` for the dispatch check; `extractAsinFromXxxUrl` for the ASIN extraction) that handles both `/product-reviews/<ASIN>/` AND `/dp/<ASIN>` URL shapes. **Lesson:** when refactoring per-platform infrastructure that changes the relationship between the trigger page and the data fetched, audit the dispatch check at the same time. **Future per-platform sub-cluster sessions (eBay / Etsy / Walmart) should adopt the `isXxxScrapableUrl` + `extractAsinFromXxxUrl` symmetric helper Pattern from FF#1 from the start** — each platform's dispatch should accept ALL valid product/listing/review URLs exposing the canonical item identifier (ASIN for Amazon; ItemID for eBay; ListingID for Etsy; ItemID for Walmart). Reduces the likelihood of repeating this antipattern at each platform's first deploy session. Pairs with the §Entry 2026-05-27 sub-observation (b) "Build-session end-of-session push pattern" Pattern — both are session-pairing Patterns within the same arc (Session 1 + Session 2 + Deploy session) that surface when subsequent sessions don't fully audit the prior session's refactor footprint.

**(d) NEW reusable Pattern: "Findnextpage-link-selector empirically falsified by Amazon UI change → pageNumber-direct-increment is the more robust replacement."** FF#4 replaced `findNextPageUrl` with direct pageNumber URL construction. `findNextPageUrl` looked for `<li.a-last><a>` next-link CSS selector — a DOM-link-scraping approach that depends on Amazon rendering numbered pagination. Amazon empirically does not render that selector on the product-reviews page anymore (at least for some products; possibly all); the new UI is a "Show 10 more reviews" button. The fix-forward replacement: `buildAmazonStarFilterUrl(asin, filter, N+1)` constructs the next-page URL directly from the ASIN + filter + page number; stop signal = fetched page has 0 reviews. **Lesson:** when the underlying URL parameter contract is stable (Amazon's `?pageNumber=N` has been stable for years), prefer URL-construction-based pagination over DOM-link-scraping-based pagination — the latter is brittle to UI changes that the former is immune to. **Reinforces the P-48 Session 1 "ffprobe-first / empirical-first diagnostic" Pattern** — empirical observation of Amazon's "Show 10 more reviews" button busted the speculative `findNextPageUrl` approach; the empirical pageNumber-direct fix is the robust replacement. **Generalization observation:** the per-platform extractor design should prefer URL-construction-based navigation primitives wherever the platform exposes a stable URL parameter contract. Amazon, eBay (page number in query string), Etsy (page number in URL path), Walmart (page number in query string) all expose stable URL-parameter pagination contracts. Future per-platform sub-cluster sessions should adopt URL-construction pagination from the start, not DOM-link-scraping. The Pattern is "construct, don't scrape, the next-page URL."

**(e) LOW informational sub-observation — P-43 cwd-leak Pattern Class reproduced 4-5+ times this session** during the various /scoreboard runs. Parallel Bash calls inherited cwd from prior `cd` commands; manifested as Check 3 "Missing script: test" (when commands inherited extension-dir cwd instead of root cwd while running `npm test`) + Check 5 "0 routes" (when `next build` ran from extension-dir cwd instead of root cwd). Each reproduction was caught + recovered with explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` retry. **Mechanical prevention candidate:** add absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` prefix to ALL Bash commands in `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` (specifically Check 5 + ideally all checks) — captured in the P-43 ROADMAP entry. Today's reproductions are the Nth in the Pattern class (alongside the 2026-05-24-f + 2026-05-26 reproductions); strong empirical signal for the prevention fix. The /scoreboard re-runs after each fix-forward stressed the Pattern class because they all happened in rapid succession with different cwds (Bash calls intermingled between extension-dir for `npm run zip` and root cwd for /scoreboard).

**PLUS calibration data point — all 9 Rule 14f forced-pickers fired with director-Yes to Recommended (9/9 = 100%).** High signal that most-thorough/reliable recommendation framing was well-calibrated to director's intent across (1) deploy-mechanics pickers (Check 6 Playwright SKIP + 4 Rule 9 deploy gates); (2) Phase 4 mode picker (Manual walkthrough); (3) fix-forward shape pickers (FF#1 extend dispatch + FF#2+#3+defer issue #3 + FF#4 pageNumber-increment); (4) next-session scope picker (P-49 W4 Session 1). Reinforces the 14/15 = 93.3% calibration data point from the 2026-05-25-b design session — across two of the most high-pickering sessions in recent W#2 history, the Recommended-is-director's-choice rate held at 93-100%. **Operational implication:** `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`'s default-to-Recommended directive is well-calibrated; the recommendation framing under `feedback_recommendation_style.md` (most-thorough/reliable) is hitting director's intent reliably. Future high-pickering sessions can confidently lean on Recommended path defaults.

**Operational housekeeping — ZERO throwaway scripts created this session.** Pure deploy + fix-forward session executing well-scoped fix shapes; no diagnostic probes; no schema introspection; no ad-hoc database queries. The 3 fix-forward commits (`8bc2e7e` + `b55cdbd` + `f6944db`) are all production-quality + tested (FF#1 +18 tests; FF#2+#3 +4 tests; FF#4 integration-deferred to Playwright per Rule 27); nothing transient to clean up at session end.

**ZERO DEFERRED carry-overs at session end (Rule 26).** The Sessions 1 + 2 standing carry-over (first end-to-end real-Chrome extraction test on a real Amazon product page) RESOLVED today via Phase 4 PASS verdict. Phase 4 verification issue #3 (no way to see reviews of specific star counts on vklf.com Captured Reviews section) is NOT a carry-over because it has a destination ((a.96) RECOMMENDED-NEXT = P-49 W4 Session 1) which directly addresses it per §A.14 (star-count counter-bar with click-to-filter); captured in ROADMAP P-49 W4 entry + (a.96) opening + NEXT_SESSION.md launch prompt rather than as a NEXT_SESSION ## Standing carry-over.

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — first build session of P-49 W4; 2 NEW reusable Patterns memorialized; calibration data point with the first non-Recommended pick in recent W#2 sessions; LOW informational P-43 Pattern Class re-reproduction. No top-tier corrections-tier slip this session.

3. **Build commit `e89ae50` (9 files +2162/-43):** NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/handlers/reviews-batch-delete.ts` (~165 LOC; DI seam; `MAX_BATCH_DELETE = 2000` ceiling; dedupes + drops empty IDs; filters to project-owned IDs via `findMany {id in [...], competitorUrl: {projectWorkflowId}}`; idempotent on missing rows; recordFlake on unknown error; markWorkflowActive only when ≥1 row actually deleted) + `reviews-batch-delete.test.ts` (~250 LOC; DI seam; `MAX_REORDER_BATCH = 2000` ceiling; parent CompetitorUrl ownership lookup before write; dedupe by reviewId (last entry wins); cross-URL reviewId smuggling silently dropped; updates run inside `$transaction` for atomic write; P2025 swallowed; recordFlake on unknown error) + `reviews-reorder.test.ts` (~310 LOC; **17 new node:test cases** covering auth / invalid JSON / non-array / missing reviewId / non-integer sortRank / empty reviewId / empty orderings no-op / oversized batch / 404 parent missing / ownership scoping / dedup last-wins / zero owned no-op / happy path apply-all / negative sortRank accepted / P2025 swallowed / unknown error 500 + flake / markWorkflowActive fires) + 2 thin route shims (~50 LOC each for POST batch-delete + PUT reorder) + NEW `src/lib/competition-scraping/captured-reviews-helpers.ts` (~115 LOC; pure helpers extracted from .tsx for node:test coverage — `CUSTOMERS_SAY_SOURCE` constant + `ReviewSortKey` type + `compareReviews()` manual-sort comparator + `splitCustomersSay()` + `computeStarCounts()` + `filterByStarSelection()` + `spliceVisibleReorderIntoFull()`) + `captured-reviews-helpers.test.ts` (~210 LOC; **20 new node:test cases**) + MODIFIED `UrlDetailContent.tsx` (+637/-43; major rewrite of `CapturedReviewsSection` from ~140 LOC to ~290 LOC; new inline `StarCountCounterBar` / `BulkSelectionToolbar` / `CustomersSayBanner` helper components; @dnd-kit `useSortable` for drag-handle; 500ms debounced reorder mutation via `useRef` + `useEffect`).

Total in-Claude time landed within budget (~1-2 hours for the bundled all-three scope; the launch prompt's ~1-2 session estimate matched actual).

**(b) NEW reusable Pattern: "Pure helpers extracted from .tsx component file for node:test coverage."** When a React `'use client'` component file contains substantial pure-function logic (comparator + filter + count + split + array splice), extract those helpers into a separate `.ts` module under `src/lib/` so node:test can load + exercise them without the React tree. Pattern fired here to extract 5 helpers from `UrlDetailContent.tsx` into `captured-reviews-helpers.ts`: `compareReviews()` (manual-sort comparator with sortRank asc nulls-last + addedAt tiebreak) + `splitCustomersSay()` (splits AI-summary rows from main review rows via source discriminator) + `computeStarCounts()` (tallies per-star bucket counts) + `filterByStarSelection()` (OR filter across selected star buckets) + `spliceVisibleReorderIntoFull()` (merges the visible-list reorder back into the full-list while preserving non-visible row positions). **Lesson:** node:test can't load a `'use client'` .tsx component file because the React tree + DOM dependencies + JSX transform don't compose with the test runner. Extracting pure helpers into a separate plain `.ts` module under `src/lib/` unlocks 20 new tests in this session that would otherwise have required a Playwright spec or component-test infra (both higher-friction). **Future feature work on .tsx components should adopt this extraction Pattern from the start** — any pure function inside a `'use client'` component that takes plain data in + returns plain data out is a candidate for src/lib extraction + node:test coverage. Pairs with the P-46 W3 S3 shared debounced-mutation Pattern from 2026-05-23-f (which split `useDebouncedMutation` into a hook in a shared module) — both Patterns push test-coverage-friendly code OUT of `'use client'` files into modules that node:test can directly load. **Generalization observation:** the threshold for extraction is "any pure function with non-trivial branching" (comparator with manual + addedAt + starRating modes hit this immediately; the array splice did because of the visible-vs-full row merging logic; the per-star count did because of the out-of-range filter). Future per-platform UI sessions on the W4 surface (eBay/Etsy/Walmart star-bucket variations) can reuse these 5 helpers OR extract platform-specific variants into a sibling module under `src/lib/competition-scraping/`.

**(c) NEW reusable Pattern: "Customers-say split: separate AI-summary row from main reviews via source discriminator."** When persisting a non-review record into the captured-reviews table via the existing schema's `source: String` field (per the 2026-05-27 P-49 W2 Session 2 Rule 14f outcome: starRating=5 sentinel + source="extension-scrape:customers-say"), the consuming UI should split rows by source discriminator at render time + render the AI-summary rows as a separate banner above the main list. Pattern fired here to render `CustomersSayBanner` above the `StarCountCounterBar` in `CapturedReviewsSection`. **The split achieves four UX outcomes simultaneously:** (1) AI-summary rows don't inflate per-star counts (the `computeStarCounts()` helper filters them out via `CUSTOMERS_SAY_SOURCE` constant); (2) AI-summary rows don't participate in drag-reorder (the `SortableContext` only wraps the main-review subset); (3) AI-summary rows don't participate in bulk-select (the checkboxes only appear on main-review rows); (4) AI-summary rows visually signal their distinct nature via a different banner styling above the per-star counter-bar. **Lesson:** when a row's domain semantics diverge from the table's primary purpose (here: a CapturedReview row is normally a single user's review, but the Customers-say row is an AI-generated aggregate summary), the source discriminator field is the rendering UI's hook for "should this row participate in row-level operations?" — even though it lives in the same database table. **Pairs with the 2026-05-27 W2 Session 2 Pattern "Pre-emptive design choice rolled into Rule 14f picker"** — Session 2's Customers-say storage-shape picker (director picked starRating=5 sentinel + source discriminator) directly enables today's UI split Pattern; the discriminator field is the rendering UI's hook. **Future per-platform sub-cluster sessions (eBay / Etsy / Walmart) that add their own AI-summary equivalents should reuse this discriminator + split Pattern from the start** — define a platform-specific source value (e.g., `"extension-scrape:etsy-overview"` or `"extension-scrape:walmart-snapshot"`) + filter at render time via the same split helper.

Each reproduction recovered via explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` retry. **Running tally now ~6-7+ across recent sessions** (4-5+ on 2026-05-28 + 2 today + earlier 2026-05-24-f + 2026-05-26 reproductions). **Strong empirical signal** still mounting for the P-43 mechanical prevention small fix (add absolute `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` prefix to ALL Bash commands in `.claude/commands/scoreboard.md` — specifically Check 5 + ideally all checks). The fix is opportunistic per the P-43 ROADMAP entry but increasingly worth slotting in. **Today's reproductions reinforce yesterday's empirical observation** — multi-Bash-call sessions running /scoreboard checks in succession + interleaved with extension-dir `npm test` / `npm run zip` commands stress the Pattern class. The pre-build + post-build /scoreboard cycle is now a known-stressor on the Pattern class regardless of session shape (deploy session yesterday + build session today both stressed it).

Build commit `e89ae50` is production-quality + tested (15 + 17 + 20 = 52 new src/lib node:test cases covering the new handlers + helpers); nothing transient to clean up at session end.

Director's pick should be honored without further re-confirmation (Rule 14f forced-picker mechanics + `feedback_default_to_recommendation.md`).

## §Entry 2026-05-30 — INFORMATIONAL — P-49 W2 eBay SUB-CLUSTER SESSION 1 + DEPLOY + 5-FIX-FORWARD CASCADE + PHASE-4-PASS BUNDLED CLOSING ENTRY — First deploy session of the Reviews Phase 2 W2 eBay sub-cluster on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` → `main` via build commit `11e99e1` (3 files +1535/-99) + 5 fix-forward commits all on main under 5 separate Rule 9 deploy gates (build deploy + FF#1+#2 bundled `23b6221` deploy + FF#3 `c19f187` deploy + FF#4 `6675963` diagnostic deploy + FF#5 `23aa851` empirically-verified fix deploy) within ONE Phase 4 verification session; director Phase 4 PASS verdict on FF#5 "Everything worked perfectly"; eBay sub-cluster now fully ✅ DEPLOYED-AND-VERIFIED end-to-end on vklf.com with no remaining scope (§C.2 estimate "~3-4 sessions for the full eBay arc" compressed into "1 calendar day"; eBay Sessions 2 + 3 from §C.2 CLOSED); THREE NEW reusable Patterns memorialized ("Diagnostic-instrumentation FF as escape valve from speculative-FF antipattern" + "Tabpanel-scoped DOM walking via `[role=tabpanel]:not([hidden])`" + "JSON data-island extraction for server-rendered seller/product metadata") + 3 LOW informational sub-observations (P-43 cwd-leak Pattern Class reproduction running tally ~11+ across recent sessions + calibration data point 3/3 = 100% Yes-to-Recommended this session running cumulative 27/30 = 90% + §C.2 estimate compression memorialized); THREE Rule 14f forced-pickers fired all director-Yes-to-Recommended; FIVE Rule 9 deploy gates fired (stretches 2026-05-28 Amazon DEPLOY 4-FF Pattern by one further); Schema-change-in-flight flag STAYS NO entire session.

**Severity:** INFORMATIONAL — first deploy session of P-49 W2 eBay sub-cluster; entire eBay arc compressed into 1 calendar day; THREE NEW reusable Patterns memorialized; LOW informational P-43 cwd-leak Pattern Class re-reproductions; calibration data point well-calibrated. No top-tier corrections-tier slip this session — the 3 speculative-FF cascade isn't a slip per se since each speculative FF was a legitimate hypothesis based on director's working URL params; the Diagnostic-instrumentation FF Pattern reinforced today is captured prospectively for future sessions.

**Context (session shape):** Today's session opened on `workflow-2-competition-scraping` with the (a.98) RECOMMENDED-NEXT task = P-49 W2 eBay sub-cluster Session 1 locked at yesterday's W4 post-deploy doc-batch. The session originally framed as a build session (no deploy planned per the pre-session plan), but the first Rule 14f picker at start-of-session offered Deploy-now-vs-Exit per the new "End-of-build-session deploy-or-exit Rule 14f picker" Pattern memorialized at yesterday's W4 deploy (§Entry 2026-05-29 sub-observation f) — director picked Recommended Deploy-now, and the session expanded into a deploy session from the start. Build commit `11e99e1` (3 files +1535/-99) landed first carrying NEW `extensions/competition-scraping/src/lib/content-script/ebay-review-extractor.ts` (~390 LOC; per-platform module mirroring W2 Amazon Patterns from 2026-05-28 deploy — FF#1 symmetric `isEbayScrapableUrl` + `extractItemIdFromEbayUrl` helpers + FF#4 URL-construction-based pagination + cross-filter loop NEUTRAL→NEGATIVE + Shadow DOM trigger modal reuse with per-URL cap override) + NEW `ebay-review-extractor.test.ts` (~470 LOC; 64 new node:test cases) + MODIFY `orchestrator.ts` (eBay path added to `start-review-scrape` dispatch). Ff-merged to main under Rule 9 gate #1; Vercel auto-redeploy fired; fresh extension zip `plos-extension-2026-05-30-w2-ebay-deploy-1.zip` produced.

**Phase 4 director real-Chrome verification surfaced TWO co-occurring bugs:** (1) **Seller auto-detect failing** — modern eBay listing pages don't render `/usr/<seller>` links in the visible DOM (zero matches against the legacy `extractSellerFromListingDocument` walker); seller is embedded in a JSON data island as `"sellerUserName":"<value>"`; (2) **This Item filter returning All Items rows** — eBay's feedback page renders BOTH the "This Item" tabpanel AND the "All Items" tabpanel server-side, with the All Items panel carrying the `hidden` HTML attribute; the original walker (whole-document scope) was matching `.fdbk-container__details` rows from both panels. These two bugs persisted across three speculative fix-forwards: **FF#1+#2 bundled `23b6221`** (4 files +183/-30; +4 ext tests) trigger modal extended with optional eBay seller text input (auto-detect prefill; manual paste fallback when listing-page extraction fails) + `buildEbayFeedbackUrl` adds `q=<itemId>` + `filter=feedback_page:RECEIVED_AS_SELLER` (director's working URL params); BUSTED — still returned All Items. **FF#3 `c19f187`** (2 files +58/-25; 0 new tests) full `_item`-suffixed param set replaces `_pgn` pagination (`page_id_item=N` + `sort_item=RELEVANCEV2` + `filter_image_item=false` + `filter_video_item=false` + `filter_automated_feedback_item=true` + `filter_topic_item=`); BUSTED — still returned All Items.

**Rule 14f mid-Phase-4 picker fired at the after-FF#3-BUSTED decision point** offering (A) Diagnostic instrumentation [Recommended — dump fetched artifacts to director's Downloads folder + console.log selector probes; director uploads → empirical evidence → next FF is correct] vs (B) Defer to next session [PARTIAL deploy + capture in NEXT_SESSION.md]. **Director picked Recommended (A).** **FF#4 `6675963`** (2 files +116/-0) shipped TEMPORARY diagnostic instrumentation: dumps fetched HTML to director's Downloads folder via programmatic `<a download>` click + console.logs structured row-count breakdowns per selector probe. Director ran ONE scrape → uploaded 3 HTML files (listing + NEGATIVE feedback page 1 + NEUTRAL feedback page 1) + pasted console output.

**Analysis of the dumped HTML files + console output revealed BOTH root causes simultaneously** — confirming a) the JSON data-island seller location empirically + b) the dual-tabpanel server-render with `hidden` attribute on the inactive panel empirically. **FF#5 `23aa851` EMPIRICALLY-VERIFIED FIX — the WIN** (3 files +176/-135; +8 ext tests — 6 sellerFromListingHtml + 2 tabpanel-scoping): NEW `extractSellerFromListingHtml(html)` regex extraction from raw HTML against `"sellerUserName":"<value>"` JSON data island (legacy DOM-based `extractSellerFromListingDocument` preserved as classic-view fallback) + scope walker to `[role=tabpanel]:not([hidden])` (falls through to whole-doc scope as classic-view safety net) + removes ALL FF#4 diagnostic instrumentation. **Director Phase 4 PASS verdict on FF#5 = "Everything worked perfectly."** RESOLVES the eBay sub-cluster end-to-end on vklf.com.

**THREE NEW REUSABLE PATTERNS memorialized:**

1. **"Diagnostic-instrumentation FF as escape valve from speculative-FF antipattern"** — when 2+ consecutive speculative fix-forwards BUSTED, ALWAYS fire a Rule 14f picker offering (A) Diagnostic instrumentation [Recommended — dump fetched artifacts to director's Downloads + console.log selector probes; director uploads → empirical evidence → next FF is correct] vs (B) Defer to next session [PARTIAL deploy + capture in NEXT_SESSION.md]. **Don't ship a 3rd speculative FF.** Today's session validated this — FF#5 with empirical evidence solved BOTH bugs in one shot after 3 speculative FFs (FF#1+#2 bundled + FF#3) all guessed wrong on the same surface area. **Pairs with + extends the 2026-05-25 P-48 Session 1 "ffprobe-first / empirical-first diagnostic" Pattern at the deploy-session-cascade level** — the ffprobe-first Pattern says "inspect the actual production artifacts BEFORE drafting Session 2 fix scope"; today's Pattern says "inspect the actual fetched artifacts BEFORE shipping the Nth speculative FF in a deploy cascade". Future P-49 W2 sub-cluster deploy sessions (Etsy / Walmart) should expect the diagnostic-instrumentation FF as a normal escape valve when speculative FFs stack up — the cost of one diagnostic FF (~10-15 min Claude + ~5 min director upload roundtrip) is far smaller than the cost of N speculative FFs that keep BUSTING.

2. **"Tabpanel-scoped DOM walking — when server-rendered HTML includes both active + hidden tabpanels, scope the walker to `[role=tabpanel]:not([hidden])` to avoid capturing rows from inactive tabs"** — confirmed today by eBay's feedback page rendering BOTH the This Item + All Items panels in the same HTML, with the All Items panel carrying the `hidden` HTML attribute. **Empirical evidence:** NEGATIVE page had "This item (1)" header but 26 `.fdbk-container__details` rows total in the document (1 from the visible This Item panel + 25 from the hidden All Items panel). The fix is a one-line walker-scope addition: `(doc.querySelector('[role="tabpanel"]:not([hidden])') ?? doc)` — try the active tabpanel first, fall through to whole-doc as a classic-view safety net. **Generalizes beyond eBay to any tabbed-content scraping surface** — Amazon's tabbed review-filter UI, Etsy's per-star filter overlay, Walmart's review aggregator tabs all potentially exhibit this pattern. Future per-platform module sessions should adopt this walker-scope Pattern proactively if the platform's HTML uses tabbed UI.

3. **"JSON data-island extraction for server-rendered seller/product metadata"** — modern site listing pages frequently embed JSON blobs containing canonical seller/product fields (`"sellerUserName":"<value>"` on eBay; likely similar shapes on Etsy + Walmart for seller-name / listing-name / category metadata). **Regex extraction against raw HTML is more robust than DOM-link probing when the visible UI renders the same data via React buttons/spans rather than `<a href="...">`.** Today's eBay listing pages did NOT render `/usr/<seller>` links anywhere in the visible DOM; the seller name was rendered via a React component reading from the JSON data island. The fix is a simple regex against the raw HTML string (not the parsed Document) — `/"sellerUserName":\s*"([^"]+)"/` extracts the value reliably; the legacy DOM-link walker stays as a classic-view fallback. **Applies to future Etsy + Walmart sub-cluster sessions** — adopt the JSON data-island extraction Pattern from the start for any platform-canonical seller/listing/product metadata; preserve DOM-link walker as a classic-view fallback.

Each recovered via explicit `cd /workspaces/brand-operations-hub` retry. (e) **Calibration data point — Rule 14f forced-pickers fired 3 times today all director-Yes-to-Recommended (3/3 = 100% this session); cumulative running across recent 5 sessions: 27/30 = 90%.** Framing well-calibrated. The diagnostic-vs-defer picker at mid-Phase-4 also produced the right call (director's pick directly led to the FF#5 empirical fix that resolved the session).
